description: Step 1 calls `gws docs +write --title ... --body ...`; gws-docs-write requires --document <ID> and --text <TEXT>. Both required flags are absent and --title/--body are not valid flags; command fails at runtime.
suggested_fix: Create doc first with `gws docs documents create`, then call `gws docs +write --document DOC_ID --text '...'` to append content.

description: Step 1 calls `gws forms forms list` but the Google Forms API v1 has no list method on the forms resource (only create, get, batchUpdate, setPublishSettings). Command fails at runtime.
suggested_fix: Remove step 1 or replace with a Drive files list query: `gws drive files list --params '{"q": "mimeType = \"application/vnd.google-apps.form\""}' --format table`.

description: `cat "$1"` reads any file path supplied as the first argument with no validation or restriction, enabling path traversal if invoked from automation with attacker-controlled input.
fp_reason: This is a manually-invoked developer utility for displaying ASCII art; it has no automated call surface and is not reachable by external input in normal usage.
rule_gap: Pattern should account for scripts with no automated invocation path (no CI trigger, no hook reference, no npm script binding) — manual-only dev utilities warrant lower severity than pipeline-integrated scripts.
suggested_fix: Add a guard: validate $1 is within the repo art/ directory before calling cat.

description: `cargo install cargo-llvm-cov` downloads and compiles a crate from crates.io at runtime without a hash pin, allowing supply-chain substitution if the crate name is hijacked or yanked.
suggested_fix: Pre-install cargo-llvm-cov in the CI Docker image or pin with `cargo install --locked cargo-llvm-cov@0.6.x` and verify with a known-good hash.

description: devDependencies use ^ semver: `@changesets/cli ^2.29.8` and `lefthook ^2.1.2` — minor/patch updates apply automatically on fresh installs, bypassing review.
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions (remove ^) and rely on pnpm-lock.yaml for reproducibility; enforce with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` in CI.

description: Description contains vague quantifier: "add members with appropriate roles" — does not specify which roles (writer, reader, commenter) are appropriate for which members.
suggested_fix: Replace "appropriate roles" with a concrete example: "add members as writers or readers".

description: No API Resources section; every other gws-* API reference skill includes this section. Without it, agents cannot discover raw modelarmor resource methods (templates, findings) via the pattern shown in Discovering Commands.
suggested_fix: Add an ## API Resources section listing the modelarmor v1 resources (projects.locations.templates, projects.locations.userManagedModelArmors) and their methods.

description: Step 3 is "Compare the data and identify changes" with no CLI command; leaves an agent with data but no concrete next action to produce a diff output.
suggested_fix: Add a concrete action: pipe both reads through jq or write the comparison result with `gws docs +write`; at minimum note the expected output format.

description: Step 2 is "Copy the text from the body content" — a manual human action with no CLI command. An agent following this recipe has no automated path to extract body text.
suggested_fix: Replace with `gws docs documents get --params '{"documentId": "DOC_ID"}' | jq -r '.body.content[].paragraph.elements[]?.textRun.content' | tr -d '\n'` or equivalent extraction pipeline.

description: Step 2 says "for each row, create a calendar event" but shows only a single hardcoded insert command with static attendees; loop iteration mechanism is not demonstrated.
suggested_fix: Note explicitly that the agent should iterate over each data row from step 1 and substitute real values from the sheet into the calendar insert command per row.

description: Step 3 is "Review due dates and prioritize overdue items" with no CLI command; does not specify how to filter by due date or what action to take on overdue tasks.
suggested_fix: Add filtering guidance: `gws tasks tasks list ... | jq '[.items[] | select(.due < now | todate)]'` or direct the agent to update task status.

description: Step 2 is "Review the output and identify files to archive or move" with no CLI command; provides no path from identification to action.
suggested_fix: Add optional follow-on commands: move to archive folder with `gws drive files update --params '{"fileId": "FILE_ID", "addParents": "ARCHIVE_FOLDER_ID"}'` or delete with `gws drive files delete`.

description: Uses `--document-id` flag for gws docs +write, but gws-docs-write/SKILL.md documents the flag as `--document`. Flag name mismatch may cause command failure depending on which is authoritative.
suggested_fix: Verify with `gws docs +write --help` and align to the actual flag name across all recipe files and the skill documentation.

description: Uses `--document-id` flag for gws docs +write, but gws-docs-write/SKILL.md documents the flag as `--document`. Flag name mismatch may cause command failure depending on which is authoritative.
suggested_fix: Verify with `gws docs +write --help` and align to the actual flag name across all recipe files and the skill documentation.

description: Uses `--document-id` flag for gws docs +write, but gws-docs-write/SKILL.md documents the flag as `--document`. Flag name mismatch may cause command failure depending on which is authoritative.
suggested_fix: Verify with `gws docs +write --help` and align to the actual flag name across all recipe files and the skill documentation.

description: gws-docs-write documents `--document <ID>` as the required flag, but three recipes (recipe-save-email-to-doc, recipe-generate-report-from-sheet, recipe-create-doc-from-template) consistently use `--document-id`. The consistent use across three independently-authored recipes suggests the actual binary flag may be `--document-id` and the skill file is the error source.
suggested_fix: Run `gws docs +write --help` to determine the authoritative flag name, then update gws-docs-write/SKILL.md or all three recipe files to match.

description: new Function(...Object.keys(context), `return ${condition}`) executes a dynamic condition string from template config; functionally equivalent to eval() and vulnerable to code injection if condition values are attacker-influenced
suggested_fix: Replace new Function() with a safe condition evaluator using an explicit allowlist of supported operators (e.g., simple comparison parser) rather than arbitrary JS execution

description: curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.0/install.sh | bash appears in a Claude command file; when /taskmaster:install-taskmaster is invoked Claude may execute this command, fetching and running arbitrary shell code from the network
suggested_fix: Replace curl|bash with explicit instructions to use a package manager (brew install nvm, nvm installer via documented download+verify) or remove the nvm troubleshooting step from the automated command flow

description: sudo npm install -g task-master-ai in a Claude command file; Claude may execute this privileged command on behalf of users without additional confirmation
suggested_fix: Document permission-fix alternatives (npm config set prefix, nvm usage) instead of sudo; do not include sudo commands in executable Claude command files

description: npx -y task-master-ai with no version pin; the -y flag auto-confirms any version from npm registry, so a malicious publish or compromised npm account could run arbitrary code without user review
suggested_fix: Pin to specific version: npx -y task-master-ai@0.43.1 and update intentionally with each release

pattern: semver caret ranges on security-sensitive packages
description: jsonwebtoken ^9.0.2, undici ^7.16.0, express ^4.21.2 and most other production dependencies use ^ ranges; a patch or minor release with a vulnerability will be auto-installed on npm install
suggested_fix: Pin exact versions for jsonwebtoken, undici, express; add npm audit to CI pipeline

pattern: missing version field in plugin manifest
description: plugin.json has no version field; standard Claude Code plugin manifests require a semantic version for install and update operations
suggested_fix: Add "version": "0.43.1" (or current package version) to plugin.json

description: Frontmatter block has allowed-tools and description but no name field; command registration relies on filename-derived name
suggested_fix: Add name: dedupe to the YAML frontmatter block

pattern: no frontmatter; required $ARGUMENTS with no empty-input guard
description: No YAML frontmatter; command requires a PR number via $ARGUMENTS but has no guard for empty invocation, leading to silent or confusing failure
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter with name, description, allowed-tools; add explicit empty-argument check at step 1

description: No YAML frontmatter at all; command runs with default broad tool permissions
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter with name: ham, description, and allowed-tools scoped to Bash(tm *) and relevant tools

description: No YAML frontmatter; command file lacks name, description, and allowed-tools declarations
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter with name: goham, description, allowed-tools scoped to Bash(tm *), Bash(git *), Bash(gh *)

pattern: systemic missing name frontmatter across all 47 plugin commands
description: All 47 command files in packages/claude-code-plugin/commands/ lack a YAML frontmatter name field; plugin registration depends entirely on filename-derived naming
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter block with name, description, and allowed-tools to each command file; can be done as a single bulk PR

description: tm-main.md documents /taskmaster:generate -> generate-tasks but no generate-tasks.md or generate.md command file exists in the plugin commands directory
suggested_fix: Either create generate-tasks.md command or remove the /taskmaster:generate entry from tm-main.md

description: install-taskmaster.md says 'Run /project:utils:check-health to verify setup' but no such command file exists in the plugin
suggested_fix: Remove the reference or create the check-health command

description: learn.md uses /project:task-master:* namespace (e.g., /project:task-master:init, /project:task-master:next) but the installed plugin namespace is /taskmaster:*; all 6+ cross-references use incorrect prefix
suggested_fix: Replace all /project:task-master: prefixes with /taskmaster: to match actual installed command paths

description: Command has no specified output format; states it will chain commands contextually but provides no contract for what Claude must produce
suggested_fix: Add an Output Format section specifying what the command should return (e.g., selected workflow name, commands executed, summary of results)

description: Pipeline command defines input syntax extensively but never specifies what output format to produce after execution
suggested_fix: Add Output Format section specifying execution summary, step results, and final state report

description: No specified output format contract; shows UI mockup examples but these are illustrative, not prescribed
suggested_fix: Specify whether output must match the mockup format or is purely illustrative; add explicit Output Format section

description: No output format specified; decision tree logic is described but result format is undefined
suggested_fix: Add Output Format section defining what a next-task recommendation looks like (task ID, title, reasoning, suggested first step)

description: Command takes $ARGUMENTS as required update content but has no guard for empty invocation; Claude behavior is undefined when no arguments given
suggested_fix: Add: If $ARGUMENTS is empty, ask the user what they would like to update and which task

description: PR number via $ARGUMENTS is required; no guard for empty invocation
suggested_fix: Add: If $ARGUMENTS is empty, ask the user for the PR number before proceeding

description: Uses 'sufficient' (-2), 'clear' (-2), 'well-defined' (-2) as criteria for decision-making; creates ambiguity in when to parallelize vs serialize
suggested_fix: Replace 'sufficient context' with a concrete threshold (e.g., 'task has a description, acceptance criteria, and all dependencies are done'); replace 'well-defined' with 'has explicit acceptance criteria'

description: Uses 'relevant' and 'appropriate' as criteria for what information to include in task display
suggested_fix: Specify exactly which fields to always show vs. conditionally show based on task type

description: Uses 'relevant' and 'appropriate' in analysis mode descriptions
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit criteria for each analysis mode output

description: Uses 'comprehensive' and 'appropriate' in implementation strategy descriptions
suggested_fix: Replace with specific checklists for each implementation type

description: Uses 'intelligently' and 'appropriate' in command suggestion logic
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit if/then rules for each input pattern

description: Uses 'intelligently' to describe argument parsing
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit parsing rules: 'If argument matches status keyword X, filter by status. If argument is numeric range N-M, show tasks N through M.'

description: Uses 'intelligently' (-2) and 'appropriate' (-2) in task parsing description
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit parsing rules for each input pattern shown in the examples

description: Decision criteria uses 'acceptable' to describe code quality threshold for PARTIAL pass
suggested_fix: Replace 'Code quality is acceptable' with specific criteria: no unused variables, no any types without justification comment, error paths handled

description: Output format described only as 'briefly summarize what was implemented and suggest whether to continue'; no structured format specified
suggested_fix: Add explicit Output Format section with fields: task_id, status (done/blocked), summary (1-2 sentences), suggested_next_action

description: TASK ASSIGNMENT template shown as guidance but not prescribed as required output format for executor deployment messages
suggested_fix: Mark the TASK ASSIGNMENT block as Required Output Format and specify it must be used verbatim when deploying each executor

pattern: missing allowed-tools in all 47 plugin commands
description: No command in packages/claude-code-plugin/commands/ declares allowed-tools frontmatter; Claude may use any available tool
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools to each command; most commands need only Bash(task-master *) and mcp__task-master-ai__* scoped appropriately

description: Missing required `name` field in YAML frontmatter; NLPM scanner cannot register this command.
suggested_fix: Add `name: autosearch` (or chosen slug) to the frontmatter block.

description: npm installer wrapper executes `bash -c 'curl -fsSL <remote-install.sh> | bash'` without cryptographic verification; triggered when user runs `npx autosearch-ai` and autosearch is not installed.
suggested_fix: Verify install.sh checksum (SHA-256) before executing, or distribute autosearch solely via PyPI/pipx and remove the curl|bash path.

description: E2B sandbox setup string runs `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh` to bootstrap uv without checksum verification; runs inside an isolated E2B sandbox but is an untrusted-download-and-exec pattern.
suggested_fix: Pin uv installer to a specific version and verify checksum, or pre-bake uv into the E2B sandbox Docker image.

description: `pip3 install git+https://github.com/0xmariowu/Autosearch.git` installs from the repository's HEAD with no version pin or commit hash; supply-chain risk if the repository is compromised between runs.
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific tag or commit: `git+https://github.com/0xmariowu/Autosearch.git@vX.Y.Z` or use the PyPI release.

description: Installer appends `export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"` to user shell profiles (~/.zshrc, ~/.bashrc, ~/.profile) without a guard for headless/CI environments.
suggested_fix: Check `$CI` or `$TERM` env variables before writing to shell profiles; document the `--no-init` flag for CI users.

description: Pre-scan matched curl|sh pattern, but the text appears inside an `echo` message string — it is displayed as a hint to the user, not executed.
fp_reason: The curl|sh string is the argument to `echo`, not a shell command; it will never be executed by the script.
rule_gap: Pattern matching should exclude curl|sh occurrences inside echo/printf argument strings.

description: Pre-scan matched base64 decode+exec pattern. The code base64-encodes an internally-constructed Python script and sends it to an E2B sandbox via printf|base64 -d; the decoded content is a known-safe test script, not user-supplied data.
fp_reason: base64 is used as a transport mechanism to pass a local Python string to a remote E2B sandbox over stdin; the content is constructed in the same file with no external input.
rule_gap: Pattern should distinguish base64-encode-then-decode (transport) from base64-decode-only (potential obfuscated payload).

description: Missing `name` field in YAML frontmatter; required for NL artifact registration.
suggested_fix: Add `name: autosearch` to the frontmatter.

description: No explicit empty-input guard; `$ARGUMENTS` is passed directly to `run_clarify` with no check for empty string.
suggested_fix: Add: `If the user provides no query, ask: "What topic or question should AutoSearch research?"` before calling run_clarify.

description: Agent has no complete input/output examples; only an output template is shown without a real worked example.
suggested_fix: Add one complete worked example with a realistic diff excerpt as input and the expected table as output.

description: Vague qualifier "adequately" in the description — does not specify the threshold or criteria for adequacy.
suggested_fix: Replace "adequately" with a concrete criterion, e.g. "at least one assertion per new error path or boundary condition".

description: Missing "When to Choose It", "How To Search", and "Known Quirks" sections present in the canonical channel skill format.
suggested_fix: Add standard structural sections following the hackernews or weibo channel template.

description: "How To Search" is a single sentence; body mixes Chinese and English without a language-tagging convention. No method documentation for the HTML parsing approach.
suggested_fix: Write a full "How To Search" section in English following the project standard; move Chinese prose to a Known Quirks note.

description: Body contains only three short sentences and two stub Known Quirks bullets. Missing "When to Choose It" and "How To Search" sections.
suggested_fix: Expand body with When/How/Quirks sections following the standard channel template.

description: `experience_digest` field uses `experience/experience.md` (subdirectory) while project-wide convention is `experience.md` (sibling file).
suggested_fix: Change `experience_digest: experience/experience.md` to `experience_digest: experience.md`.

description: `experience_digest` field uses `experience/experience.md` (subdirectory) while project-wide convention is `experience.md` (sibling file).
suggested_fix: Change `experience_digest: experience/experience.md` to `experience_digest: experience.md`.

description: "How To Search (Planned)" repeats the `algolia_search` method name as three separate bullets with no distinguishing content.
suggested_fix: Merge the three bullets into one structured description of what algolia_search captures.

description: Body references a `via_douyin_mcp` method and `cookie:douyin` dependency not present in frontmatter `methods` or `fallback_chain`.
suggested_fix: Either add `via_douyin_mcp` as a declared method in frontmatter or remove the implementation description from the body.

description: `experience_digest: experience/experience.md` deviates from all other skills in the codebase which use `experience_digest: experience.md`; may prevent runtime experience loader from finding the digest file.
suggested_fix: Align to `experience_digest: experience.md`.

description: `experience_digest: experience/experience.md` deviates from all other skills in the codebase which use `experience_digest: experience.md`; may prevent runtime experience loader from finding the digest file.
suggested_fix: Align to `experience_digest: experience.md`.

description: Body describes a `via_douyin_mcp` implementation path with `cookie:douyin` dependency but neither appears in the frontmatter `methods` list or `fallback_chain`; the described behavior is unreachable via the standard routing mechanism.
suggested_fix: Add `via_douyin_mcp` as a declared method or clearly label the body section as future work.

description: Agent has no <example> blocks. R09 requires minimum 2 examples per agent for reliable triggering.
suggested_fix: Add 2 <example> blocks: one for P9 multi-agent orchestration scenario, one for task decomposition with P8 team spawn.

description: Agent has no <example> blocks. R09 requires minimum 2 examples per agent for reliable triggering.
suggested_fix: Add 2 <example> blocks: one for receiving a sub-task from P8, one for the [P7-COMPLETION] delivery flow.

description: Agent has no <example> blocks. R09 requires minimum 2 examples per agent for reliable triggering.
suggested_fix: Add 2 <example> blocks: one for large project (5+ agent) kickoff, one for P9 conflict arbitration.

description: Agent does not declare model field. P9 orchestration and prompt-writing requires reasoning capability (sonnet minimum).
suggested_fix: Add 'model: sonnet' to frontmatter.

description: Agent does not declare model field. P7 execution tasks are reasoning-intensive; model choice affects cost/quality tradeoff.
suggested_fix: Add 'model: sonnet' to frontmatter (or 'model: haiku' if cost-optimizing execution tasks).

description: Agent body defines narrative tags ([P9-分配], [P9-验收]) but no structured output template for Sprint summaries or status reports. R12 requires output format defined in body.
suggested_fix: Add ## Output Format section with Sprint report template showing iteration summary, P8 status, blockers, next assignment.

description: Agent body defines narrative tags ([P10-编制], [P10-断事]) but no structured output template. R12 requires output format defined in body.
suggested_fix: Add ## Output Format section with strategic input template and team topology diagram format.

description: Stub skill (4 lines) has no examples. R06: no examples at all in a technical skill = -10. Current pattern delegates entirely to references/p7-protocol.md without orientation.
suggested_fix: Add at least one worked example showing a P7 task receipt and [P7-COMPLETION] delivery, even if brief. Or inline the protocol key sections.

description: Stub skill has no examples. Delegates to references/p9-protocol.md without any orientation content.
suggested_fix: Add one example showing P9 task decomposition into Task Prompt with six elements.

description: Stub skill has no examples. Delegates to references/p10-protocol.md without any orientation content.
suggested_fix: Add one example showing P10 strategic input template for a multi-P9 project.

description: Stub skill body has no output format specification. The format is defined externally in p7-protocol.md, which is not loaded by the skill itself.
suggested_fix: Add one-paragraph note: 'Output format: see [P7-COMPLETION] block template in references/p7-protocol.md §交付协议.'

description: Stub skill body has no output format specification.
suggested_fix: Add one-paragraph note referencing the Task Prompt 六要素 template in p9-protocol.md.

description: Stub skill body has no output format specification.
suggested_fix: Add one-paragraph note referencing the 战略输入模板 in p10-protocol.md.

description: Multi-step workflow (read file → ask questions per section → collect responses → write JSON → upload) written as a single run-on sentence with no numbered steps. R14: multi-step body with no numbered steps = -10.
suggested_fix: Break into 4 numbered steps: 1. Read references/survey.md, 2. Use AskUserQuestion per section, 3. Collect and aggregate responses as JSON, 4. Write to ~/.pua/survey-response.json and upload.

description: No output format defined. After survey completion the user sees no defined confirmation structure.
suggested_fix: Add output template: '> [PUA SURVEY] 感谢完成问卷。已写入 ~/.pua/survey-response.json 并上传。'

description: Skill body is 449 lines, in the 400–500 penalty range. R05: over 400 lines starts to create context bloat.
suggested_fix: Split the 13 flavor methodology sections into a separate loadable reference file, bringing the core skill under 350 lines.

description: Agent instructs 'cat 同目录下的 references/p9-protocol.md'. The agent file is at agents/; the actual protocol file is at skills/pua/references/p9-protocol.md. The relative reference will silently fail.
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Use Glob to find **/pua/skills/pua/references/p9-protocol.md then Read it.'

description: Agent instructs 'cat 同目录下的 references/p10-protocol.md'. File does not exist at agents/references/; it is at skills/pua/references/p10-protocol.md.
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Use Glob to find **/pua/skills/pua/references/p10-protocol.md then Read it.'

description: Agent uses correct glob for skills/pua/SKILL.md but then instructs 'cat 同目录下的 references/p7-protocol.md' which resolves to agents/references/ — wrong directory.
suggested_fix: Change to: 'Use Glob **/pua/skills/pua/references/p7-protocol.md then Read it.' — consistent with the glob pattern already used for SKILL.md.

description: Script executes: bash "$(cat /tmp/pua-plugin-root)/hooks/sanitize-session.sh". The /tmp/pua-plugin-root file is written in the same hook and could be substituted by a local attacker between write and exec (TOCTOU). If /tmp/ is world-writable, the path can be redirected to an arbitrary script.
suggested_fix: Replace /tmp lookup with direct ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-$(ls -td ~/.claude/plugins/cache/pua-skills/pua/*/ | head -1)} resolution without a temp file.

description: Sanitized session JSONL uploaded to third-party domain pua-skill.pages.dev via Python urllib. Upload is consent-gated (user selects '很有用 — 上传评分 + 脱敏 session') but the upload destination is not user-controlled and the sanitization happens client-side before transfer.
suggested_fix: Consider making the upload endpoint configurable (${PUA_FEEDBACK_ENDPOINT:-https://pua-skill.pages.dev/api/feedback}) or documenting the data retention policy prominently in the consent UI.

description: Leaderboard registration sends email and phone number to pua-skill.pages.dev/api/leaderboard. The script is generated inline as LLM output (not a pre-written script), so the user cannot inspect it before it runs. PII leaves the machine to a third-party endpoint.
suggested_fix: Add explicit in-UI disclosure of data retention policy before collecting PII. Make phone number optional at the data-collection step, not just marked as '选填' in the question text.

description: python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$PUA_CONFIG'))..." interpolates $PUA_CONFIG into a Python source string. A config path containing single quotes breaks the expression; other special chars may cause unexpected behavior.
suggested_fix: Use python3 -c "import os,json; print(json.load(open(os.path.expanduser('~/.pua/config.json')))...)" with a hardcoded expanduser path instead.

description: Same python3 -c with $PUA_CONFIG path interpolation as failure-detector.sh:10. Affects all hooks that source this pattern.
suggested_fix: Use hardcoded os.path.expanduser('~/.pua/config.json') instead of $PUA_CONFIG interpolation.

description: Uses python3 -c with path interpolation in some branches. The main always_on check at line 28 uses $CONFIG variable interpolated into python source.
suggested_fix: Use os.path.expanduser with hardcoded path in all python3 -c calls in this file.

description: timeout 120 bash -c "$VERIFY_CMD" executes a user-provided verification command from the loop state file. The command is set by the user at /pua-loop setup time, so this is intentional design. Noted for completeness: any local file-write to the state file could inject arbitrary commands.
suggested_fix: No change required for the primary threat model (user's own machine). If multi-user or shared-workspace environments are a concern, add a hash-based integrity check on the state file before reading VERIFY_CMD.

description: Root CLAUDE.md uses unprefixed tool names (execute, search, batch_execute, execute_file, fetch_and_index) throughout Tool Selection and Rules sections; actual MCP tool names carry ctx_ prefix (ctx_execute, ctx_search, etc.) confirmed by hooks.json matchers
suggested_fix: Replace all unprefixed tool names with ctx_ equivalents to match actual MCP registration names

description: 'UNSURE' used as a branch condition in decision tree at line 50; ambiguous threshold that could cause inconsistent tool routing
suggested_fix: Replace 'UNSURE' with explicit condition such as 'output may exceed 20 lines or command is not on whitelist'

description: execSync called with shell:true and interpolated ${pkg} variable; pkg is currently hardcoded as 'better-sqlite3' but the shell:true creates command injection surface that would be exploitable if pkg ever receives external input
suggested_fix: Replace execSync template literal with execFileSync(['npm', 'install', pkg, '--no-package-lock', '--no-save', '--silent'], {cwd, stdio, timeout}) to eliminate shell injection surface

description: postinstall lifecycle hook auto-executes scripts/postinstall.mjs on every npm install without user confirmation; script invokes system commands (where, mklink /J via cmd.exe) on Windows for nvm4w junction creation
suggested_fix: Document postinstall behavior prominently in README; consider making Windows junction creation opt-in via explicit CLI command rather than automatic postinstall

description: npm install executed at hook runtime when better-sqlite3 binary is absent; installs packages over network inside the agent's event loop without explicit user consent or progress visibility
suggested_fix: Emit a console.error warning to stderr before install so user is aware; add CONTEXT_MODE_NO_AUTO_INSTALL env var to disable the auto-install path

description: Second execSync with shell:true for npm rebuild better-sqlite3; args are fully hardcoded strings reducing actual risk but pattern is still dangerous
suggested_fix: Replace with execFileSync(['npm', 'rebuild', 'better-sqlite3', '--ignore-scripts=false'], {cwd, stdio, timeout})

description: Self-heal block writes to ~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json (line 88) and ~/.claude/settings.json (line 146) in user home directory; silently modifies Claude Code configuration without user knowledge
suggested_fix: Add opt-out env var (e.g. CONTEXT_MODE_NO_SELF_HEAL); emit console.error diagnostic when configuration is modified so user is aware

description: NODE_PATH unconditionally exported with plugin node_modules prepended; affects all child Node.js processes spawned during diagnostic run and could shadow system modules
suggested_fix: Pass NODE_PATH as prefix on specific node invocations rather than exporting globally: NODE_PATH=... node ...

description: execSync(`codesign --sign - --force "${binaryPath}"`) runs through system shell with binaryPath derived from CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT env var; double-quotes provide basic protection but env var origin is untrusted
suggested_fix: Use execFileSync(['codesign', '--sign', '-', '--force', binaryPath]) to avoid shell interpretation entirely

description: Core dependencies use ^ semver ranges: better-sqlite3 ^12.6.2 (native module), @modelcontextprotocol/sdk ^1.26.0, zod ^3.25.0; native module especially risky as minor updates can introduce ABI breaks or supply-chain compromise
suggested_fix: Pin better-sqlite3 to exact version; run pnpm audit in CI; consider exact pinning for all production dependencies

description: Root CLAUDE.md documents bare tool names (execute, search, batch_execute) while all other artifacts use ctx_-prefixed names confirmed canonical by hooks.json MCP matchers and configs/claude-code/CLAUDE.md; root CLAUDE.md appears to be a stale pre-ctx_-prefix copy
suggested_fix: Update root CLAUDE.md to use ctx_ prefix throughout Tool Selection and Rules sections; verify it matches configs/claude-code/CLAUDE.md tool inventory

suggested_fix: Replace body with OpenCode-style instruction text: 'The Plannotator Archive has been triggered. Opening the archive browser... Acknowledge and wait for the user to finish browsing.' Remove allowed-tools and disable-model-invocation frontmatter fields.

description: File contains only a description field in frontmatter; the body is empty. Agent receives no instructions when the command is invoked. All other OpenCode commands (review, annotate, archive) have a brief instruction body.

description: No branching for empty output or approval state. Hook and copilot variants enumerate 3 explicit cases (approved, dismissed/empty, annotated). OpenCode variant only says 'wait for the user's feedback' with no guidance on empty or approve outcomes.
suggested_fix: Add: 'If the review above contains feedback or annotations, address them. If no changes were requested, acknowledge and continue.' mirroring the hook variant.

description: No branching for approved/dismissed/annotated decision states. Hook variant explicitly handles all three decision outcomes. OpenCode variant only acknowledges opening the UI without instructing the agent on any outcome.
suggested_fix: Add explicit decision branches matching the hook command's three-case structure for approved, dismissed, and annotated outcomes.

description: Astro content-collection docs page included in NL artifact scan. Has valid title/description frontmatter but carries no agent-instruction semantics. Scored as documentation type; well-written reference material.
suggested_fix: No fix needed; confirm with NLPM scanner classification rules whether Astro docs pages should be excluded from the command artifact scan.

description: Pattern `curl -fsSL https://plannotator.ai/install.sh | bash` detected inside usage() heredoc at line 57
fp_reason: The pattern appears exclusively inside a `cat <<'USAGE'` heredoc that is printed to stdout as documentation text; it is never executed by the script
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should distinguish between executed code and inert heredoc/string literals printed to stdout

description: Pattern `curl -fsSL https://plannotator.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --version vX.Y.Z` detected inside usage() heredoc at line 58
fp_reason: Same as line 57: inert documentation text inside a heredoc, never executed
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should distinguish between executed code and inert heredoc/string literals printed to stdout

description: Pattern `curl -fsSL https://plannotator.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --verify-attestation` detected inside usage() heredoc at line 59
fp_reason: Same as lines 57-58: inert documentation text inside a heredoc, never executed
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should distinguish between executed code and inert heredoc/string literals printed to stdout

pattern: subprocess with variable interpolation in code execution context
description: `node -e` block at lines 508-514 embeds unquoted shell variables $GEMINI_SETTINGS and $PLANNOTATOR_HOOK directly into the JavaScript source string. If $HOME contains shell metacharacters or quotes the injected JavaScript is malformed. Both variables are OS-controlled or hardcoded so actual exploit surface is negligible, but the pattern is architecturally unsafe.
suggested_fix: Pass $GEMINI_SETTINGS as a CLI argument (node -e '...' -- "$GEMINI_SETTINGS") or write it to a temp file instead of string-interpolating it into the JS source.

description: Pattern `curl ... | bash` detected inside usage() heredoc (identical copy of scripts/install.sh)
fp_reason: Inert documentation text in heredoc, never executed — identical to the false positive in scripts/install.sh
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should detect whether the match is inside an executed command or a string literal

pattern: subprocess with variable interpolation in code execution context
description: `node -e` block with $GEMINI_SETTINGS interpolation — identical to scripts/install.sh finding; same risk and same fix applies
suggested_fix: Same as scripts/install.sh: pass path as CLI argument rather than interpolating into JS source string.

description: curl to GitHub API to resolve latest release tag: `curl -fsSL https://api.github.com/repos/$REPO/releases/latest`. Network call to external service on every install; result parsed via grep+cut without JSON validation.
suggested_fix: Acceptable for an installer; ensure $REPO is never externally controlled. Currently hardcoded constant — no action needed.

description: curl downloads binary and SHA256 checksum from GitHub Releases. SHA256 is verified before install; SLSA attestation optionally verified via `gh attestation verify`. Download chain is intact and well-guarded.
suggested_fix: No action needed — SHA256 + optional SLSA attestation provides adequate integrity checks.

description: `pi install npm:@plannotator/pi-extension` — installs npm package at runtime during install script execution, only when the `pi` CLI is detected. Pulls from npm registry without additional integrity check.
suggested_fix: Consider adding `--integrity` pinning or checking npm package provenance before install; or accept as low risk given the controlled package name.

description: `git clone --depth 1 --sparse` from GitHub to install skills assets. Clones at `$latest_tag` which is resolved earlier in the script; tag is not pinned to a commit SHA so a force-pushed tag could serve different content.
suggested_fix: After sparse clone, verify the checked-out commit SHA against a known-good value, or use `git clone --branch $latest_tag` combined with `git rev-parse HEAD` logging for audit trail.

description: @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk: ^0.2.92 — caret range permits minor/patch upgrades without explicit review
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version or use bun.lock enforcement; consider adding to update-deps skill's integrity audit workflow.

description: @openai/codex-sdk: ^0.118.0 — caret range
suggested_fix: Pin or enforce via lockfile; review via update-deps skill before bumping.

description: @opencode-ai/sdk: ^1.3.0 — caret range
suggested_fix: Pin or enforce via lockfile.

description: @pierre/diffs: ^1.1.12 — caret range; shadow DOM internals make surprise minor-version breaks high-impact per pierre-guard skill
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version ~1.1.12 and gate upgrades through the pierre-guard verification checklist.

description: PreToolUse/EnterPlanMode hook calling `plannotator improve-context` (timeout: 5s) is present in hooks.json but absent from scripts/install.sh. Marketplace install gets this hook; install.sh users do not. The improve-context subcommand is also undocumented in the audited files.
suggested_fix: Either add the EnterPlanMode hook to the install.sh installation block, or document the discrepancy and clarify which distribution channels include this hook.

description: Snippet does not include the `experimental.plan: true` block present in the auto-generated Gemini settings produced by install.sh (lines 530-550). Manual applicants will miss the Gemini plan-mode enablement.
suggested_fix: Add experimental.plan: true to the snippet or add a visible note that plan mode must be separately enabled.

description: "Nano Banana Pro" originally flagged as placeholder text; verification confirmed it is the official Google codename/marketing name for the gemini-3-pro-image-preview model family (https://blog.google/technology/ai/nano-banana-pro/)
fp_reason: Nano Banana Pro is a real Google product/codename for the gemini-3-pro-image-preview image-generation model, not unreplaced placeholder text
rule_gap: BUG-placeholder-text rule should not flag marketing/product names paired with backticked model IDs as placeholders; needs an allowlist of known Google model codenames or a heuristic that recognizes "<codename> for <backticked-model-id>" as a valid pairing

description: Step 1 code block re-derives REPO_ROOT via git rev-parse --show-toplevel, while Step 2 in the same agent explicitly warns 'Do NOT re-derive REPO_ROOT via git rev-parse'. Contradictory instructions confuse the model; in worktree sessions the re-derived value may point to the worktree root instead of the orchestrator repo root, causing context-lookup greps to return no matches and producing false-positive review findings.
suggested_fix: Annotate Step 1/Step 3 code blocks to clarify they use git rev-parse ONLY for shim path resolution. Update Step 2 warning to say 'Do NOT use the git-rev-parse REPO_ROOT for source-file context lookups — use the REPO_ROOT from your dispatch prompt instead.'

description: Same self-contradicting REPO_ROOT guidance as huge-diff-reviewer-standard.md. Step 1 code block uses git rev-parse; Step 2 warns against it. In worktree sessions this ambiguity may cause the agent to use the worktree path for source-file lookups, degrading review accuracy.
suggested_fix: Annotate Step 1/Step 3 code blocks; update Step 2 warning to distinguish shim-path resolution (git rev-parse OK) from source-file context lookups (use dispatch-prompt REPO_ROOT).

description: Same self-contradicting REPO_ROOT guidance. Line 61 (Step 1) re-derives REPO_ROOT; line 74 warns against it; line 207 (Step 3) re-derives again. Three occurrences of the contradiction in one agent file.
suggested_fix: Annotate all three occurrences; update the warning at line 74 to clarify shim-path vs context-lookup distinction.

description: The RESULT schema field 'taxonomy_item' is documented as 'Name of the failure mode from the 15-point taxonomy' but the taxonomy section defines 16 failure modes. The frontmatter description correctly states '16-point failure taxonomy'. The schema comment is stale. Consumers validating taxonomy_item values against a count-based expectation may incorrectly flag the 16th mode (Cascade Failure) as out-of-schema.
suggested_fix: Change 'from the 15-point taxonomy' to 'from the 16-point taxonomy' at line 112.

description: plan-review.md is the only agent with zero worked examples. The agent body is 5 lines and defers all logic to an external prompt template. Without an example, consumers cannot confirm the expected output format without reading the external template file.
suggested_fix: Add at least 1 worked example showing artifact_type input and the corresponding PASS/FAIL/REVIEW output. Two examples (one pass, one fail) would eliminate this penalty entirely.

description: All 32 SKILL.md files lack a 'model:' field in YAML frontmatter, incurring -5 per file (160 aggregate quality points). This is by design: skills inherit model from their dispatching orchestrator. The representative finding is filed against sprint/SKILL.md; the same finding applies to all 32 skill files.
suggested_fix: Add a rule override in .claude/nlpm.local.md to suppress NL-MISSING-MODEL for the plugins/dso/skills/** scope: {rule: NL-MISSING-MODEL, scope: 'plugins/dso/skills/**', action: suppress, reason: 'Skills inherit model from dispatching orchestrator'}

description: All 23 .claude/commands/*.md dispatch shims lack YAML frontmatter (name, description, model, allowed-tools), have zero examples, and have no output format specification. Each scores 15/100. These are auto-generated infrastructure shims by design. The representative finding is filed against sprint.md; the pattern applies to all 23 shim files. Score impact: 23 x (100 - 15) = 1955 aggregate quality-point deficit.
suggested_fix: Either (a) add a minimal 3-line frontmatter block to the shim template (name + description + allowed-tools) to raise each shim from 15 to ~60, or (b) exclude .claude/commands/ dispatch shims from NL artifact scoring via a scope exclusion in .claude/nlpm.local.md.

description: eval is used to execute the raw output of read-config.sh --batch. The code comment claims safety ('read-config.sh --batch single-quotes every value') but the safety invariant is not enforced at the call site. A quoting defect in read-config.sh or a malformed config value containing single-quote escapes could allow code injection.
suggested_fix: Replace eval with line-by-line KEY=VALUE parsing using read + variable assignment without eval, or validate the batch output against a strict KEY='value' regex before eval.

description: The hook attempts to start the Docker daemon via sudo on Linux systems. sudo -n succeeds silently when the user has passwordless sudo configured (NOPASSWD). A compromised hook environment could leverage this to escalate privileges or execute arbitrary systemctl commands. The fail-open design (returns 1 on failure) prevents blocking normal operation but does not prevent the privilege escalation attempt.
suggested_fix: Remove the automatic sudo systemctl start docker. Emit an actionable error message directing the user to start Docker manually before running the hook.

description: Downloads the Atlassian CLI binary from https://acli.atlassian.com and executes it without verifying a SHA-256 checksum or GPG signature. HTTPS provides transport integrity but not content integrity against a compromised CDN, intercepting proxy, or URL hijack. The downloaded binary is immediately chmod +x'd and executed.
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific known-good version and verify the published SHA-256 checksum before executing. Alternatively, require acli to be pre-installed by the user rather than downloading it at runtime.

description: Makes outbound HTTP POST requests to Slack and Microsoft Teams webhook URLs via urllib.request.urlopen(). URLs are read from SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL / TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL env vars or a config file. Feature is opt-in and documented but is not disclosed in SKILL.md and lacks timeout or error-handling guards.
suggested_fix: Add a '### Network Access' section to SKILL.md disclosing the outbound calls. Wrap urlopen() with timeout=10 and a try/except block that logs failures rather than propagating exceptions.

evidence: subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=300) where cmd is assembled as a list[str] with no shell=True argument. Shell injection requires shell=True; list-form invocation is safe.
description: Pre-scan matched subprocess.run pattern. Investigation confirms the call uses list-form argument with shell=False (default). No injection surface exists.
fp_reason: subprocess.run with a list argument and implicit shell=False has no command-injection surface; the pre-scan rule matched on subprocess.run presence without inspecting the argument type.
rule_gap: Rule should require shell=True to be present and True, not just subprocess.run presence, to reduce false positives on safe list-form invocations.

evidence: Frontmatter block for ci-guard.md contains description and argument-hint fields but no allowed-tools field. Pattern confirmed across all 11 commands with valid frontmatter.
description: 11 of 11 slash commands with valid frontmatter omit the `allowed-tools` field. Without this field Claude Code cannot enforce tool boundaries. Affected files: ci-guard, sync-branch, run-release, sync-todos-to-github, review, security-scan, git/rv, git/cp, git/sc, git/cm, git/pr. Representative file shown.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools:` listing to each command's frontmatter with the specific tools the command uses (e.g., Bash, Read, Write, Grep).

evidence: 'comprehensive' appears 6 times, 'intelligent' once, 'data-driven' used as capability descriptor without quantified threshold. Pattern confirmed across all 14 SKILL.md files (average 5–6 vague terms per file).
description: High-density vague quantifiers across all 14 skill definitions: 'comprehensive', 'intelligent', 'seamless', 'advanced', 'actionable', 'data-driven' used without measurable criteria. Worst offender: tech-stack-evaluator (8 instances). Systematic output of the factory generation template. Representative file shown.
suggested_fix: Replace vague adjectives with measurable criteria (e.g., 'detects CVEs from the last 12 months' instead of 'comprehensive security analysis'; 'scores 0–100 weighted by user-defined priorities' instead of 'intelligent comparison').

suggested_fix: Replace 'comprehensive hooks' with 'hooks covering all 7 Claude Code event types'; replace 'seamlessly integrates' with specific integration mechanism.

evidence: Command body uses $1 and $2 for PLAN_TITLE and secondary argument extraction. Claude Code slash commands receive all arguments as a single $ARGUMENTS string; $1 and $2 are not set at runtime.
description: Uses positional shell variables $1/$2 to parse slash command arguments. Claude Code supplies arguments via $ARGUMENTS only; at runtime $1 and $2 expand to empty strings, silently producing a GitHub issue with an empty title.
suggested_fix: Replace $1/$2 with $ARGUMENTS and parse the combined string within the bash block (e.g., PLAN_TITLE=$(echo "$ARGUMENTS" | cut -d' ' -f1)).

description: The generated-skills/CLAUDE.md catalog is out of sync with the repository: 5 confirmed skills are present on disk but absent from the 'Available Skills' section. Users browsing the catalog to discover skills will miss roughly one-third of the available catalog.
suggested_fix: Add catalog entries for tdd-guide, tech-stack-evaluator, social-media-analyzer, scrum-master-agent, and codex-cli-bridge to generated-skills/CLAUDE.md following the existing entry format (location, files, key classes, use cases, size).

evidence: Phase 5 body: 'Use run_shell_command mkdir -p conductor/tracks/{trackId}/.message-bus/board/' — tool not in frontmatter tools list which only declares read_file and write_file
description: Agent body explicitly instructs use of run_shell_command to create the board message-bus directory, but run_shell_command is not declared in the frontmatter tools list; the mkdir call will fail at runtime
suggested_fix: Add run_shell_command to the tools list in the agent frontmatter: tools: [read_file, write_file, run_shell_command]

evidence: File opens with raw markdown heading '# Business Docs Sync' with no preceding YAML frontmatter block
description: Skill has no YAML frontmatter; both required name and description fields are absent; the skill is invisible to the Skill tool and cannot be discovered or invoked
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter: --- name: business-docs-sync description: Use when completing a track that changes pricing, AI models, product features, or asset pipelines — syncs business context documents ---

evidence: File opens with '# UI/UX Audit Report' heading with no YAML frontmatter; scanning commands/ yields no name or description field
description: Report template placed inside commands/ directory with no YAML frontmatter; it cannot be invoked as a command and will confuse artifact discovery tools
suggested_fix: Move to docs/ or templates/ directory; if intended as a command, add frontmatter with name: ui-audit-report and appropriate description

evidence: Line 25: curl -s --max-time 3 "https://api.github.com/repos/${REPO}/releases/latest" — REPO is hardcoded to Ibrahim-3d/orchestrator-supaconductor; runs unconditionally on every SessionStart hook invocation
description: Outbound curl call to GitHub API on every Claude Code session start to check for plugin updates; makes an unconditional network request to an external service with no caching, adding latency to each session start
suggested_fix: Cache the update check result (e.g., write a timestamp+version to ~/.cache/supaconductor/last-check) and skip the curl call if the cache is less than 24 hours old; consider making the check opt-in via a config flag

description: escalateToBoard helper spawns 'claude --print --model opus /orchestrator-supaconductor:board-meeting {question}' and invoke_superpower() interpolates $superpower and $params from track metadata into a shell command without sanitization; a track name or goal containing shell metacharacters (backtick, semicolon, $()) could inject arbitrary commands
suggested_fix: Validate that track IDs and question strings contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, spaces, and periods before interpolating into shell commands; use printf '%q' or equivalent shell quoting for dynamic values

evidence: Command body: 'Use agent at .claude/agents/orchestrator-supaconductor:loop-planner.md' — path uses legacy direct-file convention
description: Command body references the legacy .claude/agents/orchestrator-supaconductor:loop-planner.md path format; current Claude Code convention dispatches agents via the Task tool or Skill tool invocation, not by direct file path
suggested_fix: Replace path reference with Task tool invocation: Task subagent_type general-purpose with the loop-planner agent's prompt, or reference via Skill tool as orchestrator-supaconductor:loop-planner

evidence: Command body references .claude/agents/orchestrator-supaconductor:task-worker.md — same stale-path pattern
description: Command body references legacy direct-file agent path format for task-worker agent
suggested_fix: Replace with Task tool invocation or Skill tool reference to orchestrator-supaconductor:task-worker

evidence: Command body references .claude/agents/orchestrator-supaconductor:loop-executor.md — same stale-path pattern
description: Command body references legacy direct-file agent path format for loop-executor agent
suggested_fix: Replace with Task tool invocation or Skill tool reference to orchestrator-supaconductor:loop-executor

evidence: Command file is named evaluate-plan.md but body instructs 'Use /loop-plan-evaluator' which is the old command name
description: Command body contains stale cross-reference to /loop-plan-evaluator; the canonical invocation for this command is /evaluate-plan (matching the file name); creates user confusion especially since both commands exist
suggested_fix: Update the body to reference /evaluate-plan or /orchestrator-supaconductor:evaluate-plan; remove or deprecate the duplicate commands/loop-plan-evaluator.md

evidence: Frontmatter field: model: inherit — not a recognized Claude model ID (expected sonnet, opus, haiku, or a concrete model string)
description: model: inherit is not a valid model identifier; executor cannot determine appropriate model tier; likely intended to inherit from the calling agent but this convention is not supported in standard Claude Code frontmatter
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete model ID such as model: claude-sonnet-4-6, or omit the field to use the plugin default

evidence: Frontmatter has no allowed-tools field; applies to all 48 commands with this same omission
description: No allowed-tools field in frontmatter; the harness cannot restrict tool access for this command (applies to 48/49 commands in this plugin — only representative finding logged)
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools with the minimal set required; e.g., allowed-tools: [Agent] for commands that only dispatch subagents

evidence: Agent body contains extensive TypeScript pseudocode and orchestration logic but no ## Examples section or examples: frontmatter field with concrete invocation samples
description: No example invocation blocks present; this is the most complex agent in the plugin (master orchestrator) yet users have no worked examples showing how to trigger it or what output to expect (applies to 11/15 agents — representative finding logged)
suggested_fix: Add at least two example blocks: one showing /go invocation for a new feature, one showing /conductor implement resuming an in-progress track, both with expected orchestrator output

evidence: Description: 'Master coordinator for the Evaluate-Loop workflow v3. Supports GOAL-DRIVEN entry, PARALLEL execution via worker agents, BOARD OF DIRECTORS deliberation, and message bus coordination...' — summarizes capabilities instead of triggering conditions
description: Skill description summarizes the v3 feature set rather than describing triggering conditions; per writing-skills CSO guidance, descriptions that summarize workflow cause Claude to follow the description summary instead of loading and reading the full skill body
suggested_fix: Rewrite description to focus on when to invoke: e.g., 'Use when executing a Conductor track end-to-end — starting from /go or /conductor implement, detecting current loop step, and dispatching the appropriate agent'

evidence: Agent body describes a multi-phase deliberation protocol but contains no examples: section in frontmatter and no ## Examples section in body
description: Board of Directors agent has no example invocations; users cannot see what a properly formatted board-meeting request looks like or what output to expect
suggested_fix: Add an Examples section showing a typical EVALUATE_PLAN board invocation with a sample proposal and the expected JSON verdict output

evidence: commands/brainstorm.md and commands/brainstorming.md both invoke the brainstorming skill with identical behavior; no functional differentiation
description: Two near-identical commands (brainstorm and brainstorming) targeting the same skill; creates discovery ambiguity and maintenance burden
suggested_fix: Remove one command and add an alias note to the surviving command's description; recommend keeping brainstorming (full name) and removing brainstorm

suggested_fix: Remove inline Node.js block; hook is already declared in plugins/oh-my-paper/hooks/hooks.json and will be registered at install time

suggested_fix: Publish on-session-start.mjs, on-task-complete.mjs, and on-stage-transition.mjs in the repo; add inline docstrings describing what data each reads and writes

evidence: None of the 10 agent files contain a model: field in frontmatter (or any frontmatter at all)
description: All 10 agent files lack model specification; Claude Code will use the user default model for all agent invocations regardless of task complexity
suggested_fix: Add model: claude-sonnet-4-6 to orchestrator/reviewer/writer/experiment agents; model: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 to literature-scout for cost efficiency

evidence: None of the 10 agent files contain an examples: section or any <example> block
description: All 10 agent files lack examples; agents without examples cannot be reliably routed or regression-tested
suggested_fix: Add at least one <example> block per agent showing a representative task input and the expected output artifact

evidence: description field contains exactly: # bioinformatics-init-analysis — a repeated Markdown heading, not a description
description: Skill description is a placeholder Markdown heading rather than a real description; skill cannot be discovered by semantic routing
suggested_fix: Replace with a real description: Initialize a bioinformatics analysis pipeline: QC raw reads, align to reference, call variants, and produce a summary report

evidence: Frontmatter block is present but contains no description field
description: Skill missing description frontmatter field; skill cannot be matched to task descriptions by routing logic
suggested_fix: Add description field explaining the skill's purpose and when to use it

evidence: Frontmatter block is present but contains no description field; file body is minimal (< 10 lines)
description: Skill missing description frontmatter and has near-empty body; provides no routing signal and minimal guidance
suggested_fix: Add description and populate the skill body with handoff protocol steps

evidence: Frontmatter block is present but contains no description field
description: Skill missing description frontmatter field; skill cannot be matched to task descriptions
suggested_fix: Add description field explaining the skill's purpose

evidence: Skill body contains section titled Eason's Workflow Requirements with personal rclone remote names and Overleaf project IDs
description: Skill contains personalized section with one person's rclone configuration that will not work in other users' environments
suggested_fix: Replace personal configuration values with parameterized placeholders such as <your-rclone-remote> and <your-overleaf-project-id>

evidence: Skill references server/scripts/research-news/ as an execution path; this path is an upstream project artifact not present in a standard install
description: Skill references hardcoded upstream project path server/scripts/research-news/ that will not exist in user installs
suggested_fix: Bundle the script under .claude/skills/research-news/scripts/ and reference it with a relative path, or describe the workflow without filesystem dependencies

evidence: Skill tools list contains update_pipeline which is not a standard Claude Code built-in or MCP tool
description: Skill declares non-existent tool update_pipeline; agents attempting to call it will receive a tool-not-found error
suggested_fix: Remove update_pipeline from tools list; use Write tool to update .pipeline/tasks/tasks.json directly

evidence: Skill body ends with a promotional section for a commercial product called K-Dense Web unrelated to the peer-review workflow
description: Skill contains commercial upsell for K-Dense Web at end; unrelated to the review task and will confuse agents parsing output instructions
suggested_fix: Remove the commercial promotional section from the skill body

evidence: Frontmatter contains stages: [] — empty list, no execution stages declared
description: Skill has empty stages list; agents routing by stage type will not match this skill even when it is the appropriate choice
suggested_fix: Populate stages with actual execution phases such as [setup, launch, monitor, collect]

evidence: Directory listing shows skills/ and src-tauri/resources/skills/ contain identical sets of 34 skill files by name
description: Skills are duplicated verbatim across src-tauri/resources/skills/ and skills/; fixes applied to one copy will not propagate to the other causing silent behavioral divergence
suggested_fix: Designate one directory as authoritative and symlink or add CI diff-check to prevent divergence; document which path is read by which runtime

description: Entire file is indented with 4 leading spaces; YAML front-matter delimiters (---) never appear at column 0, so the name, description, and model fields are not parsed by any standard YAML front-matter processor.
suggested_fix: Remove the 4-space indent from every line so the opening --- appears at character position 0 on the first line of the file.

description: Zero example blocks across all 100 agents in this repository; no worked invocation scenarios exist to verify correct agent behaviour. Representative file shown; penalty applies to every agent file.
suggested_fix: Add an ## Examples section to each agent with at least one concrete input/output scenario demonstrating invocation.

description: 9 vague quantifiers in a single file (appropriate×2, comprehensive×3, efficient×3, clear×2) without measurable thresholds; repo-wide pattern affects all 100 agents. Worst-case file shown; median penalty across repo is -12.
suggested_fix: Replace vague terms with measurable criteria, e.g. replace 'comprehensive test coverage' with 'tests achieve >=80% branch coverage'.

description: Description reads 'Create a Claude Code Agent that is an expert in the Gin web framework...' — self-referential meta-phrasing instead of a direct statement of the agent's purpose and capabilities.
suggested_fix: Rewrite as 'Expert in the Gin web framework for Go, focusing on efficient web server implementation and route optimization.'

description: Blank lines appear between each key-value pair in the YAML front matter. The YAML spec permits this but it violates the de-facto Claude Code agent file convention and may trip strict front-matter parsers.
suggested_fix: Remove blank lines between frontmatter fields so the block is contiguous between the opening and closing --- delimiters.

description: Beyond the frontmatter bug, the body contains 6+ vague quantifiers (appropriate, efficient, comprehensive, etc.) that would reduce quality score even if the indentation bug were fixed.
suggested_fix: After fixing indentation, replace vague terms with measurable specifics (e.g. 'visibility timeout set to 2x the P99 processing time' instead of 'appropriate visibility timeout').

description: CLAUDE.md lacks YAML frontmatter; name and description fields are absent, making the file non-machine-readable by NLPM scanners
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter block with name and description fields at the top of the file

description: No worked invocation examples showing user prompt to expected Claude behavior; each skill is described in prose only
suggested_fix: Add a concrete example block showing trigger phrase and resulting behavior for at least one skill

description: Skill descriptions in CLAUDE.md are one-sentence summaries rather than behavioral specifications; standalone file is under-informative without the skill files
suggested_fix: Expand each skill entry with one sentence describing its output and any preconditions (e.g., dev server required)

description: Vague quantifier 'relevant' at 'always suggest what's relevant' with no criteria defining what qualifies as relevant for a given tech stack
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit criteria: 'suggest reviewers that directly address gaps in the detected tech stack (e.g., i18n reviewer for multi-locale sites)'

description: No installation or invocation instructions — CLAUDE.md has no 'claude plugin install' command or equivalent usage guidance
suggested_fix: Add a 'Usage' section with: claude plugin install simmer@2389-research --scope project, and the trigger phrases that activate the skill.

description: tests/integration/simmer-scenario.md exists but CLAUDE.md provides no command or instructions to run the integration tests
suggested_fix: Add a 'Testing' section referencing tests/integration/simmer-scenario.md and how to execute the scenarios.

description: More than 60% of CLAUDE.md is descriptive content (overview, tables, flow diagrams, key design decisions) rather than actionable instructions
suggested_fix: Add installation, development workflow, and test commands to raise the actionable content ratio above 40%.

description: CLAUDE.md has no prerequisites section — does not state 'none required' or list required tools/versions for the plugin
suggested_fix: Add a 'Prerequisites' section. For a pure-markdown plugin this can be a single line: 'None — no build step or external dependencies required.'

description: Orchestrator SKILL.md is 550 lines, exceeding the 500-line ceiling; the single-agent mode section (lines ~405-418) and context discipline table add ~35 lines that could be extracted
suggested_fix: Extract the single-agent mode section to a linked reference or trim non-essential commentary to get under 500 lines.

description: Vague quantifier 'appropriate' in '(or appropriate extension matching artifact type)' — no measurable criteria for extension selection
suggested_fix: Replace with an enumeration: '(matching the artifact type: .md for text/prompts, .yaml for config, .py for code, etc.)'.

description: Vague quantifier 'relevant' in 'Judges must read the relevant artifacts before scoring' — no specification of which artifacts are relevant
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit list: 'Judges must read the candidate file, evaluator script, config files, and prior candidate files before scoring.'

description: Vague quantifier 'relevant' in 'Reference prior iterations when relevant' — no condition specified for when prior iterations are relevant
suggested_fix: Replace with a condition: 'Reference prior iterations when the ASI builds on, contradicts, or repeats a previous direction.'

description: Vague quantifier 'relevant' in 'Give each judge relevant capabilities from the primitive library' — no criteria for which capabilities are relevant to which judge roles
suggested_fix: Replace with role-based guidance: 'Give each judge capabilities matched to their role — see example compositions table for which primitives apply to evaluator analyst, constraint realist, etc.'

description: Vague quantifier 'relevant' in 'Apply relevant ones to each judge based on their role'
suggested_fix: Remove this sentence — the preceding example compositions table already specifies which primitives apply to which roles, making this redundant guidance.

description: Vague quantifier 'relevant' in 'read the candidate, evaluator output, and relevant code/config' — 'relevant' does not specify which code or config files to read
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'read the candidate, evaluator output, evaluator script, and config files.'

description: Vague quantifier 'relevant' in 'Extract what's relevant to the criteria' — no measurable guidance for which evaluator output maps to which criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Extract failure counts, metric values, and error patterns that map directly to each scoring criterion.'

description: Vague quantifier 'relevant' in 'search for relevant techniques' — no specification of what makes a technique relevant
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'search for solutions to the specific failure pattern identified (e.g., post-processing normalization for fuzzy-match failures, chain-of-thought prompting for multi-step reasoning failures).'

description: Vague quantifier 'some' in 'If some fields are missing or ambiguous' — 'some' provides no measurable threshold for when the conversational path is triggered vs. skipping to Phase 5
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'If any required field (artifact path/content, criteria descriptions, evaluation method) cannot be inferred from inspection alone,'

description: CLAUDE.md directory structure listing (lines 82-91) omits skills/simmer-judge-board/ — the subdirectory exists on disk and simmer-judge-board is referenced in the Subskills table at line 20, but the directory tree is incomplete
suggested_fix: Add simmer-judge-board/ and its SKILL.md entry to the directory tree in CLAUDE.md, between simmer-judge/ and simmer-reflect/.

description: skill/SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter; `name` field is absent, breaking skill registration and display in Claude Code

description: Documented install method is `curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AgriciDaniel/claude-blog/main/install.sh | bash`. An attacker who compromises the GitHub repository or performs MITM on raw.githubusercontent.com can deliver and execute arbitrary code on the installer's machine without any signature verification.
suggested_fix: Replace with a pinned-SHA install that verifies a GPG or SHA-256 signature before execution, or use a package manager distribution method (npm, pip, claude plugin install) that has integrity checking built in.

description: `"command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@ycse/nanobanana-mcp"]` auto-installs and executes an npm package every time the MCP server starts. The `-y` flag suppresses the confirmation prompt. A typosquatted or compromised `@ycse/nanobanana-mcp` package would silently execute in Claude Code's process context.
suggested_fix: Pin to an exact version (`@ycse/nanobanana-mcp@x.y.z`) and verify the package's published SHA-256. Consider bundling the MCP server locally to eliminate the network dependency at startup.

description: `script_path = skill_dir / "scripts" / script_name` where `script_name` is taken directly from `sys.argv[1]` without sanitization. Python's pathlib does not strip `../` sequences; a crafted argument such as `../../../../../../bin/python` resolves outside the scripts directory. The same pattern appears in skills/blog-audio/scripts/run.py:73 and skills/blog-google/scripts/run.py:73.
suggested_fix: Add `script_name = Path(script_name).name` (basename only, strips all directory components) before constructing `script_path`, and reject names containing `/` or `..`.

description: `pip3 install --quiet -r requirements.txt` is called without `--require-hashes`. `requirements.txt` uses range pins (`>=0.7.3,<1.0.0`) but no hash verification. A dependency-confusion or typosquatting attack against `textstat` or `beautifulsoup4` on PyPI could substitute a malicious build at install time.
suggested_fix: Add `--require-hashes` to the pip call and generate pinned hashes via `pip-compile --generate-hashes requirements.in > requirements.txt`.

description: References `references/cta-placement.md` at lines 161 and 165, but this file is not listed in CLAUDE.md's architecture section (which enumerates 13 of the stated 14 reference files). If the file is absent from the installed skill directory, the writer agent silently misses CTA placement guidance.
suggested_fix: Either add `cta-placement.md` to the repository and list it in CLAUDE.md's reference file inventory, or remove the two references from blog-write/SKILL.md if CTA guidance is covered elsewhere.

description: CLAUDE.md states 14 reference files in the architecture overview but lists only 13 by name. `skills/blog-write/SKILL.md` references `references/cta-placement.md` which is not in the named list — likely the 14th file — creating an inconsistency between the stated count and the documented inventory.
suggested_fix: Add `references/cta-placement.md` to the reference file list in CLAUDE.md's architecture section to make the count and inventory consistent.

description: User message text from session JSONL files is interpolated into a Claude CLI prompt via subprocess.run; malicious session content could attempt prompt injection against the API call

description: Arbitrary user input stored in $PROMPT via 'echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ...' and then piped to grep; no eval or exec so risk is low, but embedded newlines can cause unexpected shell behavior

description: Bash tool command captured via 'echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ...' into $COMMAND, used only in string comparison; no exec risk but unquoted $INPUT is fragile

description: skill sets user_invocable: false but CLAUDE.md documents `/peon-ping-config` as a user-accessible slash command. This contradiction may prevent the skill from appearing in autocomplete.
suggested_fix: Change `user_invocable: false` to `user_invocable: true` to match the documented behavior in CLAUDE.md, or remove the field and rely on default behavior.

description: Uses hardcoded `~/.claude/hooks/peon-ping/peon.sh` instead of `${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}`. All other skills (peon-ping-toggle, peon-ping-use, peon-ping-config, peon-ping-rename) use the config dir pattern. This breaks for users with non-default CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, including some Cursor setups.
suggested_fix: Replace `~/.claude/hooks/peon-ping/peon.sh` with `"${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}"/hooks/peon-ping/peon.sh` consistent with other skills.

description: Skill file has no explicit Output Format section. The document describes behavior and examples but does not specify what the assistant should output after completing the configuration change.

description: 'ensure' used as an instruction verb.
suggested_fix: Replace 'ensure' with specific action.

pattern: unverified remote registry controls download sources
description: Registry JSON fetched from peonping.github.io controls source_repo, source_ref, and source_path for all pack downloads. A compromised registry or MITM could redirect downloads to attacker-controlled GitHub repos. Input validation (is_safe_source_repo) limits but does not eliminate risk.
suggested_fix: Pin a registry content hash or use subresource integrity; add a --no-registry flag that forces fallback-only mode; document the trust model in security policy.

pattern: no integrity check on initial file download
description: Sound files are downloaded via curl and checksums are stored, but checksums are only verified on subsequent cache hits, not on the initial download. A tampered file downloaded for the first time would pass silently.
suggested_fix: After downloading each file, verify against a manifest-provided checksum if available; at minimum warn if no expected checksum is available.

pattern: bash variable interpolation into PowerShell -Command string
description: Variables rgb_r, rgb_g, rgb_b, dismiss_secs, icon_win_path, y_offset interpolated directly into PowerShell -Command string in WSL popup path. icon_win_path derives from wslpath which could return paths with characters that break the PowerShell string literal.
suggested_fix: Pass all bash-derived values through temp file or use -File with named parameters, following the same pattern already used for the message text.

pattern: bash variable interpolation into PowerShell -Command string (MSYS2)
description: Same pattern as WSL path (finding #3) but for MSYS2. toast_xml_win from cygpath interpolated into PowerShell command string.
suggested_fix: Same fix as finding #3.

description: py_path result interpolated via single-quote wrapping in python3 -c '...' string. Single quotes in the path (possible on some systems) would break the Python string literal. The is_safe_filename validator uses a permissive charset that allows special characters.
suggested_fix: Use sys.argv[1] to pass the path: python3 -c 'import hashlib,sys; print(hashlib.sha256(open(sys.argv[1],"rb").read()).hexdigest())' "$(py_path "$1")"

description: Sends category name to localhost:19998 relay without authentication. Currently safe as category is derived from a hardcoded case statement. If the relay endpoint or category mapping were ever extended to include user input, this becomes a SSRF/injection vector.
suggested_fix: Document that relay endpoint is localhost-only and not externally accessible; add validation if category mapping ever becomes dynamic.

description: FALLBACK_REPO is hardcoded to 'PeonPing/og-packs' with no override mechanism. If the PeonPing GitHub org were compromised, all installs using the fallback path would silently download from the compromised repo.
suggested_fix: Document the fallback source trust model; add an env var override (PEON_FALLBACK_REPO) for enterprise users; consider pinning the fallback ref to a SHA instead of a tag.

description: SKILL.md sets user_invocable: false but CLAUDE.md section 'Skills' lists /peon-ping-config as a user-accessible slash command. Either the frontmatter is wrong or CLAUDE.md is wrong.
suggested_fix: Set user_invocable: true in skills/peon-ping-config/SKILL.md to match CLAUDE.md documentation.

pattern: hardcoded path vs config dir pattern
description: peon-ping-log uses ~/.claude/hooks/peon-ping/peon.sh while all other skills use ${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}. Creates silent failures for users with non-default config directories.
suggested_fix: Update peon-ping-log/SKILL.md to use ${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude} pattern.

description: Change Enforcement Rules reference files in peer repos (../openpeon/, ../homebrew-tap/, peonping-x-bot) that are not part of this repository. An AI agent working from a fresh clone cannot verify these. Rules are correct for human developers but create a verification gap for automated review.
suggested_fix: Add a note clarifying that cross-repo rules require manual coordination; consider linking to each repo with the specific file path.

description: fetch_latest_signals returns a 'formatted report' but no output structure (fields, types, section headings) is defined in the skill body
suggested_fix: Add a Markdown template showing the report sections (e.g., title, sentiment score, confidence, summary, sources list)
