description: Section heading '### 1. Track Signal Evolution' appears twice (lines 14 and 17)
suggested_fix: Remove the first occurrence at line 14

description: 'Set Appropriate Degrees of Freedom' — 'Appropriate' provides no measurable criteria for selecting constraint level

description: 'For simple edits, modify the XML directly' — 'simple' is undefined without specifying edit scope or complexity threshold

description: Code example imports KronosPredictorUtility from 'scripts.utils.kronos_predictor' but the file is at scripts/kronos_predictor.py (not scripts/utils/); raises ModuleNotFoundError at runtime
suggested_fix: Change import to 'from scripts.kronos_predictor import KronosPredictorUtility'

description: torch.load called without weights_only=True; pickle deserialization of an untrusted .pt file can execute arbitrary code
suggested_fix: Add weights_only=True parameter; handle TypeError for torch < 1.13 with an explicit version check rather than a broad except clause

description: Identical to predictor finding #1 — torch.load without weights_only=True in a duplicated evaluation script
suggested_fix: Same as predictor finding: add weights_only=True

description: Identical to predictor finding #1 — torch.load without weights_only=True in a duplicated evaluation script
suggested_fix: Same as predictor finding: add weights_only=True

description: Fallback path at line 83 drops weights_only=True when it raises TypeError/RuntimeError/AttributeError, silently bypassing the pickle safety guard
suggested_fix: Narrow the except to TypeError only (for old torch versions), and log a security warning when the fallback path is taken; consider failing closed instead

description: Outbound HTTP GET to https://newsnow.busiyi.world — a third-party Chinese aggregation service not under the repo owner's control; compromise of this host can inject malicious content into the agent's context
suggested_fix: Document the third-party dependency; add explicit SSLError handling and content-length limits on the response to prevent oversized payloads

description: ast.literal_eval applied to strings extracted from external API responses; safer than eval but processes untrusted input — a crafted response could trigger MemoryError or expose unexpected Python literals
suggested_fix: Add a content-length guard before calling literal_eval (e.g. raise if len(content) > 10000) to prevent MemoryError on adversarially large payloads

description: Example uses `?limit=50&sort=top` on GET /x/users/{id}/tweets; user-tweets SKILL.md explicitly states `limit` and `sort` are not supported by this endpoint
suggested_fix: Remove `limit=50&sort=top` from the example URL; use cursor-based pagination and client-side sort as documented in user-tweets

description: Endpoint row shows `GET /x/tweets/search?sort=top&min_faves=5000`; the sort mode parameter is `queryType=Top`, not `sort=top`
suggested_fix: Change to `GET /x/tweets/search?q=min_faves:5000&queryType=Top`

description: MCP config routes all tool calls through external server https://xquik.com/mcp via streamable-http; API key injected per user config
fp_reason: The external MCP server connection is the documented, intended mechanism of this plugin — it is a thin protocol adapter over the same first-party REST API; there is no unexpected exfiltration surface
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish intentional first-party MCP adapters from unexpected third-party network calls based on whether the target URL matches the plugin's declared API endpoint

description: devDependency `@tanstack/intent` pinned to `"latest"` — resolves to a moving target on each install, creating a supply-chain risk for package maintainers
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific semver version (e.g., `"1.0.0"`) in package.json

description: `prepublishOnly` lifecycle script runs `node scripts/check-versions.mjs`; script is a read-only version-drift checker with no network calls or shell exec
fp_reason: The script only reads local files and compares version strings; it runs exclusively on `npm publish` by the maintainer, not on user install; no code execution risk to end users
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish prepublishOnly (maintainer-only publish gate) from postinstall (runs on user npm install); the risk profiles are entirely different

description: Uses `tool=post_extractor` while every other skill in the repo uses `toolType`; contradicts user-tweets, extract-followers, who-retweeted, and all other extraction skills
suggested_fix: Align parameter key to `toolType` to match the canonical pattern across the repo

description: Endpoint table shows `sort=top` for search endpoint; search-tweets SKILL.md documents the correct parameter as `queryType=Top`
suggested_fix: Update the endpoint row to use `queryType=Top` and move engagement floor into the `q` parameter: `q=min_faves:5000&queryType=Top`

description: Command explicitly specifies SHA-256 hash verification (comparing `source_sha256` against live `shasum` output) and uses `awk`/Grep pattern inspection, but declares no `allowed-tools`. Without `Bash` and `Grep`, the hash comparison and pattern scanning steps cannot execute. The command's core functionality is non-functional as specified.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: ["Read", "Grep", "Bash"]` to frontmatter

description: 7-check validation table lists checks to perform but provides no numbered sequential workflow steps. The command has no procedural instruction for which tool to use per check or in what order. Agents cannot reliably reproduce consistent output without step ordering.
suggested_fix: Add a numbered workflow section beneath the check table (e.g., "Step 1: Glob all `memory/wiki/*.md` files…")

description: Plugin manifest declares `"version": "9.0.1"` while all 20 SKILL.md files declare `version: "9.0.0"` in their frontmatter. This creates version drift that `/seo:validate-library` check #6 is designed to catch, suggesting the sync step was skipped after a patch release.
suggested_fix: Run `/seo:sync-versions` to propagate the manifest version to all SKILL.md files, or bump plugin.json back to 9.0.0 if the patch was not yet applied to skills

description: 7 of 15 commands missing `allowed-tools` field entirely
suggested_fix: For dispatch-only commands, add `allowed-tools: []` (explicit empty). For commands that read files to gather context (p2-review, audit-domain), add the appropriate tool list. Follow `commands/validate-library.md` as the pattern: it fully declares `["Read", "Glob", "Grep", "Bash"]`

suggested_fix: Replace with measurable alternatives per scoring rules: "comprehensive" → "covering all 8 CORE-EEAT dimensions"; "appropriate" → the specific threshold value; "thorough" → "covering all checklist items"; "significant" → ">10% change"

description: Stop hook auto-appends critical veto items (`T04`, `C01`, `R10`, `T03`, `T05`, `T09`) to every session-end message. If the user has not run an audit, this produces confusing output referencing framework items with no prior context.
suggested_fix: Add a conditional: only emit veto reminders if an audit result exists in the current session context (e.g., check for `CORE-EEAT` or `CITE` in prior output)

description: "Experimental, v9.0+" label in CLAUDE.md but the command body does not repeat this caveat or define what "drift" threshold triggers an alert
suggested_fix: Define a numeric drift threshold in the command body (e.g., "GEO Score drop ≥5 points since last snapshot constitutes notable drift")

description: Contribution rules reference `scripts/sync-versions.py` and `scripts/validate-descriptions.py` in the past tense ("replaces scripts/...") but these files no longer exist in the repo. Stale references may confuse contributors.
suggested_fix: Remove the parenthetical "replaces scripts/..." notes from the Commands section, or replace with a link to the git commit that removed them

description: Both auditor-class skills carry the identical `source_sha256` and `block_sha256` values in their runbook-sync markers. If the shared Auditor Runbook §1-5 source is updated, both files must be re-synced atomically. There is no automated CI check verifying the inline block matches the source.
suggested_fix: Add a CI step (or a `/seo:contract-lint` check) that recomputes `block_sha256` from the inline content and fails if it differs from the declared value

description: Both auditor-class skills carry the identical `source_sha256` and `block_sha256` values in their runbook-sync markers. If the shared Auditor Runbook §1-5 source is updated, both files must be re-synced atomically. There is no automated CI check verifying the inline block matches the source.
suggested_fix: Add a CI step (or a `/seo:contract-lint` check) that recomputes `block_sha256` from the inline content and fails if it differs from the declared value

description: `metadata.geo-relevance` values are hardcoded ("high", "medium") with no documented scoring rubric explaining what differentiates "high" from "medium" for GEO relevance
suggested_fix: Add a one-line comment or footnote in CLAUDE.md defining the geo-relevance tiers (e.g., "high = skill directly produces AI-citation-ready content; medium = skill informs strategy that may affect GEO")

description: Include a scraping legality note ("verify robots.txt, respect Crawl-delay, confirm TOS permits automated access") but do not enforce or prompt the user to confirm before proceeding
suggested_fix: Add a mandatory user-confirmation checkpoint: "Before crawling [domain], confirm: (1) robots.txt permits access, (2) you have authorization. Proceed? [yes/no]"

description: Include a scraping legality note ("verify robots.txt, respect Crawl-delay, confirm TOS permits automated access") but do not enforce or prompt the user to confirm before proceeding
suggested_fix: Add a mandatory user-confirmation checkpoint: "Before crawling [domain], confirm: (1) robots.txt permits access, (2) you have authorization. Proceed? [yes/no]"

description: The FileChanged hook (matcher: "hot-cache.md") fires on any edit to hot-cache.md and instructs Claude to "check HOT tier size, trim to 80 lines if needed". If two concurrent sessions both edit hot-cache.md, the trim logic may produce a race condition
suggested_fix: Document in CLAUDE.md that the memory system assumes single-session usage; add a note that concurrent session writes to hot-cache.md are not supported

description: "Cross-project mode" is described but the command provides no guidance on how to discover which projects exist or where to find their `memory/` directories
suggested_fix: Add a discovery step: "Glob `../*/memory/hot-cache.md` to enumerate active projects, then prompt user to confirm scope before aggregating"

description: Banned-vocabulary list (crucial, robust, leverage, delve, etc.) is comprehensive but has no mechanism to enforce it — the skill instructs Claude not to use these words but has no self-check step
suggested_fix: Add a validation checkpoint at the end: "Before delivery, scan output for banned words. If any found, rewrite those sentences."

description: 11-step workflow integrates CORE-EEAT and CITE scores but does not specify which version of the scoring frameworks to use if the user has a custom override in `.claude/nlpm.local.md`
suggested_fix: Add: "If the project has a `.claude/nlpm.local.md` with threshold overrides, apply those thresholds to the CORE-EEAT and CITE pass/fail gates."

description: GDPR Art 17 deletion flow is documented but the skill does not specify what happens to cross-references in `memory/hot-cache.md` and `memory/decisions.md` when a subject-data record is deleted from WARM tier
suggested_fix: Add: "On deletion, also scan hot-cache.md and decisions.md for references to the deleted entity and remove or anonymize them."

description: The `Next Best Skill` section uses markdown links to GitHub URLs. If the repo is forked or the library is installed locally, these links break.
suggested_fix: Replace GitHub links in `Next Best Skill` sections with relative paths (e.g., `../../build/seo-content-writer/SKILL.md`) or use skill names only (the runtime resolves them)

description: Tombstone rule states "tombstone review: 2026-07-10" — a hard-coded date in a command definition. If the command is not updated by that date, the review instruction becomes permanently stale
suggested_fix: Replace the hard-coded date with a relative expression: "Tombstone review: 90 days after the v7.1.0 release date (see VERSIONS.md)"

description: Step 5 says to verify all 3 cross-agent manifests match but does not specify how to verify `gemini-extension.json` and `qwen-extension.json` — they aren't in the standard plugin path
suggested_fix: Add explicit file paths for the 3 cross-agent manifests in step 5 and a Grep verification line

description: LLM Crawler Handling section names specific bots (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot) but does not note that this list changes as new AI crawlers emerge
suggested_fix: Add: "For an up-to-date list of LLM crawlers and their user agent strings, refer to each provider's published documentation. This list reflects known crawlers as of v9.0.0."

description: FTC disclosure note for `aggregateRating` is present but applies only to US/FTC jurisdiction. EU users under the Digital Services Act (DSA) have additional requirements for review authenticity.
suggested_fix: Expand the disclosure note: "US: FTC requires disclosure of material connections. EU/DSA: Ensure reviews meet authenticity requirements of Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 Art. 6."

description: `Save Results` section is identical across all 20 skills — copied verbatim. The section asks "Save these results for future sessions?" but skills that are used in pipeline mode (handoff chain) may save redundantly.
suggested_fix: Add a condition: "If running in handoff mode (previous skill result already saved this session), skip the save prompt and reference the prior memory entry instead."

description: UserPromptSubmit hook (line 36) fires on every prompt submission and instructs Claude to "check if hot-cache.md should be updated." For long sessions this creates a high-frequency background instruction that may inflate context
suggested_fix: Consider moving the hot-cache update check to the PostToolUse hook (matcher: "Write

evidence: File begins with `# Scaffold New Project` (h1); no YAML frontmatter block found; pattern applies to all 26 command files.
description: All 26 command files lack YAML frontmatter; name and description fields required for NLPM registration are absent; each file incurs -55 base penalty (name -25, description -25, allowed-tools -5).
suggested_fix:
Add frontmatter to every command file: `---
name: <slug>
description: <one-line purpose>
allowed-tools: [<tools used>]
---`

description: Plugin name is 'atm' but example invocations in command bodies use '/atm-scaffold' (hyphen); Claude Code registered paths would use 'atm:scaffold' (colon) or the bare name; inconsistent documentation confuses users.
suggested_fix: Standardise all example invocations to the actual registered form (e.g. '/atm:scaffold' for scoped installs) and add a Usage section to README.md.

description: Python script written to /tmp/claude-filter.py and executed via subprocess in a git filter-repo callback; file is constructed by Claude and never persists between runs.
fp_reason: Claude Code execution context already has user-level filesystem access; writing to /tmp and executing a self-constructed script is not a privilege escalation vector in this threat model.
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish self-constructed ephemeral scripts from scripts whose content derives from external or user-controlled input.

description: APP_PATH set from $ARGUMENTS and passed to grep without sanitization; same pattern in angular-architecture-audit, angular-performance-audit, angular-style-audit, django-api-audit, django-model-audit (6 files total).
fp_reason: grep runs inside the user's own checked-out repository with no cross-user boundary; worst case is the user auditing a directory they chose themselves.
rule_gap: Rule should account for the absence of a privilege boundary when the tool runs as the user who invoked it.

evidence: 'args': ['-y', '@upstash/context7-mcp'] — no version specifier; npx -y will install the latest published version on every MCP server start.
description: MCP server declaration uses 'npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp' without a version pin; any malicious or breaking publish of the package silently affects all plugin users.
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific release: 'args': ['-y', '@upstash/context7-mcp@X.Y.Z'] and update when a new tested version is released.

evidence: File starts at line 1 with '# Scaffold New Project'; no YAML frontmatter delimiters found. Representative file; all 26 command files share this defect.
description: Missing name (-25) and description (-25) in frontmatter across all 26 command files; allowed-tools also absent (-5); combined base penalty -55 per file applies to every command.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter block at line 1 of each command file with name, description, and allowed-tools fields.

evidence: File body sets a persona ('You are now operating as a senior accessibility engineer...') then lists topics; no numbered steps or ordered workflow present. Same pattern in angular-expert.md and django-expert.md.
description: Expert persona commands (a11y-expert, angular-expert, django-expert) define no numbered process steps; the model has no deterministic execution order for multi-step tasks.
suggested_fix: Add a numbered step sequence even for persona-mode commands, e.g. '1. Confirm scope 2. Audit area X 3. Report findings'.

evidence: SPIKE_TOPIC=$ARGUMENTS used at the top of the bash block with no preceding empty check; if invoked bare the variable is empty and SPIKE_ID derivation silently produces a malformed slug.
description: $ARGUMENTS consumed without empty check; worst-case file shown (spike-investigation scores 15); same defect in pr-review, feature-investigation, implement-pr-feedback, problem-solver, release-architect (6 files total).
suggested_fix: Add at the top of each affected command: if [[ -z "$ARGUMENTS" ]]; then echo "Usage: /atm:<command> <required-arg>"; exit 1; fi

evidence: FEATURE_ID=$ARGUMENTS assigned with no guard; downstream AC-gate step silently operates on empty string if invoked without arguments.
description: $ARGUMENTS used without empty-input guard; -10 penalty combines with 8 vague quantifiers (-16) to produce score 19.
suggested_fix: Add empty guard before FEATURE_ID assignment; display usage hint referencing JIRA ticket ID format.

evidence: 'pbcopy' appears in the step instruction for copying the commit message; pbcopy is a macOS-only command not available on Linux or Windows.
description: pbcopy used without OS detection fallback; command fails silently on non-macOS platforms; the bash skill documents a cross-platform clipboard abstraction that is not applied here.
suggested_fix: Use $OSTYPE guard: pbcopy on darwin*, xclip -selection clipboard or xsel --clipboard --input on linux*; or reference the bash skill clipboard helper.

evidence: 'Always' appears 3 times as an unconditional directive; 'graceful', 'informative', 'standard' appear without measurable thresholds.
description: 6 vague quantifiers in bash skill (Always×3, graceful, informative, standard) without observable success criteria; reduces scoring precision for commands that load this skill.
suggested_fix: Replace 'Always check tool availability' with 'Before using <tool>, test with command -v <tool> >/dev/null 2>&1 and exit 1 with an install hint if not found'.

evidence: effective, specific, appropriate, focused, relevant, clear, good, proper each appear at least once without measurable criteria.
description: 8 vague quantifiers in the ai skill; highest vague penalty of the two skill files; reduces instruction precision for commands that reference this skill for prompt-writing guidance.
suggested_fix: Replace subjective adjectives with observable criteria, e.g. 'specific examples' → 'at least two concrete input/output examples per behaviour described'.

description: eval with $(sed ...) command substitution extracts and executes Bash function code from hooks/simplify-ignore.sh at runtime; a compromised source file would execute silently during test runs
suggested_fix: Refactor test to source the script directly or extract filter_file into a shared library file that can be safely sourced without eval

description: curl -sI makes outbound HEAD request to user-agent-supplied URL without domain allowlist; could be used for SSRF or probing internal network services
suggested_fix: Validate URL scheme is https and restrict to non-private IP ranges before invoking curl; add --max-redirs 0 to suppress redirect following

description: curl -sI -L follows HTTP redirects to user-agent-supplied URL without redirect-target validation; redirect chain could reach internal services
suggested_fix: Add URL validation before curl call; use --max-redirs 3 or 0; log the final resolved URL for auditability

description: $CONTENT (full text of SKILL.md) is interpolated into a bare heredoc JSON string without escaping; double-quotes or backslashes in the skill file produce malformed JSON output delivered to Claude Code
suggested_fix: Use jq -n --arg msg "$CONTENT" '{"priority":"IMPORTANT","message":$msg}' for properly escaped JSON construction

description: Arbitrary web content received from WebFetch is written to .claude/sdd-cache/*.json without content-type or size validation; a large or crafted response could fill disk or embed instruction-like text in cached files
suggested_fix: Add max-content-size guard (e.g., skip caching if ${#CONTENT} exceeds 512 KB) and log a warning when skipping

description: Agent frontmatter has no model field; the executor cannot select an appropriate model tier without it
suggested_fix: Add model: claude-sonnet-4-5 (or appropriate tier) to the YAML frontmatter

description: Only one example present (the output template); agents benefit from at least two examples showing different invocation contexts
suggested_fix: Add a second example showing a concrete invocation scenario, e.g. reviewing a specific auth module

description: Agent frontmatter has no model field; executor cannot select model tier without it

description: Agent frontmatter has no model field

description: Usage comment documents `curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash` as the standard installation method with no integrity check — users who follow the docs execute arbitrary code from GitHub
suggested_fix: Replace with a checksummed installation: fetch a pinned release tarball and verify its SHA-256 before executing, rather than piping curl to bash

description: Usage comment documents `curl -fsSL .../quick-install.sh | bash` as the standard installation method with no integrity check — same supply-chain risk as install.sh

description: Downloads remote `airis-gateway` shell script from GitHub raw URL and immediately executes it (chmod +x at line 119, executed at line 129) without any hash verification — functionally equivalent to curl | bash

description: Downloads `airis_bootstrap.py` from GitHub raw URL without integrity check; the file is executed at line 358 via `python3 "$DIR/scripts/airis_bootstrap.py"` — remote code execution if the GitHub repo or CDN is compromised

description: subprocess.run(['start', str(path)], shell=True) on Windows branch of _open_in_browser(); pre-scan flagged as HIGH
fp_reason: 'start' is a Windows cmd.exe built-in that requires shell=True to invoke; argument is FLEET_DASHBOARD_PATH constructed from a hardcoded internal path with no user input
rule_gap: Rule should exempt platform-specific browser-open idioms (open/xdg-open/start) where the argument is a hardcoded or internally computed file path

description: subprocess.run(['powershell', '-Command', ps_cmd]) where ps_cmd is a dynamically formatted string; pre-scan flagged as HIGH
fp_reason: All dynamic values in ps_cmd are cast through int(pid) before interpolation, which prevents shell metacharacter injection; this is a standard Windows process-introspection pattern
rule_gap: Rule should account for int()-cast sanitization on numeric-only parameters passed into subprocess list-form commands

description: Generated fleet dashboard HTML hard-embeds Google Fonts CDN links (fonts.googleapis.com, fonts.gstatic.com); browser makes third-party network requests whenever user views the dashboard, leaking activity metadata to Google

description: Hooks registered across 10+ distinct event types (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, PostCompact, SessionStart, Stop, SessionEnd, StopFailure, UserPromptSubmit, CwdChanged); any defect in hook scripts will execute on a very high proportion of user interactions

description: Body references tool name `get_node_essentials` which is not in the declared tools frontmatter
suggested_fix: Replace with the correct declared tool name from the mcp__n8n-mcp-testing__ namespace, or declare the tool in the frontmatter if it exists on the server

description: Body references tool name `validate_node_config` (also at lines 67-68) which is not in the declared tools; declared tools have `mcp__n8n-mcp-testing__validate_node` and `mcp__n8n-mcp-testing__validate_workflow`

description: Body references tool name `get_node_example` which is not in the declared tools frontmatter
suggested_fix: Remove from the capabilities list or add the correct declared tool name from the mcp__n8n-mcp-testing__ namespace

description: eval "$cmd" executes a dynamically built curl command string; $cmd incorporates $1 (user-supplied server URL argument) and $4 (headers) without sanitization, enabling arbitrary shell command injection via script argument
suggested_fix: Replace eval with an array-based approach: build cmd as a bash array (`declare -a cmd=(curl -s ...)`) and invoke with `"${cmd[@]}"`; eliminates shell injection risk

description: Line contains `eval(item.json.code)` inside a JavaScript string literal that represents n8n Code node test payload data — not an actual eval() call executed by the script
fp_reason: The eval appears inside a string constant (`jsCode: 'const result = eval(item.json.code);...'`) representing workflow test data sent to n8n, not JavaScript code executed by this TypeScript script
rule_gap: Pattern matcher should distinguish eval() calls in actual code from eval() appearing inside string literals used as test data payloads

description: sudo apt-get, sudo gpg --dearmor, sudo tee, sudo usermod used in Docker auto-installer section; running this script on an unprovisioned host triggers elevated-privilege package installation
suggested_fix: Document that Docker must be pre-installed; remove the auto-install section, or gate it behind an explicit --install-docker flag with a confirmation prompt

description: source .env loads and executes an arbitrary file from the working directory; a maliciously crafted .env could inject shell commands run with deployer privileges
suggested_fix: Validate .env contains only KEY=value lines before sourcing: `grep -qvP '^[A-Z_a-z0-9]+=.*$' .env && echo 'Invalid .env' && exit 1`; alternatively use a dedicated env-parsing tool

description: "prepare": "husky" runs automatically on npm install for both the package author and anyone who installs the package as a dependency; if the package is compromised, prepare is a code execution vector
suggested_fix: Guard with INIT_CWD check: `node -e "if (process.env.INIT_CWD !== process.cwd()) process.exit(0)" && husky` so husky only runs for the repo maintainer, not downstream installers

description: Multiple devDependencies use ^ ranges (nodemon ^3.1.14, ts-node ^10.9.2, typescript ^5.8.3, vitest ^3.2.4) allowing unintended version upgrades on fresh installs that could introduce breaking changes or supply-chain issues
suggested_fix: Pin build-critical devDependencies to exact versions; run `npm shrinkwrap` or commit package-lock.json and enforce it in CI with `npm ci`

description: No model field in frontmatter; MCP analysis and TypeScript code generation requires sonnet-level reasoning — undeclared model inherits from parent which may be haiku
suggested_fix: Add `model: sonnet` to frontmatter

description: Agent body has no output format section; response structure (what fields to include, in what order) is undefined and will vary per invocation

description: CLAUDE.md line 210 embeds a marketing attribution instruction ('Add to every commit and PR: Concieved by Romuald Członkowski - link to www.aiadvisors.pl/en') in a file that R38 reserves for technical guidance; this mixes behavioral marketing into project-architecture documentation

description: MCP server args contain a hardcoded absolute Windows path c:\Users\LeoDg\.claude\... which breaks the MCP server for every user that is not the developer; all non-developer installs silently fail to start the MCP server

description: Step 2 of the installer runs `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh` without checksum verification; a compromised CDN or MitM response executes arbitrary code with user privileges at install time

description: cleanup_orphan_processes() calls subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True) on a hardcoded cmd string; while not currently user-influenced, shell=True widens the injection surface and is unnecessary when cmd can be expressed as a list
suggested_fix: Replace shell=True with shell=False and express each command as a list, e.g. ['taskkill', '/F', '/IM', 'python.exe', '/FI', 'MEMUSAGE gt 100000']

description: Agent contains explicit instructions for bypassing MetaTrader EA copy protections: account number validation bypass, HWID fingerprinting removal, trial/expiry check removal, broker restriction bypass, and demo-mode removal; references EX4-TO-MQL, IDA Pro, Ghidra, x64dbg, OllyDbg for protection circumvention; distributing these instructions in a public plugin enables DMCA-violating usage at scale

description: CLAUDE.md instructs Claude to run taskkill /F on python.exe, node.exe, bash.exe, git.exe, grep.exe, cmd.exe, and powershell.exe at the end of every task; this terminates all matching processes system-wide regardless of whether they belong to the plugin or to unrelated user work; the NUL KILLER V2.0 block uses ctypes.windll.kernel32.DeleteFileW on user home directories

pattern: unvalidated URL from argv passed to fetch
description: Script accepts any URL via process.argv[2] and makes an HTTP fetch request without validating the domain or scheme; an attacker supplying a crafted URL (e.g. http://169.254.169.254/) could trigger SSRF if the script is invoked outside the documented skill context.
suggested_fix: Add an allowlist of permitted URL prefixes (https://api.expo.dev/ and https://raw.githubusercontent.com/expo/) and throw before fetching if the URL does not match.

description: Instruction 'its first child should almost always be a ScrollView' uses the vague quantifier 'almost always' without stating the exceptions; actionable guidance should be unconditional or enumerate the exceptions explicitly.

description: Instruction 'it should almost always be the first component inside the route component' uses the vague quantifier 'almost always'.

description: Instruction 'Whenever possible, include a <Link.Preview>' is vague — no criterion for when it is impossible or undesirable.

description: Instruction 'Use link previews frequently to enhance navigation' is vague — 'frequently' gives no actionable criterion.

description: Rule 'Keep API routes focused — one responsibility per endpoint' — 'focused' is vague; the clarification 'one responsibility' partially rescues it but 'focused' adds no information.

description: Rule 'Handle errors gracefully with try/catch' — 'gracefully' is a vague adverb with no actionable meaning in this context.
suggested_fix: Remove 'gracefully': 'Wrap handlers in try/catch; return structured error JSON with an appropriate HTTP status.'

description: Decision tree branch label 'Complex app -> React Query' uses 'complex' without defining the threshold; a developer cannot self-assess whether their app qualifies.
suggested_fix: Define the threshold, e.g.: 'Multiple data sources, background sync, or optimistic updates -> React Query'

suggested_fix: Update Repository Structure and install examples to reflect the actual plugins/expo/ layout and the 'expo' plugin name.

description: agents/firetiger.md has no YAML frontmatter block at all; the `name` field is absent, preventing proper agent registration in the plugin.

description: agents/firetiger.md has no YAML frontmatter block; the `description` field is absent, breaking agent discovery and help listings.
suggested_fix: Add `description: Firetiger observability platform context and available MCP tools reference` to the frontmatter block.

suggested_fix: Add `model: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` or `model: claude-sonnet-4-6` to the frontmatter depending on desired capability tier.

description: Agent context document has no output format section; there is no specification of how the agent should structure its responses.
suggested_fix: Add an `## Output Format` section that describes the expected response structure (e.g., summary, query results, recommended actions).

description: Command frontmatter has no `allowed-tools` declaration; relies on MCP tools only but omitting the field leaves tool access unrestricted.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: []` if only MCP tools are intended, or list explicit built-in tools if Bash or Write are needed.

description: Phrase `until the agent is fully configured` uses vague completion criterion; `fully` is not defined.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete criterion such as `until the planner returns a conversation with no pending questions and the agent status is active`.

description: Command writes source files (tracing.ts, tracing.py, etc.) and updates .gitignore but declares no `allowed-tools`; Write and Edit tools are used without being listed.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Write, Edit, Bash]` to the frontmatter.

description: Step 2 says `Quickly identify the primary framework` but provides no bound on what constitutes a quick scan.
suggested_fix: Replace `Quickly identify` with `Identify` and specify the check order (e.g., `check package.json first, then requirements.txt, then go.mod`).

description: Step 4 says `Add the minimal instrumentation needed` where `minimal` is undefined; Claude must infer what counts as minimal.
suggested_fix: Replace `minimal instrumentation` with the specific artifacts to create (e.g., `a single tracing bootstrap file and the required environment variables`).

description: Command uses only MCP tools but has no `allowed-tools` declaration, leaving tool access unrestricted.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: []` to signal that only MCP tools are needed.

description: Investigation create fields include `services: Relevant services to investigate` where `relevant` is undefined.
suggested_fix: Replace `Relevant services` with `Services identified in Step 1 that produced errors or latency in the time window`.

description: Investigation create fields include `description: Detailed context about the issue` where `detailed` is undefined.
suggested_fix: Replace `Detailed context` with a concrete checklist (e.g., `include: symptom, first observed timestamp, affected services, recent deployments`).

description: Command uses Bash (gh CLI) explicitly but has no `allowed-tools` declaration.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Bash]` to the frontmatter.

description: Description says Firetiger `develops a targeted monitoring plan` where `targeted` has no defined criteria.
suggested_fix: Remove the adjective or specify: `a monitoring plan focused on the endpoints and services changed in the PR diff`.

description: Command uses only the MCP `query` tool but has no `allowed-tools` declaration.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: []` to signal MCP-only tool access.

description: Command is a SQL reference guide with no section specifying how query results should be formatted and presented to the user.
suggested_fix: Add an `## Output Format` section: e.g., `Present results as a markdown table with column headers. Summarize row count and any notable patterns before the table.`

description: Command uses Bash extensively (curl, aws, gcloud, wrangler, open/xdg-open) and writes files but has no `allowed-tools` declaration.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Bash, Write, Edit]` to the frontmatter.

description: Goal statement says `minimal user interaction` with no threshold defined for what counts as minimal.
suggested_fix: Replace with `at most one confirmation prompt per major action (instrumentation, connection, agent creation)`.

description: Section heading `Proactively connect` does not define what proactive means or when the agent should act without asking.
suggested_fix: Replace heading with `Automatically initiate (no confirmation needed)` and keep the existing parenthetical `(detected in Step 3)`.

description: Warning condition `if only some connections were skipped` uses vague `some`; quantity is unspecified.
suggested_fix: Replace with `if any connection attempt was skipped or failed` for a precise trigger.

description: Note says `Only ask for confirmation if genuinely uncertain` where `genuinely` provides no actionable decision rule.
suggested_fix: Replace with `Only ask for confirmation when two or more equally valid options exist and the choice cannot be inferred from the codebase`.

description: MCP server is a remote HTTP endpoint (https://api.cloud.firetiger.com/mcp/v1). All tool calls including get_ingest_credentials and agent management traverse this external connection. No credentials are hardcoded; trust depends on TLS and the vendor's server security.
suggested_fix: Document in the plugin README that all telemetry credentials are retrieved over HTTPS from the vendor endpoint; recommend users verify TLS certificate pinning is active.

description: Command instructs Claude to run `gh pr comment <PR_NUMBER> --body "@firetiger <monitoring context>"` where monitoring context is assembled from user input and PR diff. Unescaped shell metacharacters in user-provided text could break out of the --body argument boundary.
suggested_fix: Instruct Claude to write the comment body to a temp file and use `gh pr comment $PR_NUMBER --body-file /tmp/ft-comment.txt` to avoid shell interpolation.

description: Command instructs Claude to run `open "URL"` or `xdg-open "URL"` with a URL from the get_checkout_url MCP tool. If the MCP server ever returns a non-https scheme (file://, javascript:, etc.), the OS handler could execute unintended content.
suggested_fix: Add a guard before running open/xdg-open: `if [[ "$URL" != https://* ]]; then echo "Unexpected URL scheme, refusing to open"; fi`

description: Agent context lists supported MCP resource collections (agents, known-issues, connections, runbooks, triggers) but omits `investigations`, which is the primary resource used by commands/investigate.md.
suggested_fix: Add `investigations` to the Resources > CRUD operations list in agents/firetiger.md so the agent context is aware of the collection.

description: commands/query.md, commands/investigate.md, and commands/create-agent.md have no auth-fallback guard. If the Firetiger MCP server is not authenticated, these commands will fail silently. Only commands/setup.md handles the unauthenticated case.
suggested_fix: Add a brief Step 0 to each command: `If MCP tools are unavailable, direct the user to run /firetiger:setup first.`

title: Missing required `name` field in frontmatter
body: The frontmatter lacks the `name` field which is required for agent discovery and invocation. The file has `description` and `disable-model-invocation: true` but no `name`. Without a name the agent cannot be listed or invoked by Copilot's agent picker.
fix: Add `name: "Agentic Workflows"` (or appropriate name) to the frontmatter YAML block.

title: Non-standard pipe-separated model format
body: The `model` field contains `GPT-4.1 | 'gpt-5' | 'Claude Sonnet 4.5'` which is not a valid YAML model identifier. This appears to be a human-readable 'choose one' annotation. Copilot agent parsers will read this as the literal string, which will fail model lookup or silently fall back to the default model.
fix: Choose one model and use the standard identifier, e.g., `model: gpt-4.1`.

title: Non-standard pipe-separated model format
body: The `model` field contains `GPT-4.1 | 'gpt-5' | 'Claude Sonnet 4.5'` which is not a valid YAML model identifier. Same issue as laravel-expert-agent.agent.md.
fix: Choose one model and use the standard identifier, e.g., `model: gpt-4.1`.

title: Non-standard slash-separated tool path identifiers
body: The tools list includes `search/codebase` and `runCommands/terminalLastCommand` which use slash-separated sub-tool paths. The canonical tool identifiers are `codebase` and `terminalLastCommand`. These may not parse correctly depending on Copilot agent runtime version.
fix: Use flat tool identifiers: replace `search/codebase` with `codebase`, `runCommands/terminalLastCommand` with `terminalLastCommand`.

title: --no-verify bypasses secrets scanner in auto-commit hook
body: Line 33 of auto-commit.sh runs `git commit --no-verify` which explicitly bypasses all pre-commit hooks, including the secrets-scanner hook in hooks/secrets-scanner/scan-secrets.sh. Any session using session-auto-commit will skip secret detection before committing. A developer could accidentally commit credentials that the secrets scanner would otherwise block.
fix: Remove `--no-verify`. If commit hooks are slow, run the secrets scanner explicitly before `git commit` rather than bypassing all hooks.

title: git add -A stages all files including potentially sensitive untracked files
body: Line 29 uses `git add -A` which stages all files including untracked ones. This could inadvertently stage .env files, credential files, or large generated artifacts. The risk is compounded by --no-verify bypassing the secrets scanner (see SEC-hook-bypass finding).
fix: Replace `git add -A` with `git add -u` to only stage already-tracked modified files, or add an explicit filter step before staging.

title: Governance audit crashes on threat detection due to `local` outside function
body: Line 87 uses the `local` keyword inside a for loop at global scope (not inside a function). In bash with `set -euo pipefail`, this causes the script to exit with an error when threats are detected -- the exact condition the governance audit is designed to handle. The hook fails silently precisely when a threat is present, making the governance audit ineffective for its primary use case.
fix: Wrap the threat-processing block in a bash function, or replace `local evidence` with a plain variable assignment `evidence=`.

title: GitHub Actions template syntax in MCP server env config
body: The mcp-servers env block uses `AUTH_HEADER: "ApiKey ${{ secrets.ELASTIC_API_KEY }}"`. GitHub Actions `${{ secrets.* }}` syntax is not resolved in GitHub Copilot agent contexts. If deployed, the MCP server receives the literal string as the auth header, causing authentication failure. The intent is valid but the mechanism is wrong for this platform.
fix: Document that users must substitute their actual API key, or use a shell environment variable reference `$ELASTIC_API_KEY` if the runtime supports it.

title: Excessive vague language (14 hits, penalty capped at -20)
body: Agent body contains 14 vague quantifier hits: 'comprehensive research', 'appropriate tools', 'relevant context', 'suitable approach', 'thorough investigation', 'various sources', and similar. These reduce actionability and increase agent ambiguity during execution. Score impact: -20 pts (capped) plus -15 for no examples = 55/100.
fix: Replace vague qualifiers with concrete constraints: specify exact quantities (e.g., 'search with up to 5 queries'), enumerate specific tools by name, define what 'relevant' means in operational terms.

title: Excessive vague language including repeated use of 'obsessively'
body: Agent body contains 14 vague-word hits including 'obsessively' (used 3 consecutive times), 'exhaustively', 'recursively', 'comprehensive todo list', 'all available research avenues'. The word 'obsessively' conveys intensity but provides no actionable constraint. Score impact: -20 pts (capped) plus -5 for no model = 63/100.
fix: Replace intensity words with operational constraints: instead of 'use tools obsessively' specify 'use at least 3 different tool types per research question'; instead of 'comprehensive todo list' specify 'create a todo with one item per research question'.

title: Unused tools declared: test-running tools inappropriate for Kusto query agent
body: The agent declares findTestFiles, runNotebooks, runTests, and testFailure as tools. These are test-running tools, not relevant to a Kusto/Azure Data Explorer query assistant. This is likely from copy-paste template reuse. Score impact: -12 pts for inappropriate tools plus -5 for no model = 62/100.
fix: Remove findTestFiles, runNotebooks, runTests, and testFailure from the tools list. Retain search, codebase, and azure-relevant tools like azure_get_schema_for_Bicep if applicable.

title: Review-only agent declares write tool (edit/editFiles)
body: A 'reviewer' agent declares edit/editFiles as a tool. Architecture reviewers should produce written recommendations, not edit production files. This creates a capability-intent mismatch and risk of unintended file modifications during review sessions. Score impact: -10 pts for inappropriate tools.
fix: Remove edit/editFiles from the tools list. If the agent is intended to also generate scaffolding or templates, clarify this in the description and rename to reflect the dual role.

title: Missing model declaration
body: Agent has no `model` field in frontmatter. This is the most common deficiency across the repo (73 of 100 agents). Without a model declaration, the Copilot runtime uses a default which may not match the agent's complexity requirements. Score impact: -5 pts.
fix: Add `model: GPT-4.1` or the appropriate model identifier to the frontmatter.

title: Missing concrete examples
body: Agent describes capabilities (performance diagnosis, index recommendations, aggregation pipeline optimization) but provides no concrete input/output examples or worked scenarios. Without examples, LLM agents in chat context have weaker priors for response format and depth. Score impact: -15 pts plus -5 for no model = 75/100.
fix: Add an '## Examples' or '## Sample Interactions' section with at least one realistic user query and expected agent response, including a concrete MongoDB schema or query being analyzed.

title: Missing concrete examples
body: Agent describes Azure WAF pillar assessment approach but provides no example architectural questions, trade-off discussions, or reference architecture outputs. Score impact: -15 pts plus -5 for no model = 75/100.
fix: Add an '## Example Scenarios' section showing at least one question ('Design a multi-region web app for 99.99% SLA') with a structured WAF-pillar response outline.

title: Missing concrete examples
body: Agent describes DevOps expertise areas (CI/CD, IaC, containerization, observability) but provides no example workflows, pipeline templates, or worked debugging scenarios. Score impact: -15 pts plus -5 for no model = 75/100.
fix: Add an '## Example Interactions' section with at least one specific DevOps scenario: e.g., 'Diagnose a failing GitHub Actions pipeline' with expected diagnostic output.

title: Agent duplicated between agents/ and plugins/go-mcp-development/agents/
body: agents/go-mcp-expert.agent.md and plugins/go-mcp-development/agents/go-mcp-expert.md have identical content. This creates maintenance risk: fixes in one location won't propagate to the other. Consumers of the plugin version and the standalone version may diverge over time.
fix: Remove the standalone agents/ copy and keep only the plugin-owned version, or add a comment indicating the canonical source and a CI check to detect drift.

title: Agent duplicated between agents/ and plugins/azure-cloud-development/agents/
body: agents/terraform-azure-implement.agent.md and plugins/azure-cloud-development/agents/terraform-azure-implement.md have identical content. Same maintenance risk as go-mcp-expert duplication.
fix: Remove the standalone agents/ copy and keep only the plugin-owned version.

title: Agent duplicated between agents/ and plugins/technical-spike/agents/
body: agents/research-technical-spike.agent.md and plugins/technical-spike/agents/research-technical-spike.md have identical content. Same maintenance risk as other duplicated agents.
fix: Remove the standalone agents/ copy and keep only the plugin-owned version.

title: No MCP server catalog or discovery document
body: Multiple agents declare mcp-servers blocks (elasticsearch-observability, scientific-paper-research, neo4j-docker-client-generator, monday-bug-fixer, arm-migration) but there is no central document cataloging available MCP servers, their install procedures, authentication requirements, or tool lists. Users must read each agent file individually to understand what external services are needed.
fix: Add a docs/mcp-servers.md file cataloging all referenced MCP servers with: server name, type (SSE/local/docker), install command, auth requirements, tools exposed, and which agents use it.

description: Hook fires on every file read and grep call, adding 3000ms timeout overhead to all file-scanning operations. Slows code review, planning, and research phases significantly.
suggested_fix: Narrow matcher to 'Bash|Edit|Write|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit' — exclude read-only tools that do not modify workflow state.

pattern: Inline 700+ char minified Node.js in hook command fields
description: All four hook handlers contain identical compact Node.js one-liners that are difficult to audit at a glance. The code performs semver-sorted plugin path resolution before require(). No malicious patterns found after expansion, but reduced readability impedes future security review.
suggested_fix: Extract hook logic to $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/bin/hook-runner.cjs and reference it: 'node $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/bin/hook-runner.cjs session-start'

pattern: Dynamic require() of path resolved from plugin cache directory
description: Hook resolves gsd-tools.cjs by scanning ~/.claude/plugins/cache/gsd-plugin/gsd/ for the highest semver version, then calls require(). Path is bounded to the plugin cache, not user input. Low actual risk; any compromise of the plugin cache directory would gain hook execution on every Claude tool use.
suggested_fix: Ensure plugin cache directory has restrictive permissions (700). Consider pinning to CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT only, removing the cache fallback path when CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is set.

pattern: no example blocks in agent body
description: Agent returns a 5-column comparison table (Option/Pros/Cons/Complexity/Recommendation) but provides no sample table row or sample output. Executors and evaluators cannot verify correct format without a reference example.
suggested_fix: Add a <structured_returns> section with a sample comparison table row showing realistic option names and tradeoffs.

pattern: no example blocks in agent body
description: Three calibration tiers described (full_maturity, standard, minimal_decisive) but no sample output for any tier. Confidence level assignments and assumption structure are undocumented by example.
suggested_fix: Add a sample assumptions block showing at least one assumption with confidence level and evidence for each tier.

pattern: no example blocks in agent body
description: Scores coverage/infrastructure dimensions but provides no sample EVAL-REVIEW.md section or scored dimension output. Downstream consumers (eval-review skill) cannot validate format.
suggested_fix: Add a sample scored dimension block (e.g., 'Dimension: Unit Tests — COVERED — score: 85') to the structured_returns section.

description: 'focused' used to describe research depth without a measurable definition (e.g., 'focused on the core problem'). Cannot be verified by an executor.
suggested_fix: Replace with specific constraint: 'limit research to the top 3 implementation approaches with published adoption data'.

description: 'genuine recommendations' cannot be operationalized. Instructing an LLM to be 'genuine' provides no actionable guidance.
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'for each option, state one concrete tradeoff the developer will encounter within the first sprint'.

description: 'appropriate error handling' appears in verification dimension without defining what constitutes appropriate (e.g., which error categories, which status codes).
suggested_fix: Replace with measurable criterion: 'error handling covers network failures, null inputs, and 4xx/5xx status codes'.

description: 'reasonable default' used in Nyquist compliance dimension without defining what constitutes reasonable for a given test framework.
suggested_fix: Replace with a specific threshold or reference: 'at least one test per public function or endpoint, per PLAN.md must_haves'.

description: 'relevant context' appears in task generation section without specifying which context fields are relevant (file paths, decisions, prior phase outputs).
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit list: 'include phase goal, locked decisions from CONTEXT.md, and file paths from RESEARCH.md Standard Stack section'.

description: 'intelligently apply fixes' in rollback section cannot guide executor behavior. What makes a fix application intelligent vs. unintelligent is undefined.
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'apply the smallest diff that resolves the finding; do not refactor surrounding code unless the finding requires it'.

pattern: zero examples and no process section
description: Skill contains objective + execution_context + context but no <process> tag. Output is described as 'prioritized human test plan' but no file path or format is shown. Score: 75/100.
suggested_fix: Add: (1) <process> section with at least 3 numbered steps, (2) <output_format> naming the artifact (e.g., 'inline UAT checklist, no file created'), (3) one usage example.

description: Skill delegates to cleanup.md workflow with no process steps, no usage examples, and no output description beyond 'archive phase directories'. Score: 75/100.
suggested_fix: Add: (1) brief process showing 'dry-run → confirm → archive', (2) example showing before/after directory state, (3) output path: '.planning/milestones/v{X.Y}-phases/'.

description: Smart dispatcher skill has no examples of what natural language inputs map to which GSD commands. Output is 'dispatch to the best GSD command' — no concrete routing examples shown. Score: 75/100.
suggested_fix: Add a routing table: 3-5 example inputs with the command they route to (e.g., 'fix the login bug → /gsd:debug', 'add password reset → /gsd:add-phase').

description: Skill has no process steps, no examples, and no description of EVAL-REVIEW.md format. Score: 75/100.
suggested_fix: Add: (1) output format showing EVAL-REVIEW.md structure with score/verdict fields, (2) one sample covered/missing dimension entry.

description: Skill body has only an objective, execution_context, and one-line delegation. No examples of LEARNINGS.md structure. Score: 75/100.
suggested_fix: Add an output format showing the LEARNINGS.md sections (decisions, lessons, patterns, surprises) with a brief sample entry.

description: Skill describes when to use /gsd:fast vs /gsd:quick but provides no examples of the tasks it handles (typo fixes, config changes). Score: 75/100.
suggested_fix: Add: (1) 3 concrete task examples ('fix typo in README', 'update .env.example'), (2) note that output is inline completion with no artifact created.

description: Diagnoses .planning/ directory integrity but shows no sample health report or list of checks performed. Score: 75/100.
suggested_fix: Add: (1) list of 5+ checks the workflow performs (missing ROADMAP.md, invalid config.json, orphaned plans), (2) sample output showing PASS/FAIL per check.

description: Import skill mentions '--from' flag but shows no example of what a valid external plan file looks like or what GSD PLAN.md output looks like. Score: 75/100.
suggested_fix: Add: (1) example invocation '/gsd:import --from /path/to/external-plan.md', (2) note that output is a validated PLAN.md in .planning/phases/{N}-{slug}/.

description: Insert-phase shows no example of before/after roadmap numbering (e.g., '72 → 72.1 → 73') or argument format. Score: 75/100.
suggested_fix: Add: (1) example invocation '/gsd:insert-phase 72 add rate limiting', (2) show that result is phase 72.1 between phases 72 and 73 in ROADMAP.md.

description: prepare lifecycle script automatically runs node scripts/install-git-hooks.js on npm install, silently reconfiguring git core.hooksPath without user consent

description: Script writes plugin files to ~/.codex/plugins/maestro/ and modifies ~/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json outside repository directory

description: execSync executes git subprocesses automatically via prepare lifecycle hook, running on every npm install in any consuming repository

description: Modifies git core.hooksPath to .githooks/ directory; if .githooks/ contents are malicious, all future git operations execute attacker code

description: Hook command binary paths resolved from ${extensionPath} env var; attacker-controlled env var redirects hook execution to arbitrary binary

description: MCP server binary path and working directory resolved from ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} env var; poisoned env var redirects MCP server execution

description: devDependency c8 pinned with caret range (^10.1.3) allowing automatic minor and patch version upgrades on fresh installs

description: MAESTRO_RUNTIME env var selects platform adapter at runtime without validation; untrusted input could load unexpected or unintended adapter

description: Frontmatter lacks model field; all 75 agents in src/agents/, plugins/maestro/src/agents/, and claude/src/agents/ share this defect universally

description: Spoofs navigator.platform, navigator.userAgent, navigator.userAgentData, navigator.webdriver and patches server-baked OS-detection JS functions to present Windows identity. Purpose: enable Linux users to access Japan NTA e-Tax filing site that discriminates against non-Windows OSes. All manipulations are in-browser client-side; no network exfiltration.

description: Runtime dependencies use '>=' floor constraints (pydantic>=2.0, pdfplumber>=0.10, pyyaml>=6.0.3) rather than exact version pins. Future incompatible major versions could silently break tax calculations.

description: skills/tax-advisor/SKILL.md references files under 'reference/' (singular) while all other skills use 'references/' (plural). Both directories exist and all paths resolve correctly, so this is a naming inconsistency rather than a broken reference.

description: Plugin manifest has no entries/commands/skills array; automation cannot enumerate exposed surfaces without a filesystem scan
suggested_fix: Add an entries array listing the skills paths relative to the plugin root

description: Skill name field is 'clickup' while package name, plugin manifest name, and directory are all 'clickup-cli'
suggested_fix: Add a comment in frontmatter clarifying the intentional short-name alias, or align name to 'clickup-cli'

description: Step 3 of 'New unit test' says 'edge cases' without defining what constitutes an edge case for this codebase
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete examples such as 'Test with missing optional flags, invalid task IDs, and API error responses'

description: Production dependency @inquirer/prompts uses '^8.3.0'; minor-version bumps are auto-applied on fresh installs
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version '8.3.0' or use npm shrinkwrap for distribution

description: Production dependency chalk uses '^5.6.2'; minor-version bumps are auto-applied on fresh installs
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version '5.6.2'

description: Production dependency commander uses '^14.0.3'; minor-version bumps are auto-applied on fresh installs
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version '14.0.3'

description: API examples use `type: Opaque` which contradicts the explicit warning at lines 56–70 that Opaque breaks Jenkins credential sync; correct type is credential.devops.kubesphere.io/basic-auth or the matching domain-specific type.
suggested_fix: Replace `"type": "Opaque"` with the correct credential.devops.kubesphere.io type on lines 111, 134, and 469.

description: YAML template syntax error: `low: "{{low}}` is missing closing double-quote; produces invalid YAML that will fail to parse when applied as an InstallPlan.
suggested_fix: Change to: low: "{{low}}"

description: Placeholder comment says '# From Step 3' but the installation section defines only Step 1 and Step 2; agents will look for a non-existent step.
suggested_fix: Change '# From Step 3' to '# From Step 2'.

description: Bold markdown marker is not closed: `**Step 3b: For Private Repository (with credential):` is missing its closing `**`; renders incorrectly and may confuse section boundary parsing.
suggested_fix: Add closing ** at end of the line: `**Step 3b: For Private Repository (with credential):**`

description: 'Project Components' ASCII architecture diagram block is duplicated; the same content block appears twice in the file starting at line 92.
suggested_fix: Remove the second occurrence of the duplicated Project Components section.

description: Uses `xargs -n 3 sh -c 'kubectl patch $2 -n $0 $1...'` with resource names piped from kubectl output; resource names are interpolated directly into a shell string, matching the HIGH shell injection pattern.
suggested_fix: Replace xargs sh -c with a while-read loop: `while read -r ns name crd; do kubectl patch "$crd" -n "$ns" "$name" ...; done` to avoid shell string interpolation.

description: Prints OpenSearch password to stdout: `echo "  Password: ${OS_PASSWORD}"` where OS_PASSWORD is read from the vector-sinks Kubernetes Secret; credential leaks to terminal scrollback and shell logs.
suggested_fix: Replace with `echo "  Password: [redacted — read from vector-sinks secret]"` or suppress the password output entirely.

description: OAuth token cached to ~/.kubesphere_token (TOKEN_FILE) without setting restrictive file permissions; token is readable by other users on shared systems.
suggested_fix: Add os.chmod(TOKEN_FILE, 0o600) immediately after writing the token file.

description: Identical to kubesphere-core/scripts/ks_api.py (file duplicated verbatim); OAuth token cached to ~/.kubesphere_token without restrictive file permissions; same insecure token storage pattern.
suggested_fix: Add os.chmod(TOKEN_FILE, 0o600) after writing the token; consolidate duplicate ks_api.py into a shared module to prevent fix-one-miss-one maintenance problems.

description: Password 'P@88w0rd' hardcoded in documentation API examples at lines 130, 803, and 867; realistic-looking credential may be copied into production configurations.
suggested_fix: Replace all occurrences of 'P@88w0rd' with '<YOUR_SECURE_PASSWORD>' placeholder.

description: Common Issues troubleshooting table duplicated at lines 640–649 and 677–684; identical rows appear twice, bloating the file and creating a maintenance hazard.
suggested_fix: Remove the second occurrence of the duplicate Common Issues table.

description: Common Mistakes partial table at lines 1277–1282 duplicates content that appears earlier in the file.
suggested_fix: Remove the duplicate partial table section at lines 1277–1282.

description: 'Key Resources' heading appears twice in the file, creating an ambiguous document structure and confusing section navigation.
suggested_fix: Remove the second Key Resources section or merge its content under a single heading.

description: The Authorization header `-H "Authorization: Bearer ${API_TOKEN}"` appears twice in the List Pipelines curl command (lines 247–249); the HTTP request contains a duplicate header.
suggested_fix: Remove the duplicate Authorization header line from the curl example.

description: Hardcoded example password 'P@88w0rd' at lines 130, 803, and 867 appears realistic; readers may use it in production without substitution.

description: "Link to issues/PRs if relevant" uses the vague qualifier `relevant` without defining what relevance means in this context.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete condition, e.g. "Link to the issue or PR that introduced each change."

description: "Include a concise changelog summary in the PR body" uses `concise` without specifying a target length or format.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete bound, e.g. "Include the changelog bullet list (Added/Changed/Fixed/Removed) verbatim in the PR body."

description: "cmux supports wait patterns similar to agent-browser" uses `similar` without specifying which patterns are equivalent and which differ.
suggested_fix: Replace with an explicit list of supported wait modes or remove the comparison to agent-browser.

description: "the panel attempts automatic reconnection within its short retry window" uses `short` without specifying the retry duration or count.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete timeout, e.g. "within a 2-second retry window", or remove the qualifier.

description: "Schedule minimal UI/model mutation with DispatchQueue.main.async only when needed" uses `minimal` without defining what qualifies as minimal mutation.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete rule, e.g. "Schedule only a single store-property assignment per dispatch block."

description: A `python3 -c` block interpolates shell variables `$SIGNATURE`, `$DMG_LENGTH`, and `$generated_appcast_path` directly into Python source code, matching the eval-with-variables pattern. Inputs are an EdDSA base64 signature, a stat-derived integer, and a controlled temp path — none can carry a Python injection payload.
fp_reason: All three interpolated values originate from trusted internal sources (Sparkle sign_update binary, stat command, mktemp) and cannot contain Python-injectable characters.
rule_gap: The eval-with-variables rule should account for cases where the interpolated values are constrained to safe character sets (base64, integers, filesystem paths from mktemp).
suggested_fix: Refactor to write the values to a temp JSON file and read them in Python, eliminating the shell interpolation pattern entirely.

description: SPARKLE_PRIVATE_KEY is passed as a positional CLI argument to `swift scripts/derive_sparkle_public_key.swift "$SPARKLE_PRIVATE_KEY"`, exposing the private key value in the system process table for the duration of the swift invocation.
suggested_fix: Write the key to a temporary file (mode 600), pass the file path as the argument, and shred the file on exit; or read the key from the environment variable inside the Swift script instead of accepting it as argv.

description: The Sparkle signing toolchain is cloned from GitHub (`git clone --depth 1 --branch $SPARKLE_VERSION`) and built from source at release time. Git tags are mutable; a rewritten or hijacked tag would silently substitute malicious build tooling into the release signing pipeline without any integrity check.
suggested_fix: Pin the clone to an explicit commit SHA (not just a tag) and verify the resulting `generate_appcast` and `sign_update` binaries against pre-computed checksums before executing them.

description: All npm/bun dependencies use `^` semver ranges, allowing minor and patch version drift on each install and enabling silent supply-chain substitution across the dependency tree.
suggested_fix: Pin all dependencies to exact versions and commit a lockfile enforced in CI.

description: Root-level `"vercel": "^50.9.5"` is an unpinned semver range.
suggested_fix: Pin to the exact version in use and lock via the package lockfile.

description: Output format is described inline across process steps rather than in a dedicated ## Output section; makes it hard for callers to parse expected return shape
