description: 'comprehensive prompt' uses the vague quantifier 'comprehensive' without defining what completeness criteria make a prompt comprehensive
suggested_fix: Replace with specific criteria: e.g., 'a prompt with role definition, input/output format, tool list, and 3+ examples'

description: 'Be thorough' is an unquantified instruction; thoroughness criteria are undefined
suggested_fix: Replace with specific guidance: e.g., 'Check all files matching *.js, *.ts, *.py for the listed vulnerability patterns'

description: 'Intuitive' used as a design principle without objective criteria for what makes a name intuitive
suggested_fix: Define a testable criterion: e.g., 'A new user should be able to guess the behavior from the name alone without reading docs'

description: 'Define behavior clearly' — 'clearly' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Document step-by-step what the element does, its preconditions, postconditions, and at least one example'

description: 'comprehensive documentation' — 'comprehensive' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'documentation including behavior, examples, and integration patterns'

description: 'Make prompts comprehensive and specific' — both 'comprehensive' and 'specific' are unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Include role, responsibilities (3+ items), output format, tool list, and at least one edge-case instruction'

description: 'Recommend appropriate tools' — 'appropriate' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'List the specific tools the agent will call (e.g., Read, Grep, Edit) and the condition that triggers each'

description: 'Manage errors gracefully' — 'gracefully' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'On agent failure, present retry/skip/abort options and preserve workflow state before halting'

description: 'Add review points for critical steps' — 'critical' is undefined
suggested_fix: Define critical: e.g., 'steps that write to production, delete data, or commit code'

description: 'Intuitive — Names/symbols hint at behavior' — no measurable criteria for intuitiveness
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'A user unfamiliar with the plugin should be able to infer the operator's effect from the symbol/name alone'

description: 'Only when truly needed' — 'truly needed' has no objective threshold
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Only create new syntax if the use case appears in at least two distinct workflows and existing syntax cannot express it'

description: 'complex multi-step tasks' — 'complex' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'tasks requiring external libraries, API authentication, or processing more than 20 items'

description: 'Set restrictive file permissions' — 'restrictive' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Set permissions to 700 (owner-only read/write/execute): chmod 700 /tmp/workflow-scripts/script.py'

description: 'complex multi-agent workflows' — 'complex' is unquantified in the skill trigger description
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'workflows requiring 3+ agents or conditional/parallel execution'

description: 'I'll proactively create temp scripts' — 'proactively' is vague without a defined detection threshold
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'I automatically create temp scripts when the user's request contains any of the trigger keywords listed below'

description: 'Show relevant syntax based on user's needs' — 'relevant' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Show the syntax example that matches the user's described operation type (sequential/parallel/conditional/retry)'

description: 'Show relevant example from docs/reference/examples.md' — 'relevant' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Show the example whose workflow type (TDD/deploy/review) most closely matches the user's described process'

description: Command requires a workflow identifier argument but has no handler for when {{ARGS}} is empty; the Phase 1 check would silently proceed with an empty string
suggested_fix: Add an explicit empty-args guard at the top: if ARGS is empty, display usage instructions and list available registry entries

description: Template command has file-not-found handling but no explicit empty-args handler; user invoking /orchestration:template with no argument gets file search on empty string
suggested_fix: Add: if ARGS is empty, list all available templates from all locations and ask user to select one

description: Absolute path /Users/mbroler/.claude/... embeds author's macOS username; TEMP-SCRIPTS-DETECTION-GUIDE.md does not exist in the repo; same file referenced portably in creating-workflows/SKILL.md also resolves to nothing
suggested_fix: Create docs/TEMP-SCRIPTS-DETECTION-GUIDE.md and reference it as ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/docs/TEMP-SCRIPTS-DETECTION-GUIDE.md

description: Five category-specific example files referenced under skills/executing-workflows/examples/ but that directory does not exist in the repository
suggested_fix: Create skills/executing-workflows/examples/ with sequential.md, parallel.md, conditional.md, error-handling.md, and checkpoints.md stub files

description: Quick-reference panel lists /orchestration:run, /orchestration:template, /orchestration:examples, /orchestration:explain without indicating they are deprecated; users following help will invoke deprecated paths
suggested_fix: Annotate deprecated entries with (deprecated) or replace with the recommended skill-based alternatives in the help output

description: name: orchestration:executing-workflows uses the plugin namespace prefix; all other skills use bare names (e.g. name: managing-agents) — inconsistent naming convention across the skill set
suggested_fix: Standardize skill names: either add orchestration: prefix to all skills or remove it from executing-workflows and creating-workflows

description: Command declares required argument <question> but provides no empty-input behaviour; invoking with no args passes empty string to ask.js which exits with a raw usage error.
suggested_fix: Add an empty-input section describing the error message or a graceful fallback.

description: Phrase 'when applicable' is a vague quantifier with no defined criteria.
suggested_fix: Replace 'when applicable' with a concrete condition, e.g. 'for every memory cited'.

description: Command declares required argument <query> but provides no empty-input behaviour; invoking with no args passes empty string to find.js which exits with a raw usage error.
suggested_fix: Add an empty-input section describing the error message or a graceful fallback.

description: Phrase 'when helpful' is a vague quantifier with no defined criteria.
suggested_fix: Replace 'when helpful' with a concrete rule, e.g. 'when 5 or more memories share the same session ID'.

description: Phrase 'when appropriate' is a vague quantifier with no defined criteria.
suggested_fix: Replace 'when appropriate' with a concrete condition, e.g. 'when 3 or more distinct stat categories are present'.

description: skills/memory/SKILL.md is byte-for-byte identical to skills/mind/SKILL.md except for the name field; dual maintenance burden and likely to diverge.
suggested_fix: Remove one of the two files; retain skills/mind/SKILL.md and delete skills/memory/SKILL.md (or vice versa).

description: skills/mind/SKILL.md is byte-for-byte identical to skills/memory/SKILL.md except for the name field.
suggested_fix: Remove one of the two files.

description: Stop hook timeout is 10 s in hooks/hooks.json but 30 s in src/hooks/hooks.json and dist/hooks/hooks.json; the stale copy should be resolved.
suggested_fix: Align hooks/hooks.json Stop timeout to 30 s or remove the root-level copy if dist/hooks/hooks.json is the runtime file.

description: File path '.mind.mv2' is incorrect; the actual memory file is stored at '.claude/mind.mv2'.
suggested_fix: Change '.mind.mv2' to '.claude/mind.mv2' on line 71.

description: File path '.mind.mv2' is incorrect; the actual memory file is stored at '.claude/mind.mv2'. (Same typo as in skills/memory/SKILL.md.)
suggested_fix: Change '.mind.mv2' to '.claude/mind.mv2' on line 71.

description: "$ARGUMENTS" is passed as a single quoted token so <query> and [limit] are concatenated; the hardcoded 10 on the bash line is always used as the limit, making the [limit] argument-hint non-functional.
suggested_fix: Split ARGUMENTS into positional parts or pass the limit as a separate shell variable so users can control it.

description: ${ARGUMENTS:-20} is unquoted in the bash invocation; word-splitting and glob expansion are active, enabling shell injection if ARGUMENTS contains metacharacters (e.g. /mind:recent $(id)).
suggested_fix: Quote the variable: "${ARGUMENTS:-20}" to prevent word-splitting and command substitution.

description: execSync('npm install …') runs a live npm registry fetch on every SessionStart; a compromised @memvid/sdk version would execute arbitrary code in the user environment.
suggested_fix: Pin @memvid/sdk to an exact version in package.json and ship a committed package-lock.json so installs are reproducible and offline-verifiable.

description: Duplicate execSync('npm install …') install guard in ask.ts; smart-install.ts already handles SessionStart installs, making this redundant and an unnecessary network surface.
suggested_fix: Remove the install guard from ask.ts; rely solely on smart-install.ts.

description: Duplicate execSync('npm install …') install guard in find.ts; same issue as ask.ts.
suggested_fix: Remove the install guard from find.ts; rely solely on smart-install.ts.

description: @memvid/sdk: "^2.0.149" uses a caret range; automatic minor/patch upgrades could introduce breaking or malicious changes between installs.
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version "2.0.149" and commit package-lock.json.

description: proper-lockfile: "^4.1.2" uses a caret range; same supply-chain drift concern.
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version "4.1.2".

description: argument-hint documents <query> [limit] but the bash line hardcodes 10 as the limit; the hint and implementation are out of sync.
suggested_fix: Either remove [limit] from the argument-hint or fix the bash invocation to pass the user-supplied limit separately.

description: hooks/hooks.json Stop timeout (10 s) differs from src/hooks/hooks.json and dist/hooks/hooks.json (30 s); one of these is stale.
suggested_fix: Align the Stop timeout across all three hooks.json copies or remove the root-level copy.

description: 8 sibling file references (tdd.md, validation.md, agent-teams.md, communication.md, marketing.md, review-pr.md, triage-issue.md, release.md) not present in audit artifact scope; if absent every workflow branch is inaccessible
suggested_fix: Confirm companion files ship with the plugin package or bundle them; add CI link-check to validate relative markdown links resolve

description: Reference Files section links to ./references/patterns-javascript.md, ./references/patterns-python.md, ./references/patterns-shell.md, ./references/anti-patterns.md; directory not in audit scope — if absent pattern guidance section is entirely dead
suggested_fix: Confirm ./references/ directory ships with the plugin; add CI step to verify all relative markdown links resolve

description: execSync with shell:true and template-literal interpolation of ${pkg}; pkg is currently hardcoded 'better-sqlite3' so no injection today, but shell:true with string interpolation is a dangerous pattern that breaks if pkg ever receives external input
suggested_fix: Replace with execFileSync(npmBin, ['install', pkg, '--no-package-lock', '--no-save', '--silent'], {cwd, stdio, timeout}) — eliminates shell parsing entirely

description: npm install better-sqlite3 triggered automatically at hook import time when native binary absent; runs over network inside agent event loop with no user visibility, no progress output, and no opt-out mechanism
suggested_fix: Emit console.error diagnostic before auto-install; add CONTEXT_MODE_NO_AUTO_INSTALL=1 env var to disable the auto-install path

description: PostToolUse matcher covers Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, TodoWrite, Agent, and all mcp__ tools; all tool arguments including file content and user prompts flow into local session SQLite DB with no documented retention policy or user disclosure
suggested_fix: Narrow matcher to tools that produce session-relevant events, or add a README section documenting exactly what data is captured and how long it is retained

description: Shell variable $path interpolated directly into a node -e JavaScript string literal inside config_file(); a $path value containing a single-quote terminates the JS string and could execute injected code if path is externally influenced
suggested_fix: Pass path via process.argv: node -e "const p=process.argv[1]; const c=fs.readFileSync(p,'utf8')..." -- "$path" to avoid shell-string injection

description: HTTPS request to registry.npmjs.org executed automatically during diagnostics without user opt-in; leaks tool version and IP to npm registry on every diagnostic run
suggested_fix: Gate the version-check behind a --check-updates flag; document the network call prominently at script start

description: export NODE_PATH permanently modifies NODE_PATH in the calling shell session; affects all subsequent Node.js invocations in the same shell and can shadow system modules
suggested_fix: Wrap in a subshell: ( export NODE_PATH=...; node ... ) so the variable does not leak to the caller

description: eval echo used for indirect variable expansion over a hardcoded ADAPTER_VARS list; safe in practice because list is static, but the eval pattern is fragile and dangerous if the list ever becomes dynamic
suggested_fix: Use Bash indirect expansion ${!pvar:-} instead of eval to eliminate the eval pattern entirely

description: execSync(`codesign --sign - --force "${binaryPath}"`) interpolates binaryPath into a shell command; binaryPath derives from the plugin directory (controlled), but double-quote quoting is insufficient if the path contains spaces or special characters
suggested_fix: Use execFileSync('codesign', ['--sign', '-', '--force', binaryPath], {stdio, timeout}) to avoid shell interpretation

description: Zero example blocks for an orchestrator skill that spawns 10-20 agents per task; no sample agent invocation, no sample gh command output, no sample routing decision shown
suggested_fix: Add at least one end-to-end example showing a complete issue triage: agent roster built, dispatched in parallel, results routed to Architect review

description: Vague quantifier 'warm' in communication guidance ('be warm, technical, and always put responsibility on contributors') — 'warm' is subjective and not actionable for an agent
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete tone directives such as 'acknowledge reporter effort before correcting', 'start responses with what is correct before what needs fixing'

description: Root CLAUDE.md and configs/claude-code/CLAUDE.md share approximately 60% identical content (Think in Code block, Tool Selection list, Output block); configs/claude-code/CLAUDE.md is the more complete version and when one is updated the other can silently diverge
suggested_fix: Make configs/claude-code/CLAUDE.md the canonical source; have root CLAUDE.md delegate to it or merge the two into a single file

description: Skill references 8 companion markdown files (tdd.md, validation.md, agent-teams.md, communication.md, marketing.md, review-pr.md, triage-issue.md, release.md) not present in the audit artifact list; cannot confirm existence without full repo access
suggested_fix: Add CI step to validate all relative markdown links in SKILL.md files resolve before release

description: Reference Files section links to ./references/ subdirectory with 4 pattern files not present in audit scope; cannot confirm existence without full repo access
suggested_fix: Add CI step to validate all relative markdown links resolve; if reference files are missing, inline essential patterns directly into SKILL.md

description: Agent documentation does not declare a model anywhere; agents without a declared model default unpredictably at runtime

description: No agent I/O examples showing a user request and the agent's response; script invocation examples are present but these are not interaction examples

description: No output format specification; the agent doc does not state what format the agent returns its results in

description: Index file has no output format specification; as an agent-adjacent file it should document what consumers can expect from listed agents

description: Examples section contains only placeholder text (`[describe input]`, `[show expected output]`) rather than a concrete demonstration; template users may copy without replacing

description: Root collection SKILL.md has no concrete input/output examples; the Practical Guidance section describes approach but shows no before/after
suggested_fix: Add a ## Examples section with at least one concrete skill-activation scenario showing input context and skill output

description: Skill is built around MiniMax M2.1 interleaved thinking without noting this is a third-party model dependency; reduces portability claimed for the collection
suggested_fix: Add a Prerequisites or Dependencies note clarifying the MiniMax M2.1 API requirement, or abstract the skill to apply to any interleaved-thinking-capable model

description: Bullet items in 'Patterns to Avoid' section use `*Silent Tool Failure**` (single leading asterisk) instead of `**Silent Tool Failure**`; bold formatting is broken throughout that section
suggested_fix: Replace `*Silent` with `**Silent` on lines 28, 31, 34, 37, 40, 43, 46 to restore bold formatting

description: No `## Examples` section with concrete input/output pairs; practical guidance and gotchas are strong but the skill lacks a dedicated worked example
suggested_fix: Add a ## Examples section showing e.g. 'Before: cold start takes 45s → After: warm pool delivers sandbox in <2s' or a session lifecycle walkthrough

description: Examples section contains only one scenario; a second example showing different threshold regimes or a failure mode would improve clarity
suggested_fix: Add a second example demonstrating aggressive vs. conservative compaction threshold trade-offs

description: GitHub token embedded directly in git clone URL string (`x-access-token:{token}@github.com/...`); token is visible in OS process listings, shell history, and any logging that captures the command
suggested_fix: Pass credentials via GIT_ASKPASS environment variable or git credential helper rather than embedding in the URL; at minimum add a comment warning this pattern exposes secrets in logs

description: All five production dependencies use caret (`^`) ranges, allowing automatic minor and patch upgrades; supply-chain compromise via dependency update is possible
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions or switch from `^` to `~` (patch-only) and run `npm audit` in CI on every install

description: Custom install path is read from user input via `read -p` and passed directly to `mkdir -p` and `cp -r` without validation; a value containing `..` or shell metacharacters could write files outside intended directories
suggested_fix: Validate the path with `realpath --canonicalize-missing` and reject values containing `..`, null bytes, or characters outside `[a-zA-Z0-9/_.-]`

description: Default port documented as 3456 in zh-CN but 8080 in EN start.md; one value is incorrect
suggested_fix: Audit actual default in packages/cli source and update the incorrect doc set to match

description: Status example output shows port 3456 in zh-CN but 8080 in EN status.md; same root cause as start.md mismatch
suggested_fix: Align status example output port with start.md after confirming actual default

description: Router manifest example uses 'openai:gpt-4' (colon separator) instead of correct 'openai,gpt-4' (comma separator)
suggested_fix: Change '"default": "openai:gpt-4"' to '"default": "openai,gpt-4"' to match actual CCR router format

description: Related command link '/docs/cli/commands/status' missing '/zh' locale prefix; navigates zh-CN users to English page
suggested_fix: Change '/docs/cli/commands/status' to '/zh/docs/cli/commands/status'

description: Related command link '/docs/cli/commands/status' missing locale prefix in current zh-CN doc; same bug as backup version, not fixed on promotion
suggested_fix: Change '/docs/cli/commands/status' to '/zh/docs/cli/commands/status'

description: npm publish to public registry for @musistudio/llms without provenance attestation or OTP enforcement; compromised CI credentials enable malicious package release
suggested_fix: Add --provenance flag and gate execution with a CI environment check before publish

description: npm publish to public registry for @CCR/cli without provenance attestation; same risk surface as line 65
suggested_fix: Add --provenance flag and CI-only guard; consider requiring OTP or publish token scope restriction

description: docker push to Docker Hub without content signing (DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST); compromised credentials enable silent image replacement
suggested_fix: Set DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST=1 before push and gate docker push to CI-only execution context

description: docker push of 'latest' tag to Docker Hub without content signing; 'latest' is the most widely pulled tag, making unsigned push highest-impact
suggested_fix: Sign images with Docker Content Trust or cosign; require CI context guard before any docker push

description: All four devDependencies use '^' semver ranges (@types/node, esbuild, shx, typescript); minor/patch bumps can silently alter build output or introduce compromised transitive deps
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions and rely on pnpm lockfile for reproducible CI builds

description: Commands ccr stop, ccr restart, ccr code, ccr ui, and ccr activate have no output or 'produces no output' note; users cannot verify expected behavior
suggested_fix: Add an Output section for each command showing sample output, or note 'Produces no output on success' for silent commands

description: zh-CN translation of other.md inherits the same missing output coverage for all five auxiliary commands
suggested_fix: Mirror the output examples added to EN other.md once that fix is applied

description: 'background: Background tasks (typically lightweight models)' uses 'typically' without specifying which model tier or concrete example qualifies
suggested_fix: Replace with specific guidance, e.g. 'typically haiku-class models (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5)'

description: Manifest example uses 'api_base_url': 'https://api.openai.com/v1' (bare) while EN and zh-CN current versions use '/v1/chat/completions'; backup has drifted
suggested_fix: Update api_base_url in example to 'https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions' to match other versions and actual OpenAI endpoint

description: EN start.md documents default port 8080; zh-CN start.md documents 3456; same binary, same option, one is wrong — affects start.md and status.md simultaneously
suggested_fix: grep for default port in packages/cli/src and update the incorrect doc set; likely the zh-CN was updated for a port change that EN missed

description: Backup directory (dated 20260101_205603) in tracked repo contains preset.md and statusline.md that have diverged from current: wrong Router separator, wrong api_base_url, missing locale links
suggested_fix: Remove backup directory from git tracking or add to .gitignore; backup artifacts accumulate drift and confuse automated scanners

description: Raw template variable left unsubstituted in rendered skill body; renders as literal XML-like tag and may confuse LLM consumers.
suggested_fix: Remove the <args>$ARGUMENTS</args> line; arguments are already available to the LLM via the invocation context.

description: plugin.json description claims 67 styles and 15 stacks but SKILL.md self-reports 50+ styles and 10 stacks; enumerated stacks in description total 12, not 15.
suggested_fix: Audit the CSV databases for authoritative counts and update plugin.json description to match.

description: execSync called with a backtick template string; all three variables are hardcoded constants so no current injection risk, but the pattern is fragile — future user-controlled substitution would create shell injection.
suggested_fix: Replace with execFileSync('node', [generateScript, '--config', DESIGN_TOKENS_JSON, '-o', DESIGN_TOKENS_CSS]) to eliminate the shell-expansion surface.

description: subprocess.run passes 'npx shadcn@latest' without a version pin; each invocation silently downloads whatever npm publishes as latest. No shell injection because list form is used, but the package itself is unverified at runtime.
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific shadcn version (e.g. shadcn@2.3.0) or read the pinned version from the project package.json.

description: downloadRelease() fetches a ZIP from GitHub releases and writes to disk without checksum verification; init.ts extracts and installs the contents into the user project. A compromised release asset would be silently installed.
suggested_fix: Fetch the expected SHA-256 checksum from the GitHub release metadata and verify the downloaded buffer before extraction.

description: All four runtime dependencies use caret ranges (^), allowing silent minor/patch updates without explicit approval. Supply chain drift risk over time.
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions in package.json or enforce via a committed lockfile (package-lock.json or bun.lockb).

description: Dev dependencies use >= floor constraints with no upper bound (pytest>=8.0.0, pytest-cov>=4.1.0, pytest-mock>=3.12.0); a future breaking major release would be silently pulled in.
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions (e.g. pytest==8.3.5) or add an explicit upper bound (pytest>=8.0,<9).

description: name field is 'ui-ux-pro-max' without the 'ckm:' namespace prefix used by all other skills in this plugin (ckm:brand, ckm:design-system, ckm:ui-styling, etc.).
suggested_fix: Change name to 'ckm:ui-ux-pro-max' to match the plugin's naming convention.

description: Step 1 contains 'Stack: React Native (this project's only tech stack)' — a project-scoped note published in a generic skill that otherwise documents 10 stacks. Contradicts the rest of the file.
suggested_fix: Remove the parenthetical or replace with a note that React Native is the example stack used in Step 1 examples.

description: Workflow steps reference ai-artist, ai-multimodal, chrome-devtools, frontend-design, project-management, assets-organizing skills not bundled in this plugin with no fallback instructions for missing dependencies.
suggested_fix: Add a Prerequisites or Dependencies section listing required companion skills, or add fallback instructions for when they are absent.

description: Brand skill provides no output format description, no workflow example, and no sample of what brand context extraction looks like — pure routing to reference files.
suggested_fix: Add a sample output block showing what inject-brand-context.cjs emits, and a minimal 3-step workflow.

description: Slides skill has no output format, no example of generated slide HTML, and no direct workflow steps; all content is deferred to references/create.md which is not in the plugin manifest.
suggested_fix: Add a minimal output example (slide HTML snippet) and a 3-step quick-start workflow inline.

description: Code block references model ID 'gemini-3-pro-image-preview' which is not a confirmed public Gemini model identifier as of audit date; may cause silent generation failures.
suggested_fix: Verify the correct model ID against the Gemini API documentation and update the example command.

description: Vague qualifiers: 'stunning' (description), 'distinctive direction' (line 10), 'compelling' (line 14). These are aesthetic adjectives with no measurable threshold.
suggested_fix: Replace 'stunning' with a concrete output property, e.g., 'uses a named preset from slide-patterns.md'.

description: Vague quantifiers: 'comprehensive visual implementation plan' (line 4), 'relevant codebase' (line 16), 'similar features' (line 27).

description: Vague qualifiers: 'beautiful standalone HTML diagram' (description), 'distinctive aesthetic' (line 7), 'genuinely enhance the page' (line 8).
suggested_fix: Replace 'genuinely enhance' with a concrete condition, e.g., 'when the topic involves physical or human elements that Mermaid cannot represent'.

description: Vague quantifiers: 'comprehensive visual project recap' (line 4), 'concise note' (line 54), 'key source files' (line 23).
suggested_fix: Replace 'key source files' with 'entry-point files (index, main, app) and files changed most frequently in the time window'.

description: Vague quantifiers: 'comprehensive visual plan review' (line 4), 'significant area of concern' (line 64).
suggested_fix: Replace 'significant area of concern' with 'any risk with severity medium or higher'.

description: 'key' used ~6 times as a vague filter with no concrete threshold: 'key snippets', 'key metrics', 'key invariants', 'key function names', 'key implementation details', 'key decisions'. Each occurrence leaves qualification criteria undefined for the agent.
suggested_fix: Replace each 'key X' with a measurable criterion, e.g., 'key snippets' → 'the 5-10 lines that illustrate the core logic (already specified nearby — apply that threshold consistently throughout)'.

description: git clone fetches and installs external code from GitHub at runtime; supply-chain compromise of the upstream repo would silently deliver malicious content to all installers.
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific git tag or commit SHA and document the expected checksum.

description: Executes an external vercel-deploy script that publishes the user's HTML file to a public URL without a pre-deploy warning about data exposure.
suggested_fix: Add an explicit public-deployment warning to stdout before the deploy call.

description: rm -rf on $HOME/.pi/agent/skills/visual-explainer removes an entire directory tree on each reinstall; path is hardcoded so risk is limited, but accidental data loss is possible.
suggested_fix: Check if the directory exists and prompt before deletion, or back it up to a temp location first.

description: sed uses | as delimiter while interpolating $SKILL_DIR (derived from $HOME); if $HOME contains |, the sed expression breaks silently.
suggested_fix: Use a delimiter that cannot appear in a filesystem path, e.g., sed -i "s#{{skill_dir}}#$SKILL_DIR#g".

description: Root marketplace plugin.json declares version '1.0.0' while plugins/visual-explainer/.claude-plugin/plugin.json declares '0.6.3'. If the wrapper is expected to mirror the plugin version, this is stale.
suggested_fix: Clarify whether the two versions are intentionally independent; if not, sync the root version to 0.6.3 and update on each release.

description: share.md is formatted as README documentation (markdown headers, code fences, sections) rather than a command template (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). Every other command in the plugin follows the frontmatter template convention.
suggested_fix: Rewrite share.md as a proper command template with YAML frontmatter and a $1-based prompt body, moving the documentation content to a separate README or the SKILL.md sharing section.

description: These agents have `readonly: true` in their frontmatter but their body text instructs them to run bash commands, write files, or execute tools. Read-only agents cannot use `Bash`, `Write`, or `Edit` tools. This makes them non-functional for their stated purpose.

None of the droid agents declare a `tools` field in frontmatter. Claude Code defaults to an empty tool list for sub-agents without explicit `tools` declarations, meaning these agents can only use text generation — no file reading, no bash, no grep. Their bodies describe complex workflows requiring these tools.

The python-pro persona body describes using shell commands and reading files but has no `tools` declaration in frontmatter. Unlike droids, this is an isolated omission in an otherwise well-formed category.

Multiple sections are explicitly marked "In Progress" or "Planned" in the skill body. This means if the skill is invoked, Claude will encounter instructions for behaviors that are described as not yet implemented. The skill should either not be installed or have a clear capability disclaimer in its description/frontmatter.

The hook uses `python3` for JSON parsing with no fallback for environments where Python 3 is unavailable (e.g., minimal Docker containers, some CI environments). If `python3` is absent, the hook will fail silently or error, disrupting the UserPromptSubmit flow.

The curl-pipe-sh command references `gogcli.sh` but the rest of the codebase (including `skill-claw/SKILL.md`) consistently references `openclaw.ai`. This domain mismatch suggests the agent body was written with a stale or incorrect URL. Combined with FINDING-01, this is both a bug and a security concern.

description: Deprecated command stub has no allowed-tools declaration.
fp_reason: The command contains no tool invocations; it only emits a text message telling the user to switch skills. allowed-tools is irrelevant for a pure-text redirect stub.
rule_gap: The missing-allowed-tools rule should be suppressed or reduced for commands whose body contains no tool calls.
suggested_fix: Either add `allowed-tools: []` explicitly, or suppress via rule override in nlpm.local.md.

description: Deprecated command stub has no empty input handling. The command always produces the same redirect message regardless of input.
fp_reason: A deprecation stub whose only behavior is to emit a fixed redirect message requires no input-branch logic — there is nothing to handle.
rule_gap: The no-empty-input-handling rule should not penalise commands whose body is a single unconditional message.
suggested_fix: No change needed for a pure redirect stub.

description: 'tipicamente até 3 meses antes' when searching for an estorno's original transaction — 'tipicamente' is not a hard bound and cannot be enforced consistently.

description: 'caso raro' used to describe when a saque should be treated as an expense rather than a transfer — no decision criteria given, leaving the classification to model judgement.
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit criteria, e.g. 'only if the person explicitly states it is a fee (taxa de saque), not a cash withdrawal'.

description: 'algo crítico' in Modo B validation step is undefined — no list of what fields are critical vs optional.
suggested_fix: Enumerate the critical fields explicitly: 'cartao sem vencimento' and 'conta sem tipo' are critical; 'categoria sem subcategorias' is not.

description: npx -y fin-app-mcp has no version pin; any new npm release including a compromised one is silently adopted on next Claude restart.
suggested_fix: Pin to the current stable release: replace 'fin-app-mcp' with 'fin-app-mcp@X.Y.Z' (run 'npm view fin-app-mcp version' to get it).

description: MCP server contacts external API at https://fin-app-wine.vercel.app on every invocation; documents a live external dependency whose availability and trustworthiness is outside the plugin's control.
suggested_fix: Document the dependency in README and consider adding a health-check fallback message in the agent when the API is unreachable.

description: npx -y auto-downloads and executes the npm package without user confirmation at each invocation; standard pattern for MCP servers but elevates supply-chain risk slightly.
suggested_fix: Pair with version pinning (SEC-unpinned-semver fix) to bound the risk; no additional change needed once version is pinned.

description: Comment on line 4 documents curl|bash installation pattern; script is explicitly designed to be fetched from a remote URL and piped into bash with no integrity verification, creating a supply-chain attack surface

Shell variable $thread_prefix is directly interpolated into SQLite query string with no sanitization; malicious input such as "' OR '1'='1'; DELETE FROM threads; --" could corrupt the threads database
suggested_fix: Validate $thread_prefix matches ^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$ before passing to sqlite3, or restructure to avoid shell-string SQL construction

description: 'postinstall' npm lifecycle script executes scripts/postinstall.js automatically on every npm install; actual content prints a banner (low actual risk) but the unconditional execution hook remains active
suggested_fix: Remove lifecycle hooks and provide explicit npx ai-agent-team init instructions in README instead

description: 'preinstall' npm lifecycle script executes scripts/preinstall.js automatically on every npm install
fp_reason: Content only reads process.version and process.platform with no system modification or network access; purely read-only environment validation
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish preinstall scripts that are verifiably read-only environment checks from those that modify system state

description: spawnSync called with shell:true at lines 168, 176, and 188; args are currently hardcoded so injection is not immediately exploitable, but shell:true on an array invocation is unnecessary and removes a safety layer
suggested_fix: Remove shell:true from all spawnSync/spawn calls; passing args as an array already bypasses the shell safely

suggested_fix: Verify tarball integrity with a published SHA-256 or GPG signature before extracting and executing

description: `$file` (from hook JSON `attached_files[]`) is interpolated directly into a double-quoted echo forming a JSON object; a filename containing `"` or `\` produces malformed JSON and could inject a `"decision":"allow"` field, silently bypassing the security block
suggested_fix: Replace bare echo with `jq -n --arg f "$file" --arg r "Security Alert…" '{"decision":"block","reason":$r}'`

description: `$file_path` originates from JSON `attached_files[]` and is passed to `stat` and `cat` without verifying the path remains within the project root; attacker-influenced input could read arbitrary host files
suggested_fix: Resolve path with `realpath --relative-base "$PROJECT_ROOT"` and reject paths that escape the project directory

description: State file at `/tmp/claude-stop-${SESSION_ID}.state` uses SESSION_ID from untrusted JSON hook input; predictable path enables TOCTOU / symlink attacks in shared `/tmp/`
suggested_fix: Use `mktemp -t claude-stop.XXXXXXXX` and store the resulting path in an env var or side-channel safe location

description: Baseline file at `/tmp/claude-baseline-${SESSION_ID}.numstat` uses SESSION_ID from untrusted hook JSON; a pre-created symlink or file at that path can poison the git-diff baseline, causing the stop hook to under-count new changes
suggested_fix: Use `mktemp` to create a unique temp file and pass the path through a safe session-scoped variable

description: `printf '%b'` in MSG construction interprets backslash escapes from `$FILE_LIST`, which contains filenames from `git diff` output; crafted filenames with `\n` or `\t` can corrupt the JSON reason string sent to Claude Code
suggested_fix: Replace `printf '%b' "$MSG"` with `printf '%s' "$MSG"` to suppress escape-sequence interpretation

description: Command declares no `allowed-tools` field; tools used (Bash, ExitWorktree) are undeclared, making automated tool-permission audits unreliable
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Bash, ExitWorktree, Read]` to frontmatter

description: `$ARGUMENTS` is embedded in the command but no guard handles the case where it is empty; the Pre-flight section proceeds unconditionally
suggested_fix: Add an early check: `If $ARGUMENTS is empty, proceed with no additional context`

description: "Stage relevant files by name" — "relevant" is undefined; no criterion distinguishes relevant from irrelevant files
suggested_fix: Specify: "Stage all files shown in git diff HEAD except .env, credentials, and binary assets"

description: Command declares no `allowed-tools` field; tools used to read @-referenced docs are undeclared
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read]` to frontmatter

description: `$ARGUMENTS` is echoed at the end with no empty-input guard; if called with no arguments the final instruction `Then process the user's request: ` is a dangling imperative
suggested_fix: Add: `If $ARGUMENTS is empty, stop after the confirmation summary and await user input`

description: All shell command blocks contain `[YOUR_TEST_COMMAND]`, `[YOUR_DEPLOY_COMMAND]`, etc. — skill is structurally correct but non-functional until customised; a naive invocation would produce no useful output
suggested_fix: Provide a worked example (e.g., a Node.js deploy) to demonstrate expected substitution; or add a preflight check that aborts with clear guidance if placeholders remain

description: Workflow ends at `Step 5 — Verify` (read the output image) with no explicit confirmation message to the user; no summary of what was done or where the output file lives
suggested_fix: Add a Step 6 Report block: `Done: <operation> applied\n  Output: <path>\n  Dimensions: <WxH>`

description: Workflow ends at Step 6 `Post-process` (copy + delete) with no formal report to the user; final state and chosen variant path are not confirmed
suggested_fix: Add a brief report after cleanup: `Generated: <path>  Size: <WxH>  Variant: N of M`

description: "If none are good enough" (L104) and "Pick the single best variant" (L102) use subjective criteria with no measurable threshold for acceptance
suggested_fix: Define acceptance criteria: e.g., "Reject if character's face shape, color palette, or art style deviates visibly from the primary reference image"

description: Multiple vague intensifiers without definition: "genuinely useful" (L11), "routine tasks" (L22), "real consequences" (L22), "add value" (L17) — each leaves the usage trigger undefined and model-dependent
suggested_fix: Replace each hedge with a concrete example or criterion, e.g., "routine" → "tasks you can answer in one step from training data"

description: Skill ends at Step 5 (create missing files) with no summary to the user of which files were updated and what changed
suggested_fix: Add a Step 6 Report: list each file updated with a one-line summary of the change made

description: "Only update files where the change is meaningful" — "meaningful" is undefined; the model must guess what meets the bar
suggested_fix: Replace with the concrete skip list already in the `When to Skip` section: "Update only when a feature, API contract, directory structure, or status milestone changed"

description: "relevant source files changed" (L29) and "substantial implementation work" (description) use undefined thresholds
suggested_fix: Tie to the 50-line threshold already in the skill: "If relevant source files (matching the patterns in Step 2) changed"

description: Sections 2 (Architecture), 4 (Coding Standards), 5 (Testing), and 6 (Privacy/Security) are entirely commented-out HTML comments with no active rules; a project that installs this template gains no guidance in those four areas
suggested_fix: Uncomment and populate at least one concrete rule per section, or add a prominent TODO marker that fails NLPM scoring until filled in

description: File has no YAML frontmatter at all — opens directly with '# Add Parallel AI Integration'. Missing required 'name' and 'description' fields; skill cannot be installed or referenced by name.
suggested_fix:
Add ---
name: add-parallel
description: Install the Parallel AI provider integration for NanoClaw
--- at the top of the file.

description: References v1 source paths that don't exist in v2: src/ipc.ts, src/channels/whatsapp.ts, src/db.ts, src/whatsapp-auth.ts, groups/CLAUDE.md. Will cause Claude to attempt edits on nonexistent files.
suggested_fix: Update all file references to v2 equivalents documented in CLAUDE.md §Key Files.

description: Contains v1-era content (registered_groups.json, WhatsApp JID management, /workspace/ipc/ paths, ~/.config/nanoclaw/sender-allowlist.json, register_group MCP tool) mixed with v2 concepts. Main agent will attempt v1 operations against a v2 system.
suggested_fix: Replace with a v2-native CLAUDE.md that references the SQLite DB (data/v2.db), v2 entity model, and correct mcp__nanoclaw__* MCP tools. Remove all JID/IPC/registered_groups.json content.

description: Instructs Claude to execute 'curl -fsSL onecli.sh/install | sh' and 'curl -fsSL onecli.sh/cli/install | sh' — remote code execution without checksum verification. If onecli.sh is compromised or hijacked, all operators running /init-onecli execute attacker code.
suggested_fix: Replace with checksum-verified install: download to temp file, verify sha256, then execute. At minimum add a warning comment about the trust assumption.

description: Phase 3 instructs Claude to run 'sudo pfctl' and 'sudo tee' to modify system packet filtering rules. Errors in pfctl rules can break network connectivity system-wide; Claude lacks interactive confirmation context.
suggested_fix: Add explicit human-confirmation prompt before sudo steps. Add a '## Requires sudo' section explaining what requires privilege and why.

description: Uses sed -i.bak "s/^PARALLEL_API_KEY=.*/PARALLEL_API_KEY=${API_KEY_FROM_USER}/" — user-controlled variable interpolated directly into sed substitution. sed metacharacters (/, &, \) in the API key value can cause unexpected behavior or injection.
suggested_fix: Use grep-filter-and-append pattern instead: grep -v '^PARALLEL_API_KEY=' .env > .env.tmp && echo "PARALLEL_API_KEY=${API_KEY_FROM_USER}" >> .env.tmp && mv .env.tmp .env

description: 'prepare': 'husky' runs on pnpm install. While husky is well-known and pnpm minimumReleaseAge/onlyBuiltDependencies policies mitigate the primary risk, the prepare hook is a known supply-chain attack vector.
suggested_fix: Existing mitigations (minimumReleaseAge: 4320, onlyBuiltDependencies) are adequate. Document the prepare hook in README for transparency.

description: Stores full Chrome browser session to disk after X.com login. The session file grants full account access and must be treated as a credential. No security warning is provided to the operator.
suggested_fix: Document in x-integration/SKILL.md that config.browserDataDir must be protected (chmod 700). Warn operators that the stored session grants full X account access.

description: execFileSync runs TypeScript files whose paths derive from process.argv[4] (newCorePath). A malicious newCorePath could execute arbitrary code. Risk is low-medium in practice as this is a developer/CI script, not agent-facing.
suggested_fix: Validate newCorePath is within the project root before executing: check that path.resolve(newCorePath) starts with process.cwd().

description: No output-format declared. Channel install skills that produce observable state changes (imports wired, pnpm install run) should document expected output so Claude can confirm success.
suggested_fix: Add 'output-format: confirmation with changed files and next steps' to frontmatter, or add a ## Expected Output section.

description: No output-format declared. Same as add-discord pattern — systematic across all channel install skills.
suggested_fix: Add output-format declaration to frontmatter.

description: No output-format declared. Systematic gap across all add-<channel> skills.
suggested_fix: Add output-format declaration to frontmatter.

description: No output-format declared.
suggested_fix: Add output-format declaration to frontmatter.

description: Contains 6+ vague quantifiers: 'appropriate', 'relevant', 'some', 'various', 'sufficient', 'reasonable'. Each -2 under R29.
suggested_fix: Replace with specific, measurable language. E.g., 'check the last 50 lines of logs/nanoclaw.log' instead of 'check relevant logs'.

description: Contains 3 vague quantifiers: 'various', 'appropriate', and 'some'. Each -2 under R29.
suggested_fix: Replace with specific terms.

description: Declares 'Compatibility: NanoClaw v1.0.0. Directory structure may change in future versions.' in a v2 repository. Creates ambiguity about whether the skill applies to the current version.
suggested_fix: Remove or update the compatibility note to reflect v2 status.

description: References /workspace/ipc/available_groups.json and /workspace/ipc/tasks/ — paths from v1 file-based IPC system removed in v2. Main agent will not find these files and may emit errors.
suggested_fix: Replace IPC file references with v2 SQLite-based equivalents and correct MCP tool calls.

description: Instructs Claude to edit src/channels/whatsapp.ts directly. In v2, channel adapters are installed from the channels branch via /add-<channel> skills — not present on trunk by default. Creates false expectation.
suggested_fix: Update skill to describe v2 channel installation pattern (/add-<channel> skills) rather than direct source edits.

description: Hooks call undefined bash functions `calculate_pr_success` and `validate_pr_merge`; these are not shell built-ins and have no definition in scope, so post-task tracking silently drops.
suggested_fix: Replace with `npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks post-task --task-id ... --success ...` or define the functions in a sourced helper script.

description: Hook calls undefined bash function `calculate_review_quality`; post-task learning never fires for issue review work.
suggested_fix: Replace with `npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks post-task --task-id ... --success ...`.

description: Hook calls undefined bash function `calculate_release_quality`; release-quality metrics are never stored.
suggested_fix: Replace with `npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks post-task --task-id ... --success ...`.

description: Pre hook calls `memory_store "sparc_phase" "specification"` — `memory_store` is not a shell command; SPARC phase tracking silently drops on every invocation.
suggested_fix: Replace with `npx claude-flow@v3alpha memory store --key sparc_phase --value specification`.

description: Pre hook calls `memory_store` and `memory_search` as bare bash commands; both are undefined shell functions.
suggested_fix: Use `npx claude-flow@v3alpha memory store/search` equivalents.

description: Pre hook calls `memory_store` as a bare bash command; undefined function causes silent failure.
suggested_fix: Use `npx claude-flow@v3alpha memory store ...`.

description: Pre hook calls `memory_store` as a bare bash command; undefined function causes silent failure.
suggested_fix: Use `npx claude-flow@v3alpha memory store ...`.

description: Pre and post hooks invoke `mcp__claude-flow__swarm_init`, `mcp__claude-flow__neural_patterns`, `mcp__claude-flow__model_save` etc. as bash commands; MCP tool identifiers are not executables and will fail with command-not-found on every hook trigger.
suggested_fix: Replace MCP tool invocations in hooks with `npx claude-flow@v3alpha` CLI equivalents, or move coordination logic into the agent body where MCP tools are called by the LLM.

description: Hooks invoke `mcp__claude-flow__swarm_init hierarchical`, `mcp__claude-flow__swarm_monitor`, `mcp__claude-flow__coordination_sync` as bash commands; all will fail with command-not-found.
suggested_fix: Use `npx claude-flow@v3alpha swarm init --topology hierarchical` etc.

description: Hooks invoke `mcp__claude-flow__swarm_init mesh`, `mcp__claude-flow__daa_communication`, `mcp__claude-flow__daa_consensus` as bash commands; all will fail.
suggested_fix: Use `npx claude-flow@v3alpha` CLI equivalents.

description: Post hook uses `$(mcp__claude-flow__swarm_status | jq -r '.topology')` as a bash subshell; the MCP tool name is not an executable, so jq receives no input and memory stores an empty string.
suggested_fix: Remove subshell or replace with `$(npx claude-flow@v3alpha swarm status --json | jq -r '.topology')`.

description: `name: analyst` in frontmatter conflicts with CLAUDE.md registry entry `code-analyzer` and body title; Claude Code will route `@code-analyzer` to the wrong file.
suggested_fix: Change `name: analyst` to `name: code-analyzer` to match the CLAUDE.md agent registry.

description: No `model` field in frontmatter; agent cannot be routed to an appropriate Claude tier by the 3-tier ADR-026 routing system.

description: No `model` field in frontmatter; affects all 7 consensus agents uniformly.

description: No `model` field in frontmatter; all 5 core agents (coder, reviewer, tester, planner, researcher) are missing model declarations.

description: Zero frontmatter `examples:` blocks; all 82 non-arch agents omit example input/output pairs, making behavioral validation from metadata impossible.

description: Zero frontmatter examples; consensus agent bodies contain JS implementation classes rather than behavioral guidance or illustrative use cases.

description: Vague quantifiers at cap: 'appropriate', 'optimal', 'intelligent', 'efficient', 'properly' appear 10+ times across the file body.
suggested_fix: Replace vague terms with specific thresholds, e.g., 'majority (>50% of nodes)' instead of 'appropriate quorum'.

description: Vague quantifiers at cap: 'intelligent', 'optimal', 'appropriate', 'dynamic', 'adaptive', 'efficiently' appear 10+ times.

description: Optimization agents (benchmark-suite, performance-monitor, load-balancer, resource-allocator, topology-optimizer) all hit the vague-quantifier cap; bodies are JS class stubs with no specific behavioral thresholds.
suggested_fix: Specify concrete performance targets (e.g., 'flag latency >500ms') rather than 'performance optimization'.

description: Benchmark-suite agent body is entirely JavaScript class stubs with no agent instructions, no output contract, and no hooks. Frontmatter has `name`, `type`, `category`, `description` but no behavioral content.

description: Body is 5 lines with no examples, tools, or hooks; provides almost no behavioral guidance beyond a single sentence.

description: File is an exact duplicate of `v3/@claude-flow/cli/.claude/agents/analysis/analyze-code-quality.md`; ~20 such duplicate pairs exist across the repo (nested `backend/`, `ci-cd/`, `unit/`, etc. subdirs mirror parent dirs).

description: `.claude/mcp.json` registers only the `flow-nexus` MCP server. All agent hooks referencing `mcp__claude-flow__*` tools (swarm, memory, neural, performance) require the claude-flow MCP server, which is not configured here. Agents break silently on a fresh clone.
suggested_fix: Add claude-flow MCP server entry to `.claude/mcp.json`: `{"claude-flow": {"command": "npx", "args": ["claude-flow@v3alpha", "mcp", "start"]}}`

description: SPARC agents use `npx claude-flow@alpha memory search-patterns` (v2 subcommand) while core agents use `npx claude-flow@v3alpha`. The v2 `search-patterns` subcommand does not exist in v3; SPARC hook memory lookups silently fail.
suggested_fix: Update SPARC agent hooks to use `npx claude-flow@v3alpha memory search --query ...`.

description: Root CLAUDE.md states '16 agent roles + custom types' in one place and '60+ agent types' in another. Audit found 84 agent files (~64 unique after deduplication). Neither count matches the actual state.
