suggested_fix: Quantify the savings, e.g., 'saves 5-10x tokens compared to manual file scanning'

description: Notes section lists 14 supported languages but CLAUDE.md architecture documents 19, explicitly naming Dart, R, Perl, Lua, and Jupyter/Databricks notebooks as additional languages
suggested_fix: Update the language list in Notes to match CLAUDE.md: 19 languages including Vue SFC, Solidity, Dart, R, Perl, Lua, and Jupyter/Databricks notebooks

description: uvx invokes 'code-review-graph' without a version pin; each session fetches and runs the latest published PyPI release, so a compromised or broken release is auto-adopted
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version: change args to ['code-review-graph==<current-version>', 'serve'] and update after intentional releases

description: Makes outbound HTTP requests to third-party APIs (MiniMax, Google Gemini, OpenAI-compatible endpoints) carrying API keys from environment variables; code content is included in request bodies when embeddings are computed
suggested_fix: Document exactly which data is sent to each provider; enforce TLS verification; ensure error messages do not echo sensitive source content

description: npm dependencies use caret ranges (better-sqlite3 ^12.4.1, d3 ^7.9.0, esbuild ^0.20.0) without a committed lockfile, allowing unexpected minor/patch upgrades on install
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions with --save-exact or commit package-lock.json to the repository

description: review-delta and review-pr use the '_tool' suffix for MCP tool names (get_review_context_tool, query_graph_tool, build_or_update_graph_tool) while debug-issue, explore-codebase, and review-changes use bare names; CLAUDE.md Key Tools table also uses bare names
suggested_fix: Standardize all skill files to bare tool names (without '_tool' suffix) to match CLAUDE.md and the majority pattern

description: build-graph Notes lists 14 languages; CLAUDE.md documents 19 — the skill's language list has diverged from the canonical architecture documentation
suggested_fix: Sync the language list with CLAUDE.md: add Dart, R, Perl, Lua, and Jupyter/Databricks notebooks to the Notes section

description: 'comprehensive' is a vague quantifier in the plugin description; does not specify what coverage means in practice
suggested_fix: Replace with specific language, e.g. 'multi-widget status line covering context, cost, and rate limits'

description: Step-2 heading 'add the appropriate function' uses vague quantifier 'appropriate'; selection criteria are specified in sub-steps but the heading itself is imprecise
suggested_fix: Replace with 'add the platform-specific function'

description: Bash(cat:*) declared in allowed-tools but no task step in the command body uses cat; file creation uses Write and jq instead
suggested_fix: Remove Bash(cat:*) from allowed-tools frontmatter

description: $ARGUMENTS is passed unquoted to the node shell invocation; user-supplied shell metacharacters (semicolons, backticks, dollar signs) execute as arbitrary shell commands
suggested_fix: Quote the substitution: node ... "$ARGUMENTS", or whitelist accepted values (--json, --lang en, --lang ko) and reject others

description: Command writes ~/.claude/settings.json via jq and mv, modifying a file outside the plugin repository
fp_reason: Writing ~/.claude/settings.json is the explicit documented purpose of a setup command; it is not unexpected or unauthorized
rule_gap: Rule should recognize commands whose frontmatter description and task body describe config-file installation as their primary purpose

description: Command appends a shell function to ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc (or PowerShell $PROFILE) outside the plugin repository
fp_reason: The command is named 'setup-alias' and its entire documented purpose is to install a shell function into the user's RC file
rule_gap: Rule should exempt commands that disclose their shell-RC modification intent in their name and description

description: Command writes ~/.claude/settings.json via jq and mv to update the statusLine path after a plugin version bump
fp_reason: Updating ~/.claude/settings.json to point to a new plugin dist path is the documented sole purpose of this update command
rule_gap: Rule should recognize update/setup commands whose task body shows only settings-path mutation as their complete logic

description: All four devDependencies use ^ version ranges: @types/node ^20.0.0, esbuild ^0.20.0, typescript ^5.0.0, vitest ^4.0.16; minor or patch bumps may introduce breaking changes in the build pipeline
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions matching the committed lock file, e.g. "esbuild": "0.20.2", and run npm audit on a weekly schedule

description: CLAUDE.md project structure tree lists 2 commands (setup.md, check-usage.md) but 4 command files exist: setup.md, check-usage.md, setup-alias.md, update.md
suggested_fix: Add setup-alias.md and update.md entries to the commands/ subtree in CLAUDE.md, with short inline comments matching the pattern used for existing entries

description: Command frontmatter is missing the required `name` field; command discoverability and registration may fail in strict plugin systems.
suggested_fix: Add `name: docs` to the frontmatter block.

description: Command calls MCP tools `resolve-library-id` and `query-docs` but declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter field; hosts enforcing tool allowlists will reject the calls.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [resolve-library-id, query-docs]` to the frontmatter block.

description: MCP server configured as external HTTP endpoint (https://mcp.context7.com/mcp); all tool calls route over the network to a third-party service.
suggested_fix: Document in README that all queries are sent to mcp.context7.com so users are aware of data-sharing implications.

description: MCP server configured as external HTTP OAuth endpoint (https://mcp.context7.com/mcp/oauth); same external data-routing concern as the Claude plugin variant.
suggested_fix: Same README disclosure as the Claude plugin variant.

description: All devDependencies use ^ (caret) ranges allowing minor/patch version drift on reinstall, which could pull in undiscovered vulnerabilities.
suggested_fix: Enforce --frozen-lockfile in CI install steps to pin the lockfile-resolved versions.

description: All production dependencies use ^ ranges (e.g. commander ^13.1.0, open ^10.1.0) allowing minor version drift in CLI distribution.
suggested_fix: Pin exact versions for production dependencies or enforce --frozen-lockfile in the publish pipeline.

description: Agent has zero <example> blocks; R09 requires minimum 2 with Context + user message + assistant response for reliable triggering.
suggested_fix: Add at least 2 <example> blocks showing a user asking a library question and the agent resolving + fetching docs.

description: Agent has zero <example> blocks; same issue as the Claude plugin variant.
suggested_fix: Add at least 2 <example> blocks matching those added to the Claude variant.

description: Agent body lists response content (direct answer, code examples, links) but provides no formal output template; response structure will vary between invocations.
suggested_fix: Add an ## Output Format section with a markdown template showing the exact heading/section structure.

description: Same missing output template as the Claude plugin variant.
suggested_fix: Add the same ## Output Format section as the Claude variant.

description: Agent frontmatter has no `model` field; the equivalent Claude plugin agent declares `model: sonnet`, indicating this was an oversight during copy.
suggested_fix: Add `model: sonnet` to frontmatter to match the Claude plugin agent.

description: Command takes a required <library> argument but specifies no behavior when $ARGUMENTS is blank — no error message or default behavior defined.
suggested_fix: Add an ## Empty Input section: e.g. 'If no library is provided, respond: Usage: /context7:docs <library> [query]'.

description: Command states 'Results include code examples and explanations' but defines no structured output template; format varies between invocations.
suggested_fix: Add a ## Output Format section with a template: library name, version, code snippet block(s), explanation text.

description: Five vague quantifiers: 'concise' and 'actionable' (L11), 'focused' (L5), 'relevant' (L26), 'appropriate' (L24).
suggested_fix: Replace 'concise' with 'under 150 words', 'actionable' with 'includes a runnable code example', 'relevant' with 'matching the user's question topic'.

description: Same five vague quantifiers as the Claude plugin agent (identical content): 'concise', 'actionable', 'focused', 'relevant', 'appropriate'.
suggested_fix: Same fixes as the Claude agent variant; both files must be updated together.

description: Two vague quantifiers: 'best' and 'relevant' both on L33 ('finds the best matching library', 'documentation relevant to your query').
suggested_fix: Replace 'best matching' with 'highest benchmark score and closest name match'; replace 'relevant to' with 'matching the text of'.

description: Three vague quantifiers: 'better' (L30 and L55) and 'closest' (L39).
suggested_fix: Replace 'better documentation quality' with 'higher benchmark score'; replace 'closest name match' with 'exact or prefix match'; replace 'better results' with 'more targeted results'.

description: Same three vague quantifiers as the Claude plugin SKILL.md (identical content): 'better' (L30, L55), 'closest' (L39).
suggested_fix: Same fixes as the Claude plugin variant; all three SKILL.md copies must be updated together.

description: Same three vague quantifiers as the two plugin SKILL.md copies (identical content): 'better' (L30, L55), 'closest' (L39).
suggested_fix: Fix in the canonical skills/ copy first, then propagate to both plugin directories.

description: Two vague quantifiers: 'best' (L50, 'use the best result you have') and 'closest' (L101, 'Use the closest match to what the user specified').
suggested_fix: Replace 'best result' with 'highest-scored result'; replace 'closest match' with 'matching or nearest version string'.

description: Four vague quantifiers: 'best' (L50), 'most relevant match' (L80), 'closest match' (L101), 'relevant details' (L116).
suggested_fix: Replace 'best result' with 'highest-scored result'; 'most relevant match' with 'match with highest benchmark score'; 'closest match' with 'matching version string'; 'relevant details' with 'library name and query'.

description: plugin.json has no `version` field; R48 marks version as recommended and R50 requires it to be bumped in four places on every release.
suggested_fix: Add `"version": "<current-release-version>"` to plugin.json and establish a release checklist covering all four bump points.

description: skills/context7-mcp/SKILL.md is byte-for-byte identical across plugins/claude/context7/skills/, plugins/cursor/context7/skills/, and skills/ — any fix requires three identical edits, making drift likely over time.
suggested_fix: Maintain one canonical copy under skills/ and reference it from plugin directories via symlink or explicit scope note; or add a scope note in each copy pointing to the canonical source.

description: Cursor agent is a copy of the Claude agent but is missing the `model: sonnet` frontmatter field present in the original, the first visible sign of copy-paste drift between plugin variants.
suggested_fix: Add `model: sonnet` to the Cursor agent frontmatter and establish a diffing step in the release process to catch cross-plugin parity gaps.

description: Skill instructs Claude to run curl piped to bash for CLI installation. Claude executes this on the user's machine, downloading and running arbitrary remote code without integrity verification.
suggested_fix: Replace with pip install or brew install once packages are published; or add checksum verification before executing the downloaded script.

description: Identical curl-pipe-bash CLI install pattern in the self-hosted skill. Claude executes this during self-hosted setup, same risk surface as the cloud skill.
suggested_fix: Replace with pip install or brew install once packages are published; or add checksum verification before executing the downloaded script.

description: Uses shell=True to chain profile-create and daemon-start commands with &&. Arguments are pre-escaped via shlex.join() which mitigates injection risk, but shell=True remains a high-severity pattern if config values change.
suggested_fix: Refactor to run the two subprocess commands sequentially with shell=False, eliminating the need for && shell chaining.

description: Identical shell=True daemon pre-start pattern in the codex integration. Same shlex.join() mitigation and same refactoring recommendation applies.
suggested_fix: Refactor to run the two subprocess commands sequentially with shell=False.

description: npm prepare script runs setup-hooks.sh automatically on npm install. The script only sets core.hooksPath and is low-risk in content, but auto-executed install scripts are a supply-chain surface.
suggested_fix: Document the prepare script's behavior in CONTRIBUTING.md or move git-hook setup to an explicit post-clone step.

description: Hook script POSTs full conversation transcripts to the configured Hindsight API on every Stop event. Intentional functionality, but consumers should be aware conversation content leaves the local machine to hindsightApiUrl.
suggested_fix: Document data-flow clearly in plugin README; ensure users understand conversation content is transmitted to the configured API endpoint.

description: Hook commands use __SCRIPTS_DIR__ as a path prefix instead of a runtime-resolved variable like ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}. If deployed without install-time substitution, all codex hooks invoke non-existent paths and silently fail.
suggested_fix: Document the substitution mechanism in the codex integration README, or replace with a runtime-resolved variable consistent with the claude-code plugin pattern.

description: Five vague quantifiers in review criteria: 'significantly changed' (line 130), 'non-trivial' (lines 135, 148, 167), 'meaningful' (line 156). These leave review thresholds ambiguous.
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete criteria: e.g., 'any new function or class', 'functions with more than 10 LOC', 'test files with at least one assertion per logical branch'.

description: 'appropriate' used three times as a guidance qualifier (e.g., 'design a retrieval strategy appropriate for the application'). Leaves the decision criteria open-ended rather than offering named patterns.
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete options: 'use one of: (A) naming convention, (B) store ID in application database, (C) list all models and match by name prefix'.

description: 'non-trivial task' is a vague trigger for when to recall memories. Reader cannot determine the threshold without further judgment.
suggested_fix: Replace with examples: 'before writing code, modifying configuration, or answering technical questions about the codebase'.

description: Same 'non-trivial task' vague recall trigger as hindsight-cloud/SKILL.md line 133.
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete task examples as suggested for hindsight-cloud/SKILL.md.

description: Same 'non-trivial task' vague recall trigger repeated in the self-hosted variant.
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete task examples consistent across all three memory skill variants.

pattern: missing async, missing SessionEnd, template placeholder
description: Codex hooks.json lacks async:true on Stop hook (unlike claude-code which uses async+15s), uses 30s sync timeout instead, and is missing the SessionEnd hook entirely — meaning no guaranteed final retain on session close.
suggested_fix: Add async:true to Stop hook, add SessionEnd hook matching claude-code pattern, and document or resolve the __SCRIPTS_DIR__ placeholder.

description: Docs skill references references/ subdirectory (references/best-practices.md, references/developer/api/, etc.) which is auto-generated by scripts/generate-docs-skill.sh. Skill accuracy depends on the generator being run; stale docs reduce usefulness but no missing files detected in this audit.
suggested_fix: Add a note in SKILL.md header indicating the auto-generated nature and the command to refresh: 'Run ./scripts/generate-docs-skill.sh to update'.

description: Codex and claude-code integrations use different variable conventions for script paths: __SCRIPTS_DIR__ (install-time placeholder) vs ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} (runtime env var). This inconsistency increases maintenance burden and risk of misconfiguration.
suggested_fix: Standardize on one approach across integrations; document the chosen pattern in a shared integration authoring guide.

description: Active skill with Write/Edit/AskUserQuestion tools has no model: field; runtime model selection is unspecified.
suggested_fix: Add model: sonnet (or haiku) to the SKILL.md frontmatter to make runtime model selection explicit.

description: Instruction uses vague quantifier 'good': 'Think about what you need to make good Balanced Coupling decisions' — no threshold or criterion defined.
suggested_fix: Replace 'good' with the specific criterion, e.g. 'well-informed' or 'decisions grounded in all three coupling dimensions'.

description: Instruction uses vague quantifier 'particularly': 'Implicit coupling … is particularly dangerous' — no actionable threshold given.
suggested_fix: Remove 'particularly' or quantify the risk: e.g. 'is the highest-risk form because the integration point is invisible to both parties'.

description: All 20 command files lack the required 'name' frontmatter field; Claude Code command registration relies on filename convention only, breaking discoverability in frontmatter-aware registries.
suggested_fix: Add 'name: devops' (and matching names) to frontmatter of every command file.

description: 14 agent-dispatch commands omit 'allowed-tools'; Claude Code cannot communicate permitted tools to the model, unlike sibling commands that correctly use 'allowed-tools: Task'.
suggested_fix: Add 'allowed-tools: Task' to all 14 agent-dispatch commands (api-docs, api, content, deploy, devops, frontend, marketing, plan, product, requirements, shadcn, tdd, test-first, ui).

description: Agent instructs the model to run ShadCN installation commands ('Install the required ShadCN components using the appropriate installation commands') but Bash is not declared in the tools list, making execution impossible.
suggested_fix: Add Bash to the tools frontmatter field, or rewrite installation instructions to use Task-based delegation instead of direct shell execution.

description: Agent instructs 'ALWAYS use the MCP server during planning' but no .mcp.json exists in the repository and no MCP tool appears in the declared tools list; MCP server is unresolvable.
suggested_fix: Add a .mcp.json with the relevant ShadCN MCP server configuration, or remove the MCP server reference and substitute with documented tool-based alternatives.

description: spawn('npm', ['run', 'dashboard:start'], { shell: true }) uses shell:true unnecessarily; all command components are hardcoded but the pattern enables shell injection if future edits interpolate user-controlled input.
suggested_fix: Remove shell: true (omit the key or set it to false); the array-form spawn does not require a shell and correctly handles argument separation.

description: Eight production dependencies use ^ semver ranges (chalk ^5.3.0, cli-table3 ^0.6.3, commander ^11.1.0, fs-extra ^11.2.0, inquirer ^9.2.12, ora ^8.0.1, yaml ^2.3.4, open ^10.0.0); a compromised minor release could inject malicious code.
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions or use ~ (patch-only) ranges; commit package-lock.json and enable npm audit in CI.

description: All dashboard dependencies (radix-ui packages, lucide-react, tailwind-merge, etc.) use ^ semver ranges; next is pinned at 14.1.0 but its dependencies are not, creating a transitive supply-chain risk.
suggested_fix: Pin all dashboard dependencies to exact versions; commit dashboard/package-lock.json.

description: Agent file contains CI/CD code templates and YAML configs but zero input-output examples of the agent responding to a user request; a reader cannot see how to invoke the agent or predict its response shape.
suggested_fix: Add an ## Examples section with at least two request/response demos (e.g., 'Set up a GitHub Actions pipeline for a Node.js app' → agent's actual output).

description: No ## Output Format section; the agent provides extensive reference templates but no defined structure for how it will communicate results back to the user.
suggested_fix: Add an ## Output Format section specifying the response structure (e.g., summary block, files modified list, verification status).

description: No explicit ## Output Format section; the agent has component examples and testing patterns but no defined response template for how it reports completed work.
suggested_fix: Add ## Output Format describing what the agent communicates when it finishes a feature (files created, patterns used, test results).

description: No explicit ## Output Format section; the agent has detailed test examples but no structured template for reporting test results or coverage metrics to the caller.
suggested_fix: Add ## Output Format with a coverage summary block similar to the test-runner agent.

description: Only one example workflow is provided (login page); the scoring rubric requires at least two examples to avoid a -5 penalty.
suggested_fix: Add a second example workflow, such as a data table or dashboard card implementation.

description: NotebookRead is declared in the tools list but the agent body contains no notebook-reading logic or use case; likely copied from a template.
suggested_fix: Remove NotebookRead from the tools list unless a specific notebook-based workflow is documented.

description: Command body uses $ARGUMENTS but provides no fallback when the user runs /refactor with no arguments; the agent receives 'Use the refactor agent to improve code structure and maintainability for: ' with a trailing blank.
suggested_fix: Add: 'If no arguments provided, refactor all recently modified files identified by git diff.'

description: Command body uses $ARGUMENTS with no empty-input handling; running /document bare produces an incomplete instruction.
suggested_fix: Add: 'If no arguments provided, generate or update documentation for all public APIs in the current project.'

description: Command body uses $ARGUMENTS with no empty-input handling; running /debug bare produces an incomplete instruction.
suggested_fix: Add: 'If no arguments provided, analyze the most recent error from terminal output or failing tests.'

description: Three vague quantifiers: 'robust' (l.7), 'scalable' (l.7), 'proper' (l.403 'Implement proper role-based access control'); none give the model measurable criteria.
suggested_fix: Replace with specific constraints, e.g. 'three-tier architecture' instead of 'robust', '10k req/s target' instead of 'scalable', 'least-privilege RBAC' instead of 'proper RBAC'.

description: Three vague quantifiers: 'comprehensive' (l.8), 'optimal' (l.64 'optimal agent assignments'), 'realistic' (l.154 'realistic time estimates').
suggested_fix: Use concrete terms: 'covering all phases' instead of 'comprehensive', 'matching agent specialization to task type' instead of 'optimal', 'based on historical velocity' instead of 'realistic'.

description: Four vague quantifiers: 'robust' (desc), 'scalable' (desc), 'comprehensive' (l.218), 'clean' (l.302); none specify measurable quality targets.
suggested_fix: Specify concrete targets, e.g. 'horizontally scalable to 3+ replicas', 'statement coverage ≥80%', 'consistent with project style guide' instead of vague adjectives.

description: Four vague quantifiers: 'appropriate' ×3 (ll.21,29,65 - 'appropriate ShadCN components', 'appropriate installation commands', 'appropriate'), 'comprehensive' (l.25).
suggested_fix: Replace 'appropriate ShadCN components' with 'ShadCN components matching the UI pattern in the demo tool'; replace 'comprehensive' with 'covering component hierarchy, install sequence, and responsive breakpoints'.

description: Three vague quantifiers: 'comprehensive' (desc), 'user-friendly' (desc), 'appropriate' (l.247 'appropriate documentation type').
suggested_fix: Use 'covering installation, API reference, and examples' instead of 'comprehensive'; 'navigable with a table of contents' instead of 'user-friendly'; 'matching the type inferred from context' for 'appropriate documentation type'.

description: Two vague quantifiers: 'Adequate' (l.67 'Adequate test coverage'), 'appropriate' (l.38 'Appropriate abstraction levels').
suggested_fix: Replace with 'minimum 80% statement coverage for new code' and 'abstraction depth matching the project's existing conventions'.

description: Two vague quantifiers: 'comprehensive' (desc), 'developer-friendly' (desc).
suggested_fix: Use 'covering all endpoints with request/response examples, error codes, and at least one SDK snippet per endpoint' instead of 'comprehensive and developer-friendly'.

description: Two vague quantifiers: 'actionable' (l.14 'clear, actionable user stories'), 'realistic' (l.154 'realistic time estimates').
suggested_fix: Replace 'actionable' with 'containing acceptance criteria and a definition of done'; replace 'realistic' with 'within ±30% of historical sprint velocity'.

description: Two vague quantifiers: 'modern' (desc), 'performant' (desc); neither gives the model a measurable target.
suggested_fix: Replace 'modern' with 'using React 18+ with hooks' and 'performant' with 'Lighthouse performance score ≥90'.

description: Two vague quantifiers: 'comprehensive' (desc), 'maintainable' (l.149 'maintainable and clear').
suggested_fix: Replace 'comprehensive' with 'covering all discovered test files'; replace 'maintainable' with 'avoiding test interdependencies and magic constants'.

description: Vague quantifier: 'comprehensive' (l.11 'comprehensive security audit').
suggested_fix: Replace with 'scanning secrets, OWASP Top 10 patterns, dependency CVEs, and configuration issues'.

description: Vague quantifier: 'compelling' in description ('compelling technical marketing materials'); not a measurable quality criterion.
suggested_fix: Replace 'compelling' with 'conversion-optimized, benefit-first'.

description: Vague quantifier: 'Appropriate abstraction levels' in the review checklist; no criterion for what counts as appropriate.
suggested_fix: Replace with 'abstraction layers matching existing codebase conventions (checked by grep of similar patterns)'.

description: Vague quantifier: 'comprehensive' in description ('comprehensive test suites').
suggested_fix: Replace with 'test suites achieving ≥80% statement coverage with unit, integration, and edge-case tests'.

description: Vague quantifier: 'appropriate agent based on task type' (l.45); does not specify the selection rule.
suggested_fix: Expand to the keyword-to-agent mapping already defined in the section below (api/endpoint → api-developer, test → tdd-specialist, etc.) and reference it explicitly.

description: Vague quantifier: 'related' in 'Launch ALL related agents simultaneously' — 'related' is undefined; the model must guess which agents qualify.
suggested_fix: Replace with 'Launch all agents whose tasks are independent of each other's outputs' or list the specific parallelizable combinations.

description: Twelve agent files and context-forge commands reference a JavaScript memory API (memory.set, memory.get, memory.isContextForgeProject, memory.getAvailablePRPs, memory.updatePRPState) as a live Claude Code feature; src/commands/run.js implements only an in-process in-memory store (src/memory/index.js) with no cross-agent sharing, making the advertised coordination non-functional inside Claude Code.
suggested_fix: Either implement the memory API as an MCP server accessible to Claude Code agents, or update all agent body text to remove memory coordination examples and replace with file-based state sharing.

description: commands/deploy.md and commands/devops.md both dispatch to the devops-engineer agent with no documented distinction; similarly commands/marketing.md and commands/content.md both dispatch to marketing-writer. Users cannot determine which command to use.
suggested_fix: Either merge the duplicate commands into one with an argument-hint, or document the specific use-case distinction (e.g., /deploy for deployment-only tasks, /devops for full pipeline setup).

Command frontmatter is missing the required name field; only description and allowed-tools are present, so the slash command cannot be registered or invoked by name
suggested_fix: Add 'name: agent-workflow' to the frontmatter block

description: CLAUDE.md refers to the utility agent as 'refactor-agent' but agents/utility/refactor-agent.md declares name: code-refactorer-agent; any call using the CLAUDE.md name will fail
suggested_fix: Either rename the frontmatter field to 'name: refactor-agent' or update CLAUDE.md line 111 to 'code-refactorer-agent'

description: CLAUDE.md defines Gate 2 threshold as 80%; spec-orchestrator defines it as 85%; agent-workflow.md collapses all gates into a single 95% threshold — three incompatible models in one system
suggested_fix: Adopt a single canonical threshold definition and update all three artifacts to reference it

description: CLAUDE.md documents --quality, --skip-agent, --phase, and --language flags for /agent-workflow but none of these are parsed in commands/agent-workflow.md; the command accepts only a raw $ARGUMENTS string
suggested_fix: Remove unimplemented flag documentation from CLAUDE.md or implement flag parsing in the command

description: CLAUDE.md shows 'cp agents/* .claude/agents/' but agents are in subdirectories; the glob will copy directory names not contents; the correct form is 'cp -r agents/* .claude/agents/'
suggested_fix: Change 'cp agents/*' to 'cp -r agents/*' in the setup instructions

description: Agent frontmatter has no model field; system default model will be used instead of an intentional tier choice
suggested_fix: Add 'model: claude-sonnet-4-5' or appropriate tier to frontmatter

description: Agent has zero usage or invocation example blocks; only implementation code templates are present, not examples showing how to invoke or what to expect from the agent
suggested_fix: Add at least two <example> blocks in the description showing user prompt and agent response

description: Agent frontmatter has no tools field; agent cannot read existing design files or write output documents in production; registration defaults to conversation-only mode
suggested_fix: Add 'tools: Read, Write, Glob' at minimum to enable file I/O

description: Agent frontmatter has no model field
suggested_fix: Add model field to frontmatter

description: No explicit output format section; the agent describes its analysis process but does not define the schema of refactoring proposals it produces
suggested_fix: Add Output Format section defining proposal structure: issue, why, before code, after code

description: NotebookEdit is declared in tools but the agent body makes no reference to Jupyter notebooks or notebook editing in its workflow
suggested_fix: Remove NotebookEdit from tools list unless notebook refactoring is an explicit use case

description: Command uses $ARGUMENTS as the feature description but provides no guard or user prompt for the empty-arguments case; invoking /agent-workflow with no text starts the full chain with no feature description
suggested_fix: Add an empty-input guard at the top: 'If $ARGUMENTS is empty, ask the user for a feature description before proceeding'

description: for (const key in process.env) adds ALL current env vars to esbuild define; any secret var referenced as process.env.X in the TypeScript source will be baked into the published dist/index.js binary
suggested_fix: Replace the loop with an explicit allowlist of only non-secret build-time vars (e.g. NODE_ENV); never derive the define map from the full environment

description: SECRET_KEY is explicitly added to esbuild define; if process.env.SECRET_KEY is referenced in source and a real value is set at build time, it will be embedded in the published npm package binary
suggested_fix: Remove line 10; SECRET_KEY should be read at runtime, not replaced at build time — gate any injection behind a build flag and ensure no real secret is present during npm publish

description: 'valuable' (line 32): 'unless interaction is valuable' gives Claude no actionable threshold for when to include JavaScript
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete criterion, e.g. 'unless the page requires user input or dynamic content rendering'

description: 'useful' (line 52): 'only when useful to the recipient' is not actionable — no rule tells Claude how to decide what is useful
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete criterion, e.g. 'only when the recipient is a developer or the deployment target is not already documented in the project'

description: 'appropriate' (line 53): 'only if appropriate' gives no decision rule for when to include provenance in the share page
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete criterion, e.g. 'only if the user explicitly requested attribution or the artifact was created in a Codex session'

description: 'when needed' (line 203): 'Worker-compatible libraries can be added when needed' sets no threshold for when extra dependencies are justified
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete trigger, e.g. 'when the standard Web API lacks a required feature such as JWT signing or UUID generation'

description: 'clear need' (line 249): 'if there is a clear need, it can also be adapted to return HTML' sets no concrete threshold for when non-JSON responses are warranted
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete criterion, e.g. 'when the route serves a browser-facing page or a redirect that must return a 3xx with Location header'

description: "relevant PinMe integration skill" — 'relevant' is a vague quantifier (R01); no criteria given for which skill to pick
suggested_fix: Name the specific skills explicitly: [[pinme-auth]], [[pinme-email]], [[pinme-llm]]

description: "Use clean, responsive HTML/CSS" — 'clean' is a vague adjective without measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete constraints, e.g. 'no external CSS files, no CSS frameworks, inline all styles'

description: "Keep the design restrained and readable" — 'restrained' is a vague adjective with no actionable definition
suggested_fix: Specify what restrained means, e.g. 'max 2 font sizes, max 3 colors, no animation'

description: "sensible mobile spacing" — 'sensible' is a vague quantifier; no specific spacing values given
suggested_fix: Specify concrete spacing, e.g. 'min 16px padding on mobile viewports'

description: "Keep the response short" — 'short' is vague; no token or sentence budget given
suggested_fix: Specify a concrete limit, e.g. '3 sentences or fewer'

description: "unrelated logs" — 'unrelated' is vague; criteria for what to exclude are not enumerated
suggested_fix: List explicit exclusion categories: build logs, test output, dependency install logs, etc.

description: "Do not require JavaScript unless interaction is valuable" — 'valuable' gives Claude no decision criterion
suggested_fix: Define the condition: 'only add JS if the page has user-triggered state changes (e.g. accordion, tab switching)'

description: "simple manual routing" — 'simple' is a vague adjective; no complexity threshold defined
suggested_fix: Remove 'simple' or define what constitutes the routing boundary

description: "some fs capabilities" — 'some' is a vague quantifier (R01); does not specify which APIs are available
suggested_fix: List the specific Node.js fs APIs that are available in Workers, or link to Cloudflare's compatibility docs

description: "if there is a clear need" — 'clear' is a vague qualifier; no decision criterion provided
suggested_fix: Replace with an explicit trigger condition, e.g. 'if the endpoint must return HTML for SEO or direct browser access'

description: "only splitting when isolation is truly needed" — 'truly needed' is vague; no isolation criteria defined
suggested_fix: Define isolation conditions, e.g. 'only when different tenants must not share data or billing is per-database'

description: Main pinme skill duplicates email, auth, and share content inline without scope notes pointing to the dedicated skills (pinme-email, pinme-auth, pinme-llm, pinme-share)
suggested_fix: Add scope notes at the top of each sub-section: 'For detailed auth integration, see [[pinme-auth]]'; remove inline duplicates

description: Cross-reference to sibling skills uses vague prose ('any relevant PinMe integration skill') instead of explicit [[slug]] links
suggested_fix: Use explicit references: 'use [[pinme]], [[pinme-auth]], [[pinme-email]], or [[pinme-llm]] first, then use pinme-share'

description: Web search example uses model ID 'openai/gpt-5.2'; this model may not exist and could mislead users copying the example verbatim
suggested_fix: Replace with a widely available model like 'openai/gpt-4o-mini' or add a comment noting the model ID should be replaced

description: for (const key in process.env) injects ALL build-environment variables into esbuild compile-time defines; combined with prepublishOnly auto-running this script on npm publish, any CI secret present during publish is baked into the publicly-distributed dist/index.js npm binary
suggested_fix: Allowlist only the specific non-secret env vars that the CLI needs at runtime (e.g. IPFS_PREVIEW_URL); never inject the full process.env object

description: process.env.SECRET_KEY is explicitly injected into the published bundle; if SECRET_KEY is a signing or encryption key set in the build environment, it ships publicly in the npm package
suggested_fix: Do not inject SECRET_KEY at build time; load it at runtime from the user's environment or a config file, never bundle it into the binary

description: All dependencies in the supabase example use caret ranges (^), allowing unexpected minor/patch upgrades; lower risk because this is example code, but reduces reproducibility
suggested_fix: Pin exact versions or add a comment documenting that floating ranges are intentional for the example

description: Email-sending code (handleSendEmail function and API reference) is duplicated between skills/pinme/SKILL.md and skills/pinme-email/SKILL.md, creating two sources of truth for the same API
suggested_fix: Remove the inline email section from skills/pinme/SKILL.md and replace with a scope note pointing to [[pinme-email]]

description: CLAUDE.md contains only '@AGENTS.md' redirect with no YAML frontmatter; cannot register as a skill.
suggested_fix: Add proper YAML frontmatter with name and description, or remove from skill discovery scope.

description: Frontmatter declares 'name: html-ppt-presenter-mode' but the directory is 'html-ppt-presenter-mode-reveal'; registry resolution by name diverges from directory-based lookup.
suggested_fix: Change 'name:' to 'html-ppt-presenter-mode-reveal' to match the directory name.

description: Frontmatter declares 'name: magazine-web-ppt' but the directory is 'guizang-ppt'; plugin install and trigger resolution diverge.
suggested_fix: Align name field with directory: change 'name:' to 'guizang-ppt', or rename directory to 'magazine-web-ppt'.

description: 'npx -y skills add heygen-com/hyperframes' downloads and executes a third-party npm package at runtime with auto-confirmation; no version pin, no hash verification.
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific package version and verify integrity with --integrity flag, or vendor the skill directly without runtime npx download.

description: postinstall script executes 'node ./scripts/postinstall.mjs' on every npm/pnpm install, running builds for multiple workspace packages automatically.
fp_reason: Script only invokes 'pnpm build' for internal workspace packages with no network calls, credential access, or dynamic code execution.
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between postinstall scripts that perform internal deterministic builds vs those that fetch remote code or run arbitrary shell commands.
suggested_fix: No action needed; document in CONTRIBUTING.md that postinstall runs workspace builds.

description: Downloads binary spritesheet files from j20.nz and Supabase edge function; magic-byte validation present but no cryptographic integrity check. External domain not under repo owner's control.
suggested_fix: Add SHA-256 checksum verification against a signed manifest; move asset hosting to a repo-controlled CDN.

description: Fetches from hardcoded Supabase project URL; if Supabase project is transferred or compromised, fetched data changes silently. No content-hash verification.
suggested_fix: Store Supabase URL in env var; add content-hash check on response.

description: Fetches raw markdown from two YouMind-OpenLab repos at 'main' branch HEAD; upstream changes propagate silently.
suggested_fix: Pin URLs to specific commit SHAs instead of branch names.

description: Fetches model price JSON from BerriAI/litellm at 'main' branch HEAD; no version pinning.
suggested_fix: Pin to a tagged release URL.

description: OD_KEEP_HF_SYNC_TMP env var skips temp-dir cleanup; combined with npx remote execution, exposes intermediate skill content under predictable tmpdir path.
suggested_fix: Use a randomized subdirectory within tmpdir; document the env var in README.

description: CLAUDE.md has no YAML frontmatter at all — missing both 'name' (-25) and 'description' (-25) fields.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter with name and description, or exclude from skill scanning.

suggested_fix: Add output contract section specifying that a 16:9 HTML deck matching these style constraints is emitted.

description: od: mode: utility only; output contract not specified in the primary frontmatter or early body section.

description: 'choose a short appropriate name' — 'appropriate' is a vague quantifier with no fallback rule or constraints given.

description: 'keep only the knobs you decided are relevant to this artifact' — 'relevant' is underspecified; no decision rule given for what makes a knob relevant.
suggested_fix: Use the five yes/no rules already present in the skill body (accent used ≥3 times → yes; type-driven → yes; etc.) and reference them explicitly here.

description: docs/examples/saas-landing-skill/SKILL.md and skills/saas-landing/SKILL.md are nearly identical; the example version is missing 'craft: requires:' block present in production version and includes author-guidance prose not suitable for the skills/ directory.
suggested_fix: Keep example version as pure reference; ensure any production updates to skills/saas-landing are mirrored to the example or add a 'see also' cross-link.

description: The deck composer reads '../editorial-collage/styles.css' at compile time; if editorial-collage is moved or renamed, the deck composition silently fails with no skill-level warning.
suggested_fix: Add a runtime check in scripts/compose.ts that errors clearly if ../editorial-collage/styles.css is not found.

description: Two skills have name/directory mismatches: 'html-ppt-presenter-mode-reveal' dir contains name 'html-ppt-presenter-mode'; 'guizang-ppt' dir contains name 'magazine-web-ppt'. These create inconsistent identities in the skill registry.
suggested_fix: Align name fields with directory names across the repo (or vice versa).

description: name field is 'html-ppt-presenter-mode' but folder is 'html-ppt-presenter-mode-reveal'; master html-ppt/SKILL.md references this template as presenter-mode-reveal — daemon will register wrong name and full-deck template will never activate
suggested_fix: Change name to html-ppt-presenter-mode-reveal

description: name: saas-landing collides with docs/examples/saas-landing-skill/SKILL.md which declares the same name; whichever loads second silently shadows the first in the daemon skill registry
suggested_fix: Rename docs/examples/saas-landing-skill name to saas-landing-example or move examples out of skills-loadable scan paths

description: name: saas-landing collides with skills/saas-landing/SKILL.md; this is the example copy — rename to disambiguate
suggested_fix: Change name to saas-landing-example

description: postinstall hook fires node ./scripts/postinstall.mjs on every pnpm install; script content verified: runs pnpm build on 5 internal workspace packages only — no network access, no remote code execution
suggested_fix: No action required; add a comment in postinstall.mjs listing the exact allowed package set to prevent silent future expansion

description: Chrome launched with --no-sandbox reducing renderer isolation; acceptable for local CI but risks being inadvertently used in cloud/shared environments
suggested_fix: Gate --no-sandbox behind CHROME_NO_SANDBOX=1 env var; default off

description: subprocess.run(["curl",...]) calls https://api.openai.com/v1/images/edits and /generations with OPENAI_API_KEY from os.environ; key visible in process args (visible to ps/proc)
suggested_fix: Replace subprocess curl with Python requests library; pass API key as header dict, never as process arg

description: Writes to ~/.codex/ and reads CODEX_HOME env var, creating side-effects outside project root that persist after run completion
suggested_fix: Gate ~/.codex writes behind explicit CODEX_HOME env var; document side-effect in SKILL.md output contract

description: Chrome path hardcoded to /Applications/Google Chrome.app/... (macOS only); silently fails on Linux/Windows CI
suggested_fix: Add cross-platform Chrome discovery: check google-chrome, chromium-browser, then macOS path in order

description: Uses spawnSync to invoke pnpm build; low risk (internal packages only) but any future addition to the package list could introduce supply-chain exposure without re-review
suggested_fix: Add inline comment listing exact allowed packages; add automated check to prevent unauthorized additions

description: od block fields are scattered across skill body rather than structured YAML frontmatter; triggers absent from YAML header making the skill undiscoverable by daemon trigger matching
suggested_fix: Restructure frontmatter to include standard od block with mode, platform, scenario, preview, design_system; add triggers list

description: od block contains only mode and scenario; missing platform, preview, design_system; no explicit output contract section
suggested_fix: Complete od block with all required fields; add output contract section specifying audit report format

description: 4-bullet workflow insufficient for structured invoice output; output contract omits field inventory and file format; vague: some, appropriate

description: 3-bullet workflow; output contract missing required field inventory; vague: some, relevant

description: 4-bullet workflow; no output schema for the spec document produced; vague: some, appropriate

description: 3-bullet workflow; output contract omits OKR structure (objective count, key result format); vague: some

description: No artifact tag in output contract; skill dispatches to external tool without specifying returned artifact shape or MIME type; vague: some, various
