suggested_fix: Either rename folder to magazine-web-ppt or change name field to guizang-ppt; update any cross-references

description: Vague: some, appropriate; subagent script paths partially assume Codex environment without documenting cross-environment portability requirements
suggested_fix: Replace vague quantifiers with specific counts or thresholds; document minimum required environment variables in od block

description: Artifact hand-off from the hyperframes engine is not explicitly contracted; consumer must read dispatcher implementation to understand output shape; vague: some
suggested_fix: Add explicit output contract section; document the video artifact format and delivery path

description: Single-line @AGENTS.md redirect provides no NLPM frontmatter or project summary; acceptable as delegation pattern but undiscoverable by skill indexing
suggested_fix: No change needed if delegation is intentional; optionally add a comment noting AGENTS.md is the primary instruction source

description: Vague quantifier 'appropriate' in 'Choose the most appropriate visualization' — no criteria given for what makes a visualization appropriate.
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete decision criteria, e.g. 'Choose graph TB for sequential steps, mindmap for hierarchical concepts, sequence for actor interactions.'

description: Vague quantifier 'balanced' in 'balanced between simplicity and information' — subjective trade-off with no measurable boundary.
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete guidance, e.g. 'Include all primary steps and key decision points; omit implementation details and sub-steps.'

description: Vague quantifier 'sensible' in 'Use sensible defaults for unspecified options' — does not define what sensible means in context.
suggested_fix: List the explicit defaults: direction=TB, detail=standard, style=professional.

description: Vague quantifier 'appropriate' in 'Use appropriate arrow styles' — no criteria for selecting arrow style.
suggested_fix: Replace with rule: 'Use straight arrows (fromEnd) for parent-child hierarchy; use curved arrows for cross-group references.'

description: Vague quantifier 'subtle' in 'Use subtle background colors' — no color value or opacity range specified.
suggested_fix: Specify concrete values, e.g. 'Use preset color 5 (cyan) at reduced opacity, or hex colors with opacity=0.15.'

description: Vague quantifier 'strategic' in 'Strategic colors: Use colors to encode meaning' — 'strategic' adds no precision.
suggested_fix: Drop the adjective: 'Use colors to encode meaning, not decoration. Reserve red (1) for warnings, green (4) for completed items.'

description: Example blocks in the Examples section show only process steps, not actual JSON Canvas output. A reader cannot verify output format compliance without loading the deferred reference doc.
suggested_fix: Add a minimal 2-node JSON Canvas snippet (one root node, one child node, one edge) directly in the Examples section.

description: 'Comprehensively' in description is a vague quantifier — replace with a measurable scope such as 'Reviews across 9 rule sets'
suggested_fix: Replace 'Comprehensively reviews' with 'Reviews … across 9 rule sets (API, views, data, navigation, design, accessibility, performance, Swift, hygiene)'

description: 'modern API usage' — 'modern' is undefined without a version anchor
suggested_fix: Replace 'modern API usage' with 'iOS 26+ / Swift 6.2+ API usage'

description: 'written optimally' — 'optimally' is unmeasurable; no criterion given for what optimal means
suggested_fix: Replace with 'written without redundant re-renders or unnecessary view rebuilds'

description: 'configured correctly' — 'correctly' is in R01's explicit vague list; replace with a structural rule
suggested_fix: Replace with 'follows single-source-of-truth data flow (no duplicated state)'

description: 'relevant reference files' — 'relevant' is in R01's explicit vague list; ambiguous about which files to load
suggested_fix: Replace with 'only the reference files whose sections apply to the code under review'

description: 'consistent project structure' — 'consistent' is unmeasurable without naming the structure
suggested_fix: Replace with 'feature-grouped project structure (one folder per app feature, not flat root)'

description: 'relevant line(s)' in Output Format — 'relevant' is in R01's explicit vague list
suggested_fix: Replace with 'the specific line number(s) containing the violation'

description: 'brief before/after code fix' — 'brief' is unmeasurable; no line-count guidance given
suggested_fix: Replace with 'a ≤5-line before/after code fix'

description: 'Comprehensively' in description is a vague quantifier — replace with a measurable scope
suggested_fix: Replace 'Comprehensively reviews' with 'Reviews across 9 rule sets (API, views, data, navigation, design, accessibility, performance, Swift, hygiene)'

description: 'modern API usage' — 'modern' is undefined without a version anchor
suggested_fix: Replace 'modern API usage' with 'iOS 26+ / Swift 6.2+ API usage'

description: 'written optimally' — unmeasurable; no criterion given
suggested_fix: Replace with 'written without redundant re-renders or unnecessary view rebuilds'

description: 'configured correctly' — 'correctly' is in R01's explicit vague list
suggested_fix: Replace with 'follows single-source-of-truth data flow (no duplicated state)'

description: 'relevant reference files' — 'relevant' is in R01's explicit vague list
suggested_fix: Replace with 'only the reference files whose sections apply to the code under review'

description: 'consistent project structure' — unmeasurable without naming the structure
suggested_fix: Replace with 'feature-grouped project structure (one folder per app feature, not flat root)'

description: 'relevant line(s)' in Output Format — 'relevant' is in R01's explicit vague list
suggested_fix: Replace with 'the specific line number(s) containing the violation'

description: 'brief before/after code fix' — 'brief' is unmeasurable; no line-count guidance given
suggested_fix: Replace with 'a ≤5-line before/after code fix'

description: Root-level SKILL.md has skill frontmatter (name: swiftui-pro, version: 1.1) but is not registered in plugin.json (skills: ./skills/); it is silently ignored by the plugin system and uses bare relative paths instead of ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} so references would fail if loaded standalone
suggested_fix: Either delete swiftui-pro/SKILL.md and back-port any improvements to the canonical skills/swiftui-pro/SKILL.md, or register it explicitly

description: Canonical SKILL.md declares version 1.0 while root SKILL.md declares version 1.1; the registered skill is the older copy, suggesting changes were made to the wrong file
suggested_fix: Bump canonical skills/swiftui-pro/SKILL.md to version 1.1 after reconciling any content differences

description: Command declares no allowed-tools field; Claude Code cannot restrict tool usage for this command.
suggested_fix: Add 'allowed-tools: [Bash]' to frontmatter since the command only needs to run 'refly login'.

description: No output format specified; success and error messages shown to the user after login are undefined.
suggested_fix: Add an Output section: on success print authenticated user and endpoint; on failure print the error hint from CLI JSON.

description: Command prompts for API key but does not specify behavior when invoked with no key and REFLY_API_KEY env var is unset.
suggested_fix: Add: 'If no API key provided and REFLY_API_KEY is not set, prompt the user to obtain a key from the Refly dashboard before retrying.'

description: Command declares no allowed-tools field.
suggested_fix: Add 'allowed-tools: [Bash]' to frontmatter.

description: Command declares no allowed-tools field.
suggested_fix: Add 'allowed-tools: [Bash]' to frontmatter.

description: No output format specified; what Claude tells the user after the upgrade completes is undefined.
suggested_fix: Add an Output section describing success message (e.g., 'SKILL.md updated to version X') and instructions for when upgrade fails.

description: new Function(`return ${str}`)() executes translation file content as JavaScript; a malicious translation file could run arbitrary code in the developer's environment.
suggested_fix: Replace eval-based parsing with a dedicated TypeScript/JSON parser or restrict the regex preprocessing to guarantee the input is a safe JSON-compatible object before evaluation.

description: 'prepare': 'husky' runs automatically on npm install; legitimate husky pattern but satisfies postinstall-script HIGH pattern.
fp_reason: husky is a well-known, widely-audited git hooks manager and 'prepare: husky' is its canonical recommended installation; no custom code is executed beyond husky's own init.
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between known-safe tool invocations (husky, lint-staged) and custom scripts in prepare/postinstall hooks.

description: MINIO_EXTERNAL_ACCESS_KEY and MINIO_EXTERNAL_SECRET_KEY read from env and passed to external MinIO client; misconfigured or leaked env vars expose storage credentials.
suggested_fix: Add startup validation to fail-fast with a clear error if credential env vars are empty, and ensure vars are not logged.

description: Full database connection URL (with embedded credentials) read from REFLY_DATABASE_URL_LOCAL; URL is easily captured by logging or error output.
suggested_fix: Redact the credentials segment of the URL before including it in any error messages or logs.

description: REFLY_REPO env var controls the git clone URL; an attacker who controls the environment before running the script can redirect the clone to a malicious repository.
suggested_fix: Add a URL prefix check asserting REFLY_REPO starts with 'https://github.com/refly-ai/' before executing git clone.

description: Most devDependencies use ^ ranges allowing automatic minor/patch resolution; supply-chain drift can introduce vulnerabilities between lockfile updates.
suggested_fix: Pin critical tools to exact versions and enforce --frozen-lockfile in CI.

description: SKILL.md References section cites 'rules/execution.md' etc. matching the deployed path (~/.refly/skills/base/rules/) but source files live at packages/cli/skill/references/; upgrade/install must map references/ to rules/ correctly or references silently break.
suggested_fix: Either rename the source directory from 'references/' to 'rules/' to match the documented deployed structure, or add a note in SKILL.md clarifying the source vs. deployed path distinction.

description: Step 4 instructs creating plugins/claude-hud/config.json (new file), but Write is not in allowed-tools. Edit cannot create new files in Claude Code; only Write can.
suggested_fix: Add Write to allowed-tools frontmatter: `allowed-tools: Bash, Read, Write, Edit, AskUserQuestion`

description: Vague quantifier: 'within a few seconds' gives no actionable timeout threshold for deciding when output is a failure vs. slow.
suggested_fix: Replace with a specific bound, e.g. 'within 5 seconds; if it hangs longer, cancel and debug.'

description: 'most common cause on macOS' implies an ordering claim not supported by data; reads as filler.
suggested_fix: Remove the parenthetical or replace with 'required step — the HUD cannot appear in the session where setup was run.'

description: Description 'Configure claude-hud as your statusline' omits cross-platform detection, ghost-install cleanup, and runtime discovery that the command performs.
suggested_fix: Expand to: 'Detect runtime, configure statusLine in ~/.claude/settings.json, and verify claude-hud is working (macOS/Linux/Windows)'

description: execAsync(cmd) calls Node's exec() which runs through /bin/sh. cmd is a user-provided string from --extra-cmd CLI arg. Shell injection is by design (user controls their shell), but exec() spawns an unnecessary shell wrapper.
suggested_fix: Parse --extra-cmd value into argv array and use execFile() to eliminate the shell layer; document that compound pipelines are unsupported.

description: Command runs `gh repo star jarrodwatts/claude-hud` (or gh api PUT /user/starred/...) which makes an authenticated GitHub API mutation. Gated on explicit user consent.
suggested_fix: No change needed; user consent gate is adequate. Consider noting that the user must have gh CLI authenticated.

description: devDependency @types/node uses ^ range (^25.6.0); allows automatic minor/patch upgrades on fresh installs.
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version or rely exclusively on package-lock.json with npm ci.

description: devDependency c8 uses ^ range (^11.0.0); allows automatic minor/patch upgrades on fresh installs.
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version or rely exclusively on package-lock.json with npm ci.

description: devDependency typescript uses ^ range (^6.0.3); allows automatic minor/patch upgrades on fresh installs.
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version or rely exclusively on package-lock.json with npm ci.

description: Write and Edit declared in allowed-tools but never referenced in any of the 5 command steps; all file operations are delegated to Bash scripts
suggested_fix: Remove Write and Edit from allowed-tools; keep Bash, Read, Glob, Grep

description: No formal ## Output section; output format guidance is embedded in step prose rather than a structured template
suggested_fix: Add an ## Output section listing the expected fields and format of the architecture summary

description: "better results" is an undefined comparator; does not specify the metric by which vineflower+dex2jar improves output
suggested_fix: Replace with "Recommend vineflower for higher-quality Java output on complex code and dex2jar for APK→JAR conversion required by fernflower"

description: "better Java output" is an undefined comparator with no metric specified
suggested_fix: Replace with "fernflower produces fewer decompilation errors on lambdas, generics, and anonymous classes"

description: "best quality" is an undefined comparator
suggested_fix: Replace with "if the user explicitly wants to compare both decompilers for accuracy"

description: "best results" is an undefined comparator with no metric
suggested_fix: Replace with "recommended for higher-quality decompilation of complex Java patterns"

description: "better Java output" in the engine-selection table is an undefined comparator
suggested_fix: Replace with "fewer decompilation errors on lambdas/generics"

description: "better code" is an undefined comparator
suggested_fix: Replace with "fewer JADX_WARNING annotations"

description: "broad sweep" is a vague scope quantifier; does not define which pattern categories are included
suggested_fix: Replace with "Run the API search script to scan all pattern categories (Retrofit, OkHttp, Volley, URLs, auth)"

description: pkg_install() invokes sudo apt-get/dnf/pacman unconditionally when sudo is available; package names are hardcoded so no injection risk, but sudo elevation proceeds without user confirmation
suggested_fix: Prompt the user before invoking sudo, or require an explicit --allow-sudo flag

description: add_to_profile() permanently appends export PATH and FERNFLOWER_JAR_PATH to ~/.zshrc, ~/.bashrc, or ~/.profile without asking the user
suggested_fix: Print the lines and ask the user to add them manually, or add a --no-profile-modify flag defaulting to off

description: install_jadx downloads a zip from GitHub releases via curl/wget with no SHA-256 or signature verification before extracting and making it executable
suggested_fix: Fetch the checksum file published alongside each GitHub release and verify with sha256sum before unzip

description: install_vineflower downloads a JAR from GitHub releases with no integrity check before setting FERNFLOWER_JAR_PATH and executing it
suggested_fix: Verify SHA-256 of downloaded JAR against the checksums published in the GitHub release

description: install_dex2jar downloads a zip from GitHub releases with no integrity check before extracting and symlinking scripts into ~/.local/bin
suggested_fix: Verify SHA-256 of downloaded zip against release checksums before extraction

description: gh_latest_tag() makes an outbound call to api.github.com to resolve the latest release tag; the response is parsed with sed/grep and used directly to construct a download URL without validation
suggested_fix: Pin to a minimum known-good version as a fallback (already done for dex2jar); consider pinning all three

description: cat prints manifest.json from a user-supplied XAPK archive directly to stdout; display-only but content is fully unsanitised
suggested_fix: Pipe through jq . to validate and pretty-print, rejecting non-JSON; or omit the cat and note the manifest path instead

description: run_grep() captures only $1 as pattern; calls run_grep -i 'regex' pass '-i' as the pattern and silently drop the regex, breaking the --auth case-insensitive search for credentials and base URLs
suggested_fix: Refactor run_grep to accept optional flags before the pattern, or add a separate run_grep_i() function that passes -i to grep

description: No ## Output section; skill drives a log investigation but does not specify what structured evidence or root-cause summary the agent should produce.
suggested_fix: Add a ## Output section describing the expected debugging report: root cause, evidence lines with timestamps, session_id, and probable failing stage.

description: Vague quantifier "relevant" in "Check these when the relevant run is older" — does not define a measurable threshold for when to check rotated logs.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete criterion, e.g. "Check rotated logs (log/symphony.log.*) when the target session predates the current log file's first entry."

description: No ## Output section; pure reference guide but callers cannot infer what structured result (e.g. JSON payload, confirmation field) to expect from each operation.
suggested_fix: Add a ## Output section noting that each workflow returns a GraphQL response object; document which fields (success, id, url) callers should check.

description: No ## Output section; step 9 describes a merge summary inline but no dedicated declaration clarifies expected deliverable format.
suggested_fix: Add a ## Output section: "A completed merge commit on the current branch; a summary comment naming the hardest conflict files, resolution strategy, and any follow-up assumptions."

description: Vague quantifier "minimal" in "Prefer minimal, intention-preserving edits" — does not specify how to measure edit minimality.
suggested_fix: Replace with "Prefer edits that touch only the lines needed to resolve the conflict marker, preserving surrounding context unchanged."

description: Vague quantifier "complex" in "For complex conflicts, search for related files" — leaves the threshold for "complex" undefined.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete signal, e.g. "When a conflict spans more than one logical concern (e.g. a rename plus a behavior change), search for related files."

description: No ## Output section; step 8 mentions "Reply with the PR URL" inline but no dedicated output declaration exists.
suggested_fix: Add a ## Output section: "The URL of the created or updated PR, printed to stdout. Validation errors from mix pr_body.check are surfaced before the URL is emitted."

description: Vague quantifier "proper" in "Write a proper PR title that clearly describes the change outcome" — does not define what makes a title proper.
suggested_fix: Replace with "Write a PR title in imperative mood, under 72 characters, that names the shipped capability or fix (e.g. 'Add retry backoff to Codex session runner')."

description: Vague quantifier "clearly" in "Write a proper PR title that clearly describes the change outcome" — paired with "proper", both in the same instruction without concrete criteria.
suggested_fix: Remove "clearly" and encode clarity in the structural constraint (imperative mood, named capability).

description: No ## Output section; the terminal state (squash-merged PR) is implicit in the goals but not declared as a formal output.
suggested_fix: Add a ## Output section: "A squash-merged PR on the main branch. The agent reports the merge commit SHA, the PR URL, and a one-line summary of any review feedback addressed."

description: Vague quantifier "judgment" in "Use judgment to identify flaky failures" — leaves flakiness detection undefined.
suggested_fix: Replace with observable signals, e.g. "Treat a failure as flaky if it is a timeout or platform-specific race that has not reproduced on the second run and has no code correlation."

description: Vague quantifier "brief" in "with a brief reason (e.g., out-of-scope, conflicts with intent, unnecessary)" — length is undefined.
suggested_fix: Replace "brief" with a concrete bound, e.g. "with a one-sentence reason".

description: Vague quantifier "brief" (second occurrence) in "offer a brief alternative or follow-up trigger" — same pattern as line 211.
suggested_fix: Replace "brief" with "one-sentence" for consistency.

description: Reference to AGENTS.md without a path prefix; no root-level AGENTS.md exists in the repo — the file is at elixir/AGENTS.md. An agent searching from the workspace root will fail to locate it.
suggested_fix: Change the reference to elixir/AGENTS.md.

description: SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter block; the required 'name' field is absent, preventing reliable skill indexing and cross-linking.
suggested_fix: Add a YAML frontmatter block at line 1 with 'name: phone-input'.

description: SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter block; the required 'description' field is absent, preventing trigger-phrase resolution and /help surfacing.
suggested_fix: Add 'description: "Call the user for real-time voice input, status reporting, or blocked-task clarification via Twilio phone calls."' to the frontmatter block.

description: 'significant work' has no measurable threshold; the hook cannot reliably distinguish significant from insignificant work.
suggested_fix: Replace with 'wrote or modified at least one file, or executed a command with observable side-effects'.

description: 'genuinely blocked' is undefined; no criteria distinguish genuine blockage from ordinary uncertainty.
suggested_fix: Replace with 'cannot proceed without a decision the user must make (e.g., choosing between two implementation approaches)'.

description: 'Trivial tasks' is vague; only one example given ('reading files'), leaving the boundary ambiguous.
suggested_fix: Replace with 'read-only tasks (file reads, searches, status checks)'.

description: 'significant task' has no size or scope criterion; any task could be argued significant by the model.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete threshold, e.g. 'a task that took more than one tool call to complete'.

description: 'complex decisions' is undefined; no criteria for what makes a decision complex enough to warrant a phone call.
suggested_fix: Replace with 'decisions requiring trade-off evaluation between two or more non-equivalent approaches'.

description: 'prestart' script runs 'bun install --silent' on every server start, fetching remote packages from the registry each invocation; combined with semver ranges, the installed dependency set can change between starts.
suggested_fix: Remove the 'prestart' script and document a one-time 'bun install' setup step in the README.

description: @ngrok/ngrok opens an outbound public internet tunnel on every server start; intentional for Twilio webhook delivery but means every run exposes a new public endpoint.
suggested_fix: Document the tunnel exposure in the README security section; consider restricting ngrok tunnel to Twilio IP ranges if the ngrok SDK supports it.

description: 'bun-types' is pinned to 'latest' with no semver range; resolves to whatever version is current at install time.
suggested_fix: Replace 'latest' with a concrete semver range such as '^1.1.38'.

description: Four runtime dependencies (@modelcontextprotocol/sdk, @ngrok/ngrok, openai, ws) use '^' ranges; minor/patch upgrades are pulled automatically on each bun install.
suggested_fix: Pin runtime dependencies to exact versions to ensure reproducible server behavior.

description: Bash(ls *) declared in allowed-tools but no ls invocation appears in any documented step.
suggested_fix: Remove Bash(ls *) from allowed-tools in the frontmatter.

description: 'gracefully' is a vague quantifier in 'handle ENOENT gracefully'; the expected fallback (return default Access object) should be stated explicitly.
suggested_fix: Replace 'gracefully' with the concrete behavior: 'If file missing, return {dmPolicy:"pairing", allowFrom:[], pending:{}}'.

description: Bash(mkdir *) declared in allowed-tools but no mkdir invocation appears in any configure step; directory creation is delegated to bun scripts.
suggested_fix: Remove Bash(mkdir *) from allowed-tools in the frontmatter.

description: 'complete picture' is a vague quantifier in 'give the user a complete picture'; the numbered list below already specifies the content concretely.
suggested_fix: Drop the 'complete picture' phrase; the numbered list (Credentials, Access, What next) is the spec.

description: plugin.json declares version 0.4.0 but package.json and server.ts MCP registration both declare 0.1.0; tooling that correlates these will see inconsistent versions.
suggested_fix: Align all three version strings to a single value, preferably driven from package.json.

description: start script runs 'bun install' on every MCP server launch, resolving and downloading dependencies at runtime rather than at install time, creating a supply-chain window.
suggested_fix: Move dependency installation to a postinstall hook or require users to run 'bun install' once; use 'bun run --frozen-lockfile' in start to prevent silent upgrades.

description: Auto-installs qrcode-terminal via Bun.spawnSync if node_modules is missing; same supply-chain risk as the package.json start script finding.
suggested_fix: Remove the auto-install block; print a clear error directing the user to run 'bun install' in the plugin directory instead.

description: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk uses '^1.0.0' and qrcode-terminal uses '^0.12.0'; caret ranges allow unreviewed minor/patch version bumps on each install.
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions or commit a bun.lockb and use '--frozen-lockfile' in all install invocations.

description: '--shell=bun' arg routes npm script execution through Bun's built-in shell rather than /bin/sh.
fp_reason: '--shell=bun' selects Bun's cross-platform shell, which is more restricted than /bin/sh and does not enable arbitrary shell injection; this is a safer choice, not a risk.
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between OS shell (sh/bash) and sandboxed runtime shells (bun shell, deno shell) which have narrower attack surfaces.

description: plugin.json (0.4.0) and package.json/server.ts (0.1.0) track versions independently; the public plugin version has diverged from the npm package version.
suggested_fix: Use a single source-of-truth version field, e.g. read it from package.json at build time or keep both files in sync via a release script.

description: Agent defines no example blocks; zero examples leave the model without calibration anchors for correct vs. incorrect behavior.
suggested_fix: Add at least two ## Example sections with concrete before/after prompt-response pairs.

description: No output format section; agent provides no guidance on expected response structure, length constraints, or rendering conventions.
suggested_fix: Add an ## Output Format section specifying response style, length, and any mandatory structural elements.

description: Vague quantifier: 'genuinely unclear' — does not define a measurable threshold for when to enter plan mode.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete condition, e.g., 'more than two mutually exclusive implementation paths exist'.

description: Vague quantifier: 'genuinely independent' — leaves model to judge parallelism threshold without a defined criterion.
suggested_fix: Specify a concrete criterion such as 'no shared output files and no sequential dependency between agents'.

description: Vague quantifier: '尽量' (try to / as much as possible) appears 5 times (lines 27, 29, 31, 32, 46); each instance avoids a concrete trigger or threshold.
suggested_fix: Replace each '尽量' with a specific condition or threshold that makes the guidance actionable and deterministic.

description: spawn() called with shell:true; command string is derived from process.argv.slice(2) or HELLO2CC_CCSTATUSLINE_COMMAND env var, creating a shell injection surface if either source is attacker-controlled.
fp_reason: shell:true is intentional design for a bridge script that must execute a user-configured downstream command; the script is not invoked by any hook in hooks.json and the command source is the developer, not untrusted hook input flowing from Claude's tool pipeline.
rule_gap: SEC-shell-true should distinguish between hook-invoked scripts processing user input and standalone dev-utility bridge scripts where shell:true is an expected implementation choice.
suggested_fix: If hardening is desired, accept the downstream command as a validated array of argv tokens and use spawn(cmd, args, {shell:false}) instead.

description: fetch() to api.github.com at runtime; GH_TOKEN / GITHUB_TOKEN env vars are read and sent as an Authorization Bearer header. Dev-only utility; not reachable from any hook invocation.
suggested_fix: Document the network dependency and token usage in README; add a --no-network fallback that omits acknowledgement resolution when the token is absent.

description: HELLO2CC_DEBUG_ROUTE_PATH env var controls the destination path of writeFileSync without path validation; a maliciously set env var could direct writes outside the repo.
suggested_fix: Validate the resolved path against a known safe root (plugin data dir or os.tmpdir()) before calling writeFileSync.

description: Default downstream command is 'npx -y ccstatusline@latest'; @latest is unpinned and will silently pull future versions on every invocation, introducing supply-chain risk.
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version tag, e.g., 'npx ccstatusline@1.2.3', and update explicitly on each plugin release.

description: Empty $ARGUMENTS not validated: running /review-loop with no argument silently creates a state file with a blank task body and starts the loop. The standalone setup-review-loop.sh correctly exits with an error for empty input but the command's inline bash omits this check.
suggested_fix: Add a guard before the state-file write: if [ -z "$ARGUMENTS" ]; then echo 'Error: No task description provided.' && exit 1; fi

description: Multi-step command body (setup → implement → stop → run Codex → address feedback) presented in unnumbered prose paragraphs rather than a numbered list, making sequencing ambiguous.
suggested_fix: Replace the prose flow with numbered steps: 1. Run setup bash. 2. Implement task. 3. When done, stop — hook will block and provide runner script. 4. Execute runner script. 5. Read review and address findings.

description: Command declares argument-hint but the body never specifies behavior when $ARGUMENTS is blank. Duplicate of Bug #1; counted as quality penalty too.
suggested_fix: Add explicit empty-input handling in the body: 'If no task description is provided, report an error and exit without creating state.'

description: No output format defined. The command ends with behavioral RULES but provides no report template or completion summary structure for what Claude should say when the review loop finishes.
suggested_fix: Add a Report section: 'Report: Review loop complete (ID: X). Addressed N findings: [list]. Skipped M findings: [list with reasons].'

description: Vague quantifier: 'thoroughly and completely' has no measurable criteria.
suggested_fix: Replace with specific expectations e.g. 'Implement all acceptance criteria, add tests for each new function, and verify no existing tests break.'

description: Vague quantifier: 'well-structured' has no measurable criteria.
suggested_fix: Specify the structural expectation: e.g. 'following existing module boundaries and naming conventions.'

description: Vague quantifier: 'well-tested' has no measurable criteria.
suggested_fix: Specify: e.g. 'with unit tests for each new function and integration tests for new endpoints.'

description: Multi-step body (check active → read state → remove files → report) presented in unnumbered prose; the four sequential steps have no explicit numbering.
suggested_fix: Number the steps: 1. Check if active. 2. If active, read state. 3. Remove state/lock/generated files. 4. Report cancellation with phase and review ID.

description: REVIEW_LOOP_CODEX_FLAGS is read from the environment and expanded unquoted at line 350 into a heredoc-generated shell script. If the variable contains shell metacharacters (;, &&, |), they are baked into .claude/review-loop-run-codex.sh and execute when Claude runs bash on it.
suggested_fix: Validate REVIEW_LOOP_CODEX_FLAGS against ^[a-zA-Z0-9 _=-]*$ before use, or store flags as an array and pass them safely rather than relying on unquoted word-splitting in a generated script.

description: The UX agent section of build_review_prompt() instructs the Codex agent to run 'npm install -g agent-browser' with no version pin. If the package name is squatted or typosquatted, a Codex agent operating on a UI project will install a malicious package.
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version (npm install -g agent-browser@<version>) or document agent-browser as a manual prerequisite and remove the auto-install suggestion from the prompt.

description: Script header uses ERR trap for cleanup but omits 'set -u' and 'set -o pipefail'. Unbound variables silently produce empty strings; pipeline failures in the middle of a compound command may go undetected.
suggested_fix: Change shebang line to: set -euo pipefail. The existing ERR trap already handles cleanup so the risk of double-cleanup is low.

description: Install instruction 'npm install -g @openai/codex' has no version pin. A breaking change in the Codex CLI could silently break the review workflow without any error at install time.
suggested_fix: Pin to a minimum known-working version, e.g. 'npm install -g @openai/codex@<version>'.

description: scripts/setup-review-loop.sh is a fully-implemented setup script with proper dependency and empty-input checks, but commands/review-loop.md embeds equivalent logic inline and never calls the script. The script has no documented callers and may drift from the inline bash over time.
suggested_fix: Either delete the script and rely solely on the inline bash, or refactor the command to call the script (which also fixes Bug #1 since the script already validates empty input).

description: "A handful of dimensions" — vague quantifier (comparable to R01 'several') with no measurable criterion for count.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete range, e.g. '3–5 dimensions'.

description: "a few lines: parse the threshold flag" — vague quantifier (comparable to R01 'some') with no measurable criterion.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete count, e.g. '~5 lines'.

description: "without meaningfully different framings" — 'meaningfully' is a vague modifier with no operationalized criterion for when framing difference is sufficient.
suggested_fix: Define a concrete test, e.g. 'briefs that target different files, hypotheses, or gate names are considered distinct'.

description: "a meaningfully different idea after an evaluated/discarded outcome" — same vague modifier as optimize/SKILL.md L147; subagents cannot determine what 'meaningful' means without a threshold.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete decision rule, e.g. 'a different file, function, or approach that was not already attempted on this branch'.

description: subprocess.run calls the claude CLI (network call to Anthropic API) with a prompt constructed from internal CASES data. No shell=True; inputs are not from untrusted external sources. Eval-tooling context only.
suggested_fix: Document that this script makes outbound API calls in the README; guard with a --dry-run flag for CI environments that restrict network access.

description: subprocess.run calls the claude CLI with a hardcoded prompt string for trial execution. No shell=True; no user-controlled input in the command array.
suggested_fix: Same as generate_fixture.py: document network dependency and add --dry-run flag.

description: subprocess.run calls the claude CLI as an LLM judge with a prompt assembled from ground truth + model output JSON. No shell=True; data is local fixture content, not external user input.
suggested_fix: Document network dependency; optionally add a --no-llm flag to skip the judge and use only the strict scorer.

description: Writes raw LLM output to /tmp/rlm_narr_{case_id}_raw.txt on JSON parse failure. case_id is drawn from the hardcoded CASES dict so no traversal risk, but /tmp writes are non-portable on Windows.
suggested_fix: Use tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(prefix='rlm_narr_', suffix='.txt', delete=False) for cross-platform portability.

description: Creates trial directory in tempfile.gettempdir() for test isolation. uuid4 suffix prevents collisions; intentional isolation pattern.
fp_reason: tempfile.gettempdir() is the standard cross-platform mechanism for temporary directories; this is intentional test-isolation, not a security risk.
rule_gap: The rule should distinguish between intentional temp-dir isolation patterns and uncontrolled writes to /tmp with user-controlled paths.

description: EVO_TRACES_DIR env var determines where trace files are written; EVO_RESULT_PATH determines the result file path. By design (evo wire protocol); caller is evo run which controls these vars.
fp_reason: EVO_TRACES_DIR and EVO_RESULT_PATH are the documented evo SDK contract; they are always set by the trusted evo CLI, not by untrusted user input.
rule_gap: The rule should account for env vars that are part of a framework's published API contract, not arbitrary external input.
suggested_fix: Add a comment noting that these vars must be set by the evo CLI, not by untrusted input.

description: Runtime deps flask>=3.0.0, portalocker>=2.8.0, pyyaml>=6.0.0 use open >= specifiers. uv.lock pins exact versions for managed installs but pipx/pip users without the lockfile may pull future breaking majors.
suggested_fix: Add upper bounds: flask>=3.0.0,<4; portalocker>=2.8.0,<3; pyyaml>=6.0.0,<7.

description: Description is a one-phrase summary ('Install Wasp agent skills...that add Wasp knowledge...') rather than 3+ specific trigger phrases matching real user queries.
suggested_fix: Rewrite as 3+ trigger phrases: 'Use when adding Wasp plugins, installing agent skills for Claude Code, or setting up Wasp-specific AI tooling in your project.'

description: No scope note linking to the two closely-related sibling skills (getting-started, guided-tour). Without it, Claude cannot distinguish between them when all three are active.
suggested_fix: Add at end: 'Covers Wasp plugin installation only. For first-run setup see [[getting-started]]; for a project walkthrough see [[guided-tour]].'

description: No scope note linking to guided-tour (natural next step) or add-wasp-skills (optional AI-tooling companion). Skill selection is ambiguous when all three siblings are loaded.
suggested_fix: Add at end: 'Covers initial setup only. For a project walkthrough see [[guided-tour]]; to add Wasp AI plugins see [[add-wasp-skills]].'

description: Description has 2 action phrases ('Take a guided tour', 'walks you through project structure, features, and customization checklist') — R04 requires 3+.
suggested_fix: Expand to 3+ phrases: 'Use when exploring Open SaaS project structure, understanding built-in features, or following the customization checklist step by step.'

description: No scope note linking to getting-started (prerequisite) or add-wasp-skills. Users who haven't completed setup may invoke guided-tour prematurely with no redirect guidance.
suggested_fix: Add at end: 'Prerequisite: complete [[getting-started]] first. To add Wasp AI plugins after the tour, see [[add-wasp-skills]].'

description: Script element created at runtime loading from https://static.reo.dev/<clientID>/reo.js — supply-chain risk if reo.dev CDN is compromised.
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific versioned CDN URL and add a Subresource Integrity (SRI) hash attribute to the script element.

description: PostHog project key CdDd2A0jKTI2vFAsrI9JWm3MqpOcgHz1bMyogAcwsE4 hardcoded in client-side script.
fp_reason: PostHog client project keys are intentionally public and must be embedded in browser code per PostHog's architecture; this is not a secret key.
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between public client-side analytics keys (PostHog, Segment write keys) and server-side secret keys to avoid false positives on standard analytics integrations.

description: Multiple devDependencies use ^ semver ranges (e.g. eslint ^9.39.4, prettier-plugin-tailwindcss ^0.6.14), allowing minor/patch drift on each install.
suggested_fix: Pin exact versions in package.json and rely on npm ci in CI to ensure reproducible installs.

description: wasp new -t saas base-app downloads a project template from Wasp's servers at script runtime; build reproducibility depends on Wasp CDN availability and template integrity.
suggested_fix: Document the external dependency in a comment; consider caching the base template or verifying a checksum post-download.

description: Skill declares no output format; no specification of how installation guidance should be presented to the user.
suggested_fix: Add a response format note, e.g. 'Present instructions as a numbered list; confirm with the user after each step.'

description: Step 3 uses vague directive 'Walk the user through' without specifying format, depth, or confirmation checkpoints.
suggested_fix: Replace with 'Present each installation step as a numbered list item; pause for user confirmation before proceeding.'

description: Skill declares no output format; no specification of response structure or completion signal.
suggested_fix: Add a completion statement, e.g. 'Confirm with the user once the app is running or explain what to do next.'

description: Step 4 uses vague directive 'walk them through the steps' without specifying presentation format or granularity.
suggested_fix: Specify 'Present each step from the guide as a numbered list item; verify success before continuing.'

description: Skill declares no output format; no specification of section presentation format or tour completion signal.
suggested_fix: Add format guidance, e.g. 'Use headers for each section; end the tour with a summary of what the user explored.'

description: Step 3 item 1 uses unmeasurable qualifiers 'clearly and concisely' with no definition of what constitutes clear or concise output.
suggested_fix: Remove qualifiers or replace with specific constraints, e.g. 'summarize each section in 3-5 bullet points.'

description: 'Keep the pace comfortable' is an unmeasurable directive with no heuristic for determining comfortable pacing.
suggested_fix: Replace with actionable instruction: 'After each section, pause and ask the user if they have questions before continuing.'

description: PostHog project API key hardcoded directly in public client-side JS file.
fp_reason: PostHog project API keys are intentionally public — they are used client-side to send analytics events and carry no write or admin privileges.
rule_gap: Rule should exempt client-side analytics keys (PostHog, Segment, Mixpanel) that are public by design from the hardcoded-credentials pattern.
suggested_fix: No change required; key is intentionally public. Optionally inject via build-time env var for configurability.

description: Reo analytics clientID hardcoded in public client-side JS file.
fp_reason: Client-side analytics identifiers (Reo, PostHog, GA) are intentionally embedded in public JS and carry no privileged access.
rule_gap: Rule should exempt analytics client IDs used in public browser scripts from hardcoded-credential findings.
suggested_fix: No change required. Optionally extract to a build-time constant for easier configuration across environments.

description: Production dependencies (stripe, openai, @aws-sdk/*) use caret ^ ranges, allowing automatic minor/patch upgrades that could introduce supply-chain risk.
suggested_fix: Pin exact versions for security-critical packages (stripe, openai, @aws-sdk/*) and rely on a lockfile with CI integrity checks.

description: npm script uses kill -9 with PID derived from grep on ps output; unintended process match could kill a wrong process in a shared environment.
suggested_fix: Store PID to a file when starting the stripe listener process and read from that file to kill it cleanly.

description: CLAUDE.md preview block lists `wonder` and `reflect` as core agents but no corresponding agent files exist in src/ouroboros/agents/. Every install that copies this block propagates the stale reference.
suggested_fix: Remove `wonder` and `reflect` from the preview block, or create the corresponding agent markdown files.

description: Frontmatter declares `mcp_tool: ouroboros_execute_seed` but Step 3 instructs calling `ouroboros_start_execute_seed`. The router and skill body reference different tool names.
suggested_fix: Align both references: update frontmatter to `ouroboros_start_execute_seed` to match the skill body, or vice versa.

description: `cat <meta.pm_path> | pbcopy` is macOS-only; fails silently on Linux and Windows with no fallback.
suggested_fix: Add cross-platform clipboard fallback: detect OS and use `xclip`/`xsel` on Linux or `clip` on Windows.

description: Skill instructs Claude to present `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh` in a Bash code block; Claude Code will execute this directly when the user chooses 'set up now', downloading and running an external shell script without integrity verification.
suggested_fix: Replace with documented out-of-band install step or use checksum-verified download; do not pipe to sh in automated Claude flows.

description: Script is designed to be invoked via `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Q00/ouroboros/main/scripts/install.sh | bash`; any compromise of the GitHub repository or CDN delivers arbitrary code execution to all users who run the install command.
suggested_fix: Publish signed releases with checksums and document verification steps; consider using a package manager exclusively instead of curl-pipe-bash.

description: Script echoes `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh` as an installation recommendation, instructing users to pipe a second external shell script into sh and compounding supply-chain risk.
suggested_fix: Reference the official uv installation docs URL instead of echoing a curl-pipe-sh command; users should verify instructions from the official source.

description: `urllib.request.urlopen('https://pypi.org/pypi/ouroboros-ai/json', timeout=5)` is called on every session start via session-start.py hook, creating a mandatory external dependency and latency in the hook path.
suggested_fix: Increase cache TTL or make update checks opt-in via a prefs flag; avoid blocking session start on external network calls.

description: Dynamically loads and executes version-check.py on every SessionStart hook, triggering a PyPI network call; exceptions are silently swallowed which masks connectivity or SSL failures.
suggested_fix: Add explicit error logging distinguishing network failures from version parse failures; consider running version check asynchronously.

description: `curl -s --max-time 3 https://api.github.com/repos/Q00/ouroboros/releases/latest` runs on every interview invocation for a version pre-check; not piped to shell but adds external dependency to every interview.
suggested_fix: Move version check to session-start.py where caching is already implemented; remove redundant check from interview skill.

description: `ouroboros-ai[mcp,claude]` has no version constraint in the MCP server command; a future breaking release will silently degrade or break existing installs.
suggested_fix: Pin to a range: `"--from", "ouroboros-ai[mcp,claude]>=0.36.0,<1.0.0"` or use a lock file mechanism.

description: Hook command `python3 "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/session-start.py"` interpolates an environment variable; a CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT value containing spaces or shell metacharacters could alter script resolution or cause command injection.
suggested_fix: Verify that CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is set by the trusted plugin runtime and cannot be overridden by user environment; document the trust boundary.

description: Agent declares no model tier; runtime will use the session default which may not match the intended complexity level for an autonomous research agent.
suggested_fix: Add `model: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` for lightweight research or `model: claude-sonnet-4-6` for complex synthesis.

description: 'optimal' in skill description is a vague trigger condition; no measurable definition of what constitutes an optimal pattern
suggested_fix: Replace 'ensure optimal performance patterns' with 'apply performance patterns described in this guide'

description: 'performance improvements' in the When to Apply trigger list is too broad; any code change could qualify
suggested_fix: Replace with a specific trigger like 'Diagnosing or resolving a measured performance regression'

description: SKILL.md references 'AGENTS.md' as the full compiled document but no AGENTS.md exists in the skill directory
suggested_fix: Create AGENTS.md by expanding all rule files, or remove the reference if the compiled document is not yet generated

description: execFileSync passes --eval flag with a template-literal script variable; pre-scan flagged critical
fp_reason: Script is built entirely from hardcoded template literals; API_CONFIG_MODULE is a path.resolve() result escaped with JSON.stringify(); no user input reaches the eval'd string; this is a test-harness isolation pattern
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish test-harness eval patterns (controlled inputs, isolated subprocess) from production code that passes external/user input to eval

description: spawn() called with shell:true on Windows for npm/npx/.cmd commands; args originate from user-configured MCP server settings, enabling shell injection via malicious MCP config
suggested_fix: Validate and sanitize args before spawning with shell:true, or use execFile without shell and handle Windows cmd/bat resolution separately

description: spawn() called with shell:true on Windows for npm/npx/.cmd commands; same risk as stdio-tools-getter.js — args from user MCP config could inject shell commands
suggested_fix: Validate and sanitize args before spawning with shell:true, or use execFile without shell and handle Windows cmd/bat resolution separately

description: All devDependencies and dependencies use ^ semver ranges, allowing minor-version upgrades that could introduce breaking changes or supply-chain vulnerabilities
suggested_fix: Pin exact versions or enforce lockfile integrity (--frozen-lockfile) in CI to prevent silent upgrades

description: Production dependency sql.js uses ^1.12.0 semver range; a database-access library in production should be pinned to an exact version
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version: "sql.js": "1.12.0"

description: AGENTS.md is cited as the full compiled document but does not exist in the skill directory; the individual rules/ files are present
suggested_fix: Generate AGENTS.md by concatenating all rules/*.md files, or update the reference to point to an existing resource

description: metadata.json abstract says '40+ rules' but SKILL.md header states '70 rules across 8 categories'; the rules/ directory contains 70 files confirming the SKILL.md count is correct
suggested_fix: Update metadata.json abstract to state '70 rules across 8 categories'

description: Four runtime dependencies (@modelcontextprotocol/sdk, zod, @google/genai, openai) use `^` semver ranges; downstream builds may silently pick up minor-version changes
suggested_fix: Lock to exact versions in production or commit a lockfile (package-lock.json) and review dependency updates explicitly

description: plugin.json declares version 1.2.0 but mcp-server/package.json declares 1.3.1; manifest is one minor version behind
suggested_fix: Update plugin.json `version` field to `1.3.1` to match the npm package

description: plugin.json has no `commands` field; commands/setup-video-vision.md and commands/watch-video.md may not be registered if Claude Code requires explicit declaration

description: plugin.json has no `agents` field; agents/frame-describer.md may not be registered if Claude Code requires explicit declaration
suggested_fix: Add `"agents": "./agents/"` to plugin.json to ensure frame-describer is registered

description: MSApp.execUnsafeLocalFunction call in vendored React DOM production build — IE compatibility shim in minified library, not custom code
fp_reason: This is a standard IE compatibility shim inside the vendored React DOM production minified library; the repo author did not write this code and cannot be injected by application logic
rule_gap: Pattern scanner should exclude files in known vendor/library paths (src/main/resources/libs/) and check for MSApp context which indicates IE browser-API usage rather than arbitrary code execution

description: spawn() called with shell:true on Windows when MCP server command is npx/npm/pnpm/yarn/.cmd/.bat; command originates from user-supplied MCP config, enabling shell injection via malicious server config
suggested_fix: Use shell:false and resolve .cmd/.bat wrappers explicitly via cross-spawn or shebang detection; never enable shell mode for externally-sourced command strings

description: Same spawn+shell:true pattern as stdio-tools-getter.js; verification path for STDIO MCP servers on Windows inherits the same shell injection risk
suggested_fix: Same fix as stdio-tools-getter.js: avoid shell:true; use cross-spawn or explicit resolution of Windows script wrappers

description: NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 is read from user settings.json and injected into process.env; disables all TLS certificate verification creating MITM exposure; code warns but still applies the value
suggested_fix: Refuse to inject NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 and document NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS as the supported corporate proxy alternative; or require explicit --unsafe-tls CLI flag rather than a settings.json key

description: import(resolvedUrl) where resolvedUrl is built from ~/.codemoss/dependencies/ (user-writable directory); a tampered package placed in that directory would execute at load time without integrity verification
suggested_fix: Verify a lockfile hash or subresource integrity value against a pinned manifest before importing; at minimum assert that resolvedUrl starts with the expected prefix and stat the file for unexpected mtime

pattern: execFileSync with --eval and dynamic script string
description: execFileSync passes a dynamically constructed template-literal script to node --eval; current content is derived from static file paths but the pattern makes future injection easier if input sources change
suggested_fix: Refactor test helpers to import api-config.js directly in the same process rather than spawning a child node process with --eval; if isolation is required, use a worker_thread with a hardcoded module path

description: All devDependencies and production dependencies use ^ or ~ version ranges; any future npm install could pull a compromised minor/patch release
suggested_fix: Pin all dependencies to exact versions and commit a package-lock.json or pnpm-lock.yaml; use npm ci in CI pipelines

description: sql.js dependency declared as ^1.12.0; patch or minor update could introduce a regression or supply-chain compromise
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version: "sql.js": "1.12.0"

description: SKILL.md references `AGENTS.md` as the full compiled guide but no AGENTS.md file exists anywhere in the repository
suggested_fix: Either create AGENTS.md by concatenating all rules/*.md files, or remove the reference and point readers to the rules/ directory directly

description: 'Comprehensive' in the skill subtitle is a vague quantifier that makes no measurable commitment; prefer 'covering 70 rules across 8 priority tiers' which the document already supports

description: Skill body references Workflows/Push.md as the authoritative workflow document; directory naming differs from repo convention (references/). File existence was not confirmed.
suggested_fix: Rename directory to references/ or verify Workflows/Push.md exists and update CLAUDE.md to document the non-standard layout.

description: Skill references Workflows/Extract.md and References/QuestionDesign.md; capitalized directory names differ from repo convention. File existence was not confirmed.
suggested_fix: Verify both files exist and match the expected content, or update references to match actual paths.

description: Skill instructs Claude to POST to localhost:31337 as a voice notification side-effect on every execution. Any local process can bind that port; fixed payload but undocumented runtime dependency.
suggested_fix: Guard behind opt-in env var: [ "${LJG_NOTIFY:-0}" = "1" ] && curl ...

description: Same localhost:31337 voice notification pattern as ljg-qa. Fixed payload but assumes a trusted service is bound to that port.
suggested_fix: Guard behind opt-in env var: [ "${LJG_NOTIFY:-0}" = "1" ] && curl ...

description: Runs npm install which executes postinstall lifecycle scripts from playwright and its transitive dependencies — supply-chain execution risk on first install.
suggested_fix: Use npm ci with a committed package-lock.json, or pass --ignore-scripts and handle playwright browser install separately.

description: SKILL=$1 is used unsanitized in rsync target path ($REPO/skills/$SKILL). A caller supplying ../../outside-repo with a matching local directory would rsync content outside the skills subtree.
suggested_fix: Validate SKILL matches ^ljg-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$ before use: [[ "$SKILL" =~ ^ljg-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$ ]] || { echo 'invalid skill name' >&2; exit 2; }

description: playwright dependency uses caret range (^1.58.2) allowing automatic minor and patch version upgrades on every fresh npm install.
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version: "playwright": "1.58.2" and commit package-lock.json.

description: CLAUDE.md Skill Inventory table lists 7 skills; actual repo contains 21 skills across skills/ljg-*/SKILL.md — 14 skills are absent from the table.
suggested_fix: Run a one-time enumeration of skills/ and update the table; consider auto-generating the table from plugin.json or skill frontmatter.

description: References references/template.org as the authoritative output structure. File was not present in the audited file set; if absent the output format instructions cannot be followed.
suggested_fix: Verify references/template.org exists in skills/ljg-paper/; if it does not, create it or remove the reference.

description: References references/template.org as the authoritative output structure. File was not present in the audited file set.
suggested_fix: Verify references/template.org exists in skills/ljg-paper-river/; if it does not, create it or remove the reference.

description: allowed-tools declares Bash(npx agent-browser:*) but line 25 of the body explicitly prohibits npx: 'Always use agent-browser directly -- never npx agent-browser'
suggested_fix: Remove Bash(npx agent-browser:*) from allowed-tools to match the documented restriction

description: No allowed-tools field in frontmatter; all five other SKILL.md files declare Bash(agent-browser:*) and Bash(npx agent-browser:*); omission may be intentional since skill teaches TypeScript SDK patterns rather than direct CLI invocations
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools if the agent needs Bash access during TypeScript scaffolding, or add a comment in frontmatter noting the omission is intentional

description: postinstall lifecycle hook auto-executes node scripts/postinstall.js on every npm install; script performs network download of a native binary and rewrites global bin symlinks/shims
suggested_fix: Document postinstall behavior in README security section; consider opt-in download flag for security-sensitive environments

description: symlinkSync replaces the agent-browser symlink in the npm global bin directory (system PATH location) at install time via fixUnixSymlink()
suggested_fix: Standard npm native-binary distribution pattern; acceptable if binary provenance is verified via published checksums

description: writeFileSync overwrites .cmd and .ps1 shims in the Windows global npm prefix dir outside the package directory via fixWindowsShims()
suggested_fix: Standard Windows npm shim optimization; acceptable if binary provenance is verified via published checksums

description: Downloads platform-specific native binary from GitHub Releases over HTTPS; URL constructed from package.json version field; no SHA256 checksum verification before chmodSync makes file executable
suggested_fix: Publish SHA256 checksums alongside GitHub Releases and verify digest before calling chmodSync

description: execSync invoked with shell command string 'ldd --version 2>&1 || true' for musl libc detection inside isMusl()
fp_reason: Command string is completely hardcoded with no user-controlled interpolation; standard platform detection pattern with no injection surface
rule_gap: Rule should exempt execSync calls where the entire command string is a compile-time constant with no template interpolation

description: COMMAND=$* captures all user-supplied CLI args; value is passed via Python json.dump to AWS SSM and executed as PowerShell on a remote EC2 instance; intentional developer debug tool
suggested_fix: Add a header echo warning naming the target EC2 instance before dispatching the command so the caller knows where code will run

description: subprocess.run(command, shell=True) passes LLM-generated command string directly to shell; same pattern present in all 13 agent files (s01-s12, s_full) and 2 reference scripts
suggested_fix: Document trust boundary (local use only) and remove false-security blocklist, or replace shell=True with allowlist-based command dispatch

description: Blocklist ['rm -rf /', 'sudo', 'shutdown', 'reboot', '> /dev/'] is trivially bypassed by trailing space, uppercase variants, or indirect paths; present in all agent run_bash() implementations
suggested_fix: Remove the blocklist (honest about capability) or replace with a proper allowlist of permitted command prefixes; document that these agents require a trusted user environment

description: anthropic>=0.25.0, python-dotenv>=1.0.0, pyyaml>=6.0 allow unbounded major-version upgrades on each fresh pip install, creating supply-chain and breaking-change risk
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions (==) or add upper-bound constraints, e.g. anthropic>=0.25.0,<2.0.0

description: All npm dependencies use ^ ranges; minor/patch updates can introduce regressions in a deployed Next.js app
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions for the web app's lock file; caret ranges are acceptable for dev-only tooling

description: Skill provides agent design guidance but declares no response template or structure for how the LLM should format its output when the skill is active
suggested_fix: Add a brief '## Output Format' section with a suggested response structure (e.g. design summary, capability list, anti-patterns flagged)

description: 'Load it when relevant' does not specify what conditions make knowledge relevant; reader cannot act on this without criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with measurable trigger: e.g. 'Load it when the user asks about [specific domain]'

description: 'Perform thorough code reviews' in the description does not define what thoroughness requires; checklist body provides criteria but the descriptor is still undefined
suggested_fix: Replace 'thorough' with the specific scope: 'security, correctness, performance, and maintainability'

description: 'conducting comprehensive code reviews' provides no specifics; the subsequent checklist defines scope but the framing word is still vague
suggested_fix: Drop the adjective or replace with enumerated scope matching the checklist sections

description: 'Meaningful, specific checks' uses 'Meaningful' without defining what separates a meaningful assertion from a trivial one
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete guidance: e.g. 'Assertions must verify observable behavior, not implementation details'

description: MCP builder skill provides templates and best practices but no guidance on how the LLM should structure its response when helping a user build an MCP server
suggested_fix: Add '## Response Format' section: confirm tool list, show project structure summary, highlight security checklist results

description: 'Return meaningful error messages' does not define what makes an error message meaningful (include error code? user action? context?)
suggested_fix: Specify: 'Error messages must include the tool name, the input that failed validation, and the expected format'

description: 'Never expose sensitive operations without auth' does not define which operations are sensitive; readers cannot apply this without a criteria list
suggested_fix: Enumerate: e.g. 'operations that write files, execute commands, or access credentials require authentication'

description: 'Tools should be safe to retry' does not define what safety means in this context (idempotent? no side effects? limited blast radius?)
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Tools must be idempotent — calling the same tool twice with the same arguments must produce the same result'

description: PDF skill teaches workflows and libraries but provides no template for how the LLM should structure its reply when applying the skill
suggested_fix: Add a brief '## Response Format' noting which tool was selected, the command run, and the output summary

description: '(preferred)' labels Option 1 as preferred without stating why; reader cannot make an informed choice in edge cases
suggested_fix: Specify the preference criterion: e.g. '(preferred when poppler-utils is available — fastest, no Python dependency)'

description: '(recommended)' for Markdown-to-PDF conversion is stated without criteria; reader cannot evaluate when to deviate
suggested_fix: Specify: e.g. '(recommended when source is already Markdown — avoids layout issues from programmatic PDF generation)'

description: 'PDFs may contain various character encodings' — 'various' is a placeholder; adds no actionable information
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'PDFs may use Latin-1, UTF-8, or proprietary CID-keyed fonts; use errors="replace" when decoding extracted text'

description: Declares name: mem0 (v0.1.1), identical to skills/mem0/SKILL.md (v3.0.0); skill loader cannot distinguish them by name, causing stale content to silently shadow the current version
suggested_fix: Rename plugin-bundled copy to a distinct name (e.g., mem0-plugin-bundled) or remove the duplicate and have the plugin reference the standalone skills/ copy

description: PostHog public capture key (phc_hgJkUVJFYtmaJqrvf6CYN67TIQ8yhXAkWzUn9AMU4yX) hardcoded in inline Python; indicates undisclosed third-party telemetry dependency
suggested_fix: Add a named constant with a comment labeling it a public analytics key and document it in the project's configuration surface

description: Migration script sends authenticated user email and OS info (platform, version, machine) to PostHog (us.i.posthog.com) without any disclosure in script output; opt-out via MEM0_TELEMETRY=false is not mentioned to the user
suggested_fix: Before the first capture_migration_event call, print a one-line disclosure that anonymous telemetry is collected and can be disabled with MEM0_TELEMETRY=false

description: SessionStart hook makes HTTP POST with MEM0_API_KEY to api.mem0.ai/v1/memories/, transmitting parsed session transcript content; intended plugin behavior but users should be aware conversation data is transmitted outbound
suggested_fix: Ensure plugin installation documentation clearly states that conversation summary data is sent to api.mem0.ai on session events; no code change needed if behavior is disclosed

description: Stop and PreCompact hooks make HTTP POST with MEM0_API_KEY to api.mem0.ai/v1/memories/ in background process, transmitting parsed transcript content; same disclosure concern as capture_compact_summary.py
suggested_fix: Same as capture_compact_summary.py finding — disclosure in documentation is the appropriate fix

description: Authorization header template uses Token ${MEM0_API_KEY}; standard MCP auth pattern, key is injected at runtime from env, not hardcoded
suggested_fix: No code change needed; ensure documentation notes that MEM0_API_KEY is required at plugin install time

description: High vague-quantifier density: relevant (x2), significant (x2), important (x2), genuinely (x3), notable (x1), comprehensive (x1) — 11 instances, penalty capped at -20
suggested_fix: Replace with measurable criteria: e.g., 'significant decision' -> 'decision with rationale or trade-off'; 'genuinely new' -> 'fact not present in recalled memories'

description: Example headings 11 and 12 each appear twice in the file (mixed-categories example and consolidation example both labeled Example 11; greeting NOOP appears twice as Example 12)
suggested_fix: Renumber examples sequentially: the second Example 11 should be Example 13 and the second Example 12 should be Example 14

description: Description states the skill triggers automatically after sufficient activity (configurable) but no hook in hooks.json invokes this skill; the auto-trigger is not implemented
suggested_fix: Remove the auto-trigger claim from description, or add a hook that invokes the skill after a configurable activity threshold

description: Vague quantifiers: 'sufficient activity' in description, 'obvious problems' in Phase 1 step 4
suggested_fix: Replace 'sufficient activity' with a measurable threshold; replace 'obvious problems' with 'duplicates, entries under 10 words, or entries missing temporal anchors'

description: Plugin-bundled mem0 skill is version 0.1.1 while standalone skills/mem0/SKILL.md is 3.0.0; content diverges in cross-reference style (GitHub URLs vs. local paths) with no documented sync mechanism
suggested_fix: Establish one authoritative copy and document update procedure in CONTRIBUTING; or bump plugin-bundled copy to match standalone on each release

description: Vague quantifiers: 'appropriate' (line 51 'if appropriate, call get_memories'), 'significant' (line 61 'significant time has passed'), and multiple uses of 'relevant memories'

description: Vague quantifiers across 620-line pipeline skill: 'relevant' (x2), 'plausible' (plan section), 'non-trivial' (description), 'minimal' (dependency surface)

description: xlsx@^0.18.5 — SheetJS has a history of prototype pollution vulnerabilities; caret range allows automatic minor/patch updates without explicit review

description: 11 production dependencies use ^ caret ranges (clsx, dompurify, highlight.js, jszip, juice, lucide-react, marked, modern-screenshot, papaparse, pptxgenjs, zustand) — semver minor updates are auto-accepted without lockfile review
suggested_fix: Commit package-lock.json to the repository and run npm audit in CI with --audit-level=moderate to catch known vulnerabilities in accepted version ranges

description: Body only 7 lines; no CSS/HTML output format stated, no font families, no color palette — insufficient for consistent AI output
suggested_fix: Add explicit output format (单文件 HTML), color palette hex values, and font family fallbacks in a 【设计细节】 section

description: '合理的 subreddit / username / 票数' (reasonable subreddit/username/votes) — no default fallback values or generation rule specified
suggested_fix: Provide explicit fallbacks, e.g. 'default subreddit: r/AskReddit; username: u/user; upvotes: derive from sentiment (positive → 10k–50k, neutral → 1k–5k)'

description: '合理年份 / 章节 / 地点' (reasonable year/chapter/location metadata) — ambiguous without a fallback rule
suggested_fix: Specify fallback: 'if not in user content, default year = current year - 1, chapter = 01, location = inferred from content context'

description: CLAUDE.md contains a single @AGENTS.md import; AGENTS.md itself is only 4 lines — combined provides minimal project-specific AI context
suggested_fix: Add project overview, key conventions (template skill format, category/scenario taxonomy), and output expectations to CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md

description: References 8 named theme variants (helix/holm/vance/bevel/world-dark/world-mint/atlas/bluehouse) without specifying any color palettes — agent must guess theme colors
suggested_fix: Add a color palette table for each theme with bg/ink/accent hex values, similar to deck-guizang-editorial's palette section

description: Pattern: 18 files have 4-line bodies with section names only (no hex colors, no font specs, no output format); representative: dashboard, dating-web, docs-page, team-okrs, pricing-page, meeting-notes, mobile-app, deck-presenter-mode, wireframe-sketch, social-media-dashboard
suggested_fix: For each affected file, add a 【设计细节】 section with color palette, font families, and explicit '单文件 HTML' output declaration

description: Pattern: 19 files have 4-7 line bodies with partial specs (layout described, design brief, no typography or explicit HTML output); representative: deck-blueprint, deck-graphify-dark, hr-onboarding, kanban-board, pm-spec, finance-report, eng-runbook, saas-landing
suggested_fix: Expand each to include at minimum: color palette hex, primary font family, canvas/aspect dimensions, and explicit output format

description: xlsx: ^0.18.5 — SheetJS was removed from the npm public registry; ^0.18.5 is the last public release (~2022) with no further security patches on npm
suggested_fix: Replace with actively maintained alternative (exceljs, SheetJS via official CDN) or migrate spreadsheet functionality to server-side with patched version

description: 23 of 25 package.json dependencies use ^ (caret) semver ranges, allowing automatic minor/patch upgrades that may introduce regressions or vulnerabilities
suggested_fix: Pin exact versions for production dependencies or enforce lockfile integrity with 'pnpm install --frozen-lockfile' in CI

description: CLAUDE.md delegates entirely to @AGENTS.md; AGENTS.md provides only a 4-line Next.js version caution with no template skill conventions, category taxonomy, or output format guidance
suggested_fix: Expand AGENTS.md (or CLAUDE.md) with project-specific conventions: template skill format, supported categories/scenarios, and the design system constraints shared across skills

description: 'manageable' used without a measurable criterion; R01 lists 'adequate/sufficient' as equivalent vague words
suggested_fix: Replace 'manageable' with the concrete bound already in scope: 'to stay within 5 parallel calls'

description: 'high-value' used to qualify URLs to fetch without defining what makes a URL high-value
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit criteria, e.g. 'an arXiv abstract, evaluation paper, or benchmark leaderboard'

description: 'high-value' used to qualify URLs without a measurable definition (same pattern as research-issues)
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit criteria matching the examples already in the parenthetical on the same line

description: 'relevant' appears in R01's explicit vague-word list; 'confirm the survey's top items remain relevant' gives no criterion for relevance
suggested_fix: Replace with 'confirm the survey's top items are still actively maintained or recently updated'

description: 'generally signal' introduces hedging without a threshold; equivalent to 'usually/typically' in R01 spirit
suggested_fix: Replace 'generally signal' with 'are a reliable proxy for' or cite a specific star threshold

description: 'significantly higher' lacks a numeric threshold for when to prefer a newer item based on sc score
suggested_fix: Replace with a measurable criterion, e.g. 'when sc is more than 3 points higher'

description: Python block writes URL cache to /tmp/awesome_ja_nlp_existing_urls.txt; predictable filename is world-readable and subject to symlink attacks in shared environments
suggested_fix: Use a unique temp filename: /tmp/awesome_ja_nlp_existing_urls_$$.txt or Python's tempfile.mkstemp(), and remove after use

description: Bash find command reads ${HOME} to locate plugin data; benign in normal use but env can be poisoned in CI contexts
suggested_fix: No change required for typical use; in CI, pin the plugin data path explicitly

description: requests.get to api.crossref.org for DOI resolution. Gracefully degrades when requests is absent; no credential handling; timeout=10 set.
suggested_fix: No fix required. Expected behavior for Crossref DOI resolution. Consider adding requests to optional dependencies list.

description: SessionStart hook executes announce-ars-loaded.sh on every session start. Script is stdout-only with no write side-effects and no network calls.
