description: Script is designed for and documents execution via 'curl -fsSL URL | bash'. The script itself is fetched and executed without integrity verification; only the downloaded binary artifact is SHA-256 checked after execution has already begun.
suggested_fix: Provide a checksum-verified download of the installer script itself, or publish signed releases so users can verify the script before executing. Private disclosure required.

description: eval "$(jq -r '...export \(.key)=\"\(.value)\"' scripts/ci-secrets.json)" — JSON key names are not shell-escaped. A tampered ci-secrets.json with a key containing shell metacharacters (semicolons, backticks, $()) would execute arbitrary code.
suggested_fix: Use 'export \(.key)=\(.value | @sh)' and validate key names match ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ before eval. Private disclosure required for HIGH severity.

description: eval "$(jq -r '...export \(.key)=\(.value | @sh)' $SECRETS_JSON)" — values are @sh-escaped and keys are filtered to VITE_ prefix, but the eval of jq output and the user-controlled $SECRETS_JSON path still introduce risk in multi-user CI environments.
suggested_fix: Confirm SECRETS_JSON points to the expected path before calling jq; consider replacing eval with explicit variable assignments for the small set of known VITE_ variables.

description: eval "$joined" where values are printf %q-escaped but key names from the .env file are not validated. A .env key containing shell special characters would be injected directly into the eval string.
suggested_fix: Validate each key against ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ before appending to exports; skip or warn on invalid keys.

description: The $FILTER argument (line 11, caller-supplied jq expression) is passed directly into jq without sanitization. A caller providing a malicious jq filter could exfiltrate data or cause unexpected eval behavior.
suggested_fix: Document clearly that $FILTER is trusted-caller-only input; add a comment warning against passing externally-sourced values as the filter argument.

description: postinstall runs install.js which fetches a platform-specific binary from GitHub Releases over HTTPS and places it in bin/. SHA-256 is verified but the download and installation happen automatically on npm install.
suggested_fix: Add an OPENHUMAN_SKIP_INSTALL=1 environment variable guard in install.js so CI and audit environments can npm install without triggering the binary fetch.

description: postinstall: 'husky' runs git hooks setup automatically on npm install. Husky is a well-known tool with small attack surface, but any postinstall constitutes an automatic execution surface.
suggested_fix: No action required unless restricting all auto-execution at install time. Consider 'prepare' script instead of 'postinstall' for husky to avoid running on --production installs.

description: export PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$INSTALL_ROOT/bin:$PATH" prepends using $INSTALL_ROOT which defaults to a caller-controlled path. In shared CI environments, a poisoned INSTALL_ROOT could shadow system binaries.
suggested_fix: Resolve INSTALL_ROOT to a canonical absolute path and reject values containing '..' or paths outside expected directories before using in PATH.

description: Missing name, description, and model frontmatter. File is an internal application prompt loaded via ?raw Tauri imports — intentionally not a Claude Code agent — but lacks any structured NL metadata.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter with name, description, and model fields if this file is intended to be managed as an NL artifact.

description: No example interaction blocks and no explicit output format section. The agent has a review checklist but no illustration of what a correct review looks like.
suggested_fix: Add at least two example reviews (input diff → output critique) and an Output Format section describing expected response structure.

description: Missing name, description, and model frontmatter on an internal application prompt file.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter with name, description, and model fields.

description: 'sensible bound' used twice (slippage > a sensible bound, fee > a sensible fraction) — vague thresholds that leave agent behavior undefined.
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete defaults: 'slippage > 3%' and 'fee > 5% of transfer value'; allow these to be configurable.

evidence: Command line: bash "$HOME/.reflexio/plugin-root/scripts/cli.sh" learn --note "$ARGUMENTS" — $ARGUMENTS is user-supplied text interpolated inside double-quotes; bash still expands $(...) and backticks
description: User-supplied $ARGUMENTS passed directly to bash shell command; a crafted note containing $(command) executes arbitrary code on the user's machine
suggested_fix: Quote-protect with printf '%s' "$ARGUMENTS" piped to the CLI, or pass arguments via a temp file, or use -- to terminate option parsing and validate the note value before passing to bash

evidence: _R=$(ls -dt "$HOME/.codex/plugins/cache/reflexioai/claude-smart"/*/ 2>/dev/null | head -n 1) then . "${_R%/}/scripts/_codex_env.sh"
description: Script is sourced from a path derived from ls output; if an attacker can create directories inside the Codex plugin cache, they can redirect the sourced file to arbitrary code
suggested_fix: Prefer a fixed, verifiable path or verify the path integrity (checksum or signature) before sourcing

evidence: Setup hook: bash "$_R/scripts/smart-install.sh" where $_R is CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT or hardcoded fallback; content of smart-install.sh not in scope
description: Setup hook executes smart-install.sh from a plugin-controlled path; if smart-install.sh fetches and executes remote code, severity escalates to Critical
suggested_fix: Audit smart-install.sh content; if it downloads code, pin to a hash-verified artifact and document the remote fetch explicitly

evidence: tar.extractall(vendor_dest) — no filter= argument; Python 3.11 (minimum required) does not warn about path traversal entries in archives
description: tarfile.extractall() without a filter can extract files outside the target directory if the archive contains path traversal entries (e.g., ../../../evil); the Reflexio checkout is nominally trusted but an exploited upstream could weaponize this
suggested_fix: Pass filter='data' on Python 3.12+ or validate member paths before extraction on 3.11: check that member.name does not start with / or contain ..

evidence: urllib.request.urlopen(request, timeout=15) to https://pypi.org/pypi/reflexio-ai/<version>/json
description: Script makes outbound HTTP request to PyPI; expected for a version-check helper but worth noting for supply-chain awareness in locked-down CI environments
suggested_fix: Document in CI policy; consider caching PyPI responses or using pip index to avoid direct urllib calls

evidence: append_env_value REFLEXIO_API_KEY writes key to $HOME/.reflexio/.env as plaintext, chmod 600
description: API key stored as plaintext in .env file; chmod 600 limits access but no encryption at rest
suggested_fix: Consider integrating with OS keychain (keyring, macOS Keychain, libsecret) or document the encryption option consistent with AGENT_BROWSER_ENCRYPTION_KEY pattern used elsewhere in the codebase

evidence: run_native calls subprocess.run(['bash', '-lc', full]) where full = f"{prefix}{command}" and command = f"{cmds[0]} {args.args}" with args.args being the user-supplied --args value

evidence: run_native prepends 'sudo -n ' to command at line 204; auto-retry block at lines 386-393 re-runs any permission_denied failure with sudo without per-invocation user consent

suggested_fix: Require an explicit --allow-sudo flag per invocation rather than auto-escalating on permission_denied classification

evidence:
---
description: ...
description: Frontmatter missing required `name:` field. The command cannot be registered as a slash command in the Claude Code plugin index without a name. The plugin loader silently skips nameless commands on install.
suggested_fix: Add `name: audit-context` as the first field in the frontmatter block.

evidence:
---
description: ...
description: Frontmatter missing required `name:` field. Same effect as audit-context.md — this migration command cannot be registered or invoked. Both commands in the fabric-cli plugin are currently dead on install.
suggested_fix: Add `name: migrating-fabric-trial-capacities` as the first field in the frontmatter block.

evidence: Process steps describe output implicitly within numbered steps but no `## Output Format` heading exists
description: Agent has no explicit `## Output Format` section. The process steps describe what the agent produces implicitly, but consumers cannot rely on a stable output contract without a dedicated section.
suggested_fix: Add a `## Output Format` section that describes the written query file structure and any console output the agent emits.

evidence: Only one `## Example` block present in the agent definition
description: Only 1 example block. Convention requires ≥2 examples to illustrate distinct input contexts. The single example covers a basic R visual review but leaves more complex or edge-case scenarios undocumented.
suggested_fix: Add a second example that covers a different R visual scenario — for instance, a complex ggplot2 chart with multiple geom layers or a forecasting visual with statistical overlays.

evidence: Only one `## Example` block present in the agent definition
description: Only 1 example block. Convention requires ≥2 examples. The deneb-reviewer agent in the same plugin has 2 examples; this agent is inconsistently documented within the custom-visuals/reports plugin.
suggested_fix: Add a second example covering a different SVG visual context — for instance, a multi-segment progress ring or a sparkline SVG measure with conditional coloring.

evidence: Only one `## Example` block present in the agent definition
description: Only 1 example block. Convention requires ≥2 examples. Three of the four reviewer agents (r-reviewer, svg-reviewer, python-reviewer) share this gap; deneb-reviewer is the exception.
suggested_fix: Add a second example covering a more complex matplotlib/seaborn scenario — for instance, a multi-panel subplot or a statistical distribution plot with confidence bands.

evidence: Frontmatter has `name`, `description`, `argument-hint`, `model` but no `allowed-tools` field
description: No `allowed-tools:` frontmatter field. Commands should declare which tools they may invoke so the runtime can enforce least-privilege constraints and users can audit the command's capabilities.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read, Grep, Glob, Bash]` to the frontmatter (or the precise set of tools the command actually uses).

evidence: Command uses `$ARGUMENTS` for rule description but provides no guard or prompt when invoked without arguments
description: No empty-input handling. If a user invokes `/suggest-rule` with no argument, the command will proceed without a rule description, producing a confusing or empty output. The command should detect empty `$ARGUMENTS` and ask for a description.
suggested_fix: Add an empty-input guard at the start: check whether `$ARGUMENTS` is empty and, if so, prompt the user to describe the best practice they want to enforce before proceeding.

evidence: "appropriate Severity" appears twice in the rule generation workflow
description: Vague quantifier: "appropriate Severity" (×2). The command leaves severity assignment to judgment without specifying the mapping. This leads to inconsistent rule severity across teams.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete severity map: "Severity 3 (error) for violations that break the model or cause query failures; Severity 2 (warning) for quality or performance issues; Severity 1 (info) for documentation gaps."

evidence: Frontmatter has `description`, `argument-hint`, `model` but no `allowed-tools` field
description: No `allowed-tools:` frontmatter field. This issue compounds the missing `name` bug — the command is unregisterable AND underdeclared.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob]` to the frontmatter alongside the `name` fix.

evidence: Command uses `$ARGUMENTS` but provides no guard when invoked without a workspace or scope argument
description: No empty-input handling. The audit-context command requires a workspace or item scope to be useful. Without an argument guard it will run a generic audit with no target.
suggested_fix: Add an empty-input guard that prompts for a workspace name or Fabric item type when `$ARGUMENTS` is empty.

evidence: Frontmatter has `description` and `argument-hint` but no `model:` field
description: No `model:` frontmatter field. This multi-step migration command involves irreversible capacity operations; the runtime should route it to a capable model explicitly rather than inheriting a default.
suggested_fix: Add `model: sonnet` (or the preferred tier) to the frontmatter alongside the `name` fix.

evidence: Frontmatter has `description`, `argument-hint` but no `allowed-tools` field
description: No `allowed-tools:` frontmatter field. Compounds the missing `name` and missing `model` bugs — the command is triple-underdeclared.
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Bash, Read]` to the frontmatter alongside the other fixes.

evidence: Command uses `$ARGUMENTS` for capacity/workspace target but provides no guard for empty invocation
description: No empty-input handling. A capacity migration command that runs without a target argument could attempt to operate against an unintended scope. Empty-argument detection is especially important for irreversible operations.
suggested_fix: Add an empty-input guard that requires the user to name the target capacity before executing any migration steps.

description: skills/SKILL.md advertises '65 tools' but CLAUDE.md:14 states 'registers all 63 canonical tools'. One count is stale after a recent tool addition.
suggested_fix: Audit src/index.ts tool registrations; update the lower of the two counts to match.

evidence: Lines 113-115 read: 'must NOT name what was leaked ... include words like "security" / "hard-coded" / "sanitize". Frame as a bland refactor.'
description: Documented policy instructs developers to disguise security-patching releases as bland refactors and strip security-related keywords from public changelog, commit, and release notes. Prevents downstream users from identifying security-relevant updates.
suggested_fix: Replace policy with a coordinated disclosure approach: use a CVE or GHSA advisory for the specifics, and add a generic 'Security: see advisory GHSA-xxxx' line in the CHANGELOG so users know to upgrade.

evidence: "build": "rm -rf dist && tsc && node scripts/postbuild.mjs" — rm -rf executed unconditionally in npm build and via prepublishOnly.
description: npm build lifecycle executes `rm -rf dist` as a shell command. Scoped to dist/ in practice, but a symlink or directory confusion at that path would cause unintended deletion; also runs on every npm publish via prepublishOnly.
suggested_fix: Replace with a cross-platform Node.js fs.rmSync call or add explicit relative-path guard (./dist) to constrain scope.

evidence: All runtime deps use ^ ranges: "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "^1.29.0", "commander": "^12.1.0", "diff": "^9.0.0", "re2": "^1.24.1"
description: All four runtime dependencies and all dev dependencies use ^ semver ranges. A malicious or broken minor release in any dependency could silently affect downstream installs.
suggested_fix: Pin runtime dependencies to exact versions in package.json and rely on the committed lockfile for reproducible installs in CI.

description: Release checklist contains three instances of the literal string <personal-or-internal-string> with no explanation that substitution is required or what to substitute, making the checklist non-actionable for new contributors.
suggested_fix: Replace placeholder with a comment like '# replace with your actual leaked string before running' or document the pattern in an adjacent comment.

description: The metadata: frontmatter block embeds JSON flow syntax ({ "openclaw": { ... } }) inside a YAML document. Valid but non-standard; strict-mode YAML parsers and frontmatter linters commonly reject inline JSON flow mappings at the top level.
suggested_fix: Convert the metadata block to standard YAML indented mapping syntax rather than JSON flow style.

evidence: Line 17: 'In the `context` field, briefly explain why you're asking this and (when relevant) what you observed'
description: Behavioral contract uses two vague quantifiers in one sentence: 'briefly' (no length bound) and '(when relevant)' (no inclusion criteria). Agents following this instruction have undefined behavior on length and conditional inclusion.
suggested_fix: Replace 'briefly' with a concrete bound (e.g. '1–2 sentences') and replace '(when relevant)' with an explicit condition (e.g. '(when the user uploaded a file or gave a free-form answer in the previous turn)').

description: File is registered as a skill reference (skills/ultraapp/SKILL.md) but is structured as an agent persona: imperative behavioral contracts, tool invocation syntax, and a completion protocol. Content role does not match file-system classification.
suggested_fix: Either move to agents/ultraapp-interview.md with appropriate agent frontmatter, or retain as a skill and reframe content as reference rather than imperative instructions.

description: Tool count in skills/SKILL.md ('65 tools') disagrees with CLAUDE.md ('63 canonical tools'). CLI version option tables in SKILL.md are frozen at 2.1.111/2.1.121 while CLAUDE.md notes tested version 2.1.178.
suggested_fix: Run a count of registered tools in src/index.ts and update whichever document is stale; bump the CLI version headers in SKILL.md to 2.1.178.

description: SKILL.md contains relative Markdown links to references/council.md, references/autoloop.md, and references/tools.md that could not be verified during this audit. If absent from the repo, these are broken references for readers in editor or on GitHub.
suggested_fix: Verify that skills/references/*.md files exist and are included in the npm package files array; if not, update links or add the missing files.

evidence: plugin.json line 8 declares version 2.5.2; SKILL.md frontmatter line 10 declares version 2.5.3 — confirmed by reading both files
description: plugin.json version field (2.5.2) does not match SKILL.md metadata version (2.5.3); manifest lags by one patch release
suggested_fix: Bump plugin.json version to 2.5.3 to match SKILL.md

evidence: Line 569: fs.writeFileSync(q.file, Buffer.from(resp.result.data, 'base64')) — q.file is Object.fromEntries(parsed.searchParams) with no path restriction
description: Unvalidated ?file= query parameter passed directly to fs.writeFileSync; any local caller can write arbitrary data to any filesystem path accessible by the Node.js process
suggested_fix: Restrict q.file to os.tmpdir() or a configurable safe directory; reject paths containing '..' or absolute paths outside the allowed prefix

evidence: Line 418: const expr = body || q.expr || 'document.title'; passed to Runtime.evaluate on line 419-422 with no validation; proxy has no authentication
description: The /eval endpoint executes unvalidated JavaScript from any local caller in the user's Chrome browser via CDP; the localhost-only binding reduces but does not eliminate the attack surface
suggested_fix: Add a shared-secret token requirement for the /eval endpoint, or explicitly document the local-trust security model in the skill's README

description: SQL conditions built with string interpolation; only single-quote escaping is applied; execFileSync prevents shell injection but SQL injection via other metacharacters is theoretically possible against the local SQLite copy
suggested_fix: Use a Node.js SQLite binding with parameterised queries, or at minimum sanitise all SQL metacharacters not just single quotes

description: Vague quantifier '合适' (suitable/appropriate) in '请积极在任务合适时组合使用' — no concrete criteria specified for when a task is suitable for Jina
suggested_fix: Enumerate the specific page types or task characteristics that make Jina the preferred pre-processing layer

description: Vague quantifier '合理' (reasonable) in '鼓励合理分治给子 Agent 并行执行' — the adjacent decision table helps but the prose framing offers no actionable threshold
suggested_fix: Remove the vague adverb or anchor it to the decision table criteria immediately below

description: Vague quantifier '有必要' (when necessary) in '如果发现了有必要记录经验的新站点或新模式' — no criteria given for what makes recording necessary
suggested_fix: Specify concrete conditions: e.g. 'record when a non-obvious URL pattern, anti-scraping behaviour, or login flow was encountered'

description: Vague quantifier '大量' (large amount) in '页面中存在大量已加载但未展示的内容' — imprecise generalisation
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete description of the DOM pattern, e.g. 'elements that exist in the DOM tree but are outside the viewport or hidden by CSS'

description: Vague marketing term 'intelligent' in plugin description field — 'intelligent tool selection' provides no operational definition of the selection logic
suggested_fix: Replace with a specific description, e.g. 'rule-based tool selection (WebSearch / WebFetch / curl / CDP) based on URL availability and login-state requirements'

evidence: plugin.json version: 2.5.2; SKILL.md version: 2.5.3 — both files read and compared directly
description: Version declared in plugin.json (2.5.2) diverges from SKILL.md frontmatter (2.5.3); SKILL.md body references v2.5.3 migration notes, confirming SKILL.md is the authoritative source
suggested_fix: Align plugin.json version to 2.5.3; consider adding a CI check that asserts plugin.json and SKILL.md versions match

evidence: scripts.postinstall field present: "node scripts/postinstall.mjs" — code runs automatically on every npm install
description: postinstall lifecycle hook runs arbitrary Node.js code on npm install; content is benign (CI guard + file copy) but the hook is an arbitrary-code-execution surface at install time
suggested_fix: Document the postinstall behavior in README and consider replacing with a named setup script developers run explicitly (e.g. npm run setup)

evidence: execSync(`node esbuild.config.mjs ${args}`, ...) where args = process.argv.slice(2).join(' ') — shell metacharacters in args are interpreted by sh -c
description: process.argv args joined as a string and interpolated into execSync template literal; Node execSync(string) invokes sh -c so shell metacharacters enable injection
suggested_fix: Use spawnSync('node', ['esbuild.config.mjs', ...process.argv.slice(2)], { cwd: ROOT, stdio: 'inherit' }) to pass args as an array, bypassing shell interpretation

evidence: "obsidian": "latest" in devDependencies resolves to npm dist-tag latest at install time
description: obsidian dependency uses floating 'latest' tag; future major releases may silently introduce breaking API changes
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific semver range (e.g. "^1.8.0") and update deliberately

description: Most devDependencies use ^ prefix allowing minor and patch upgrades; a compromised minor release in any devDependency would run in the build environment
suggested_fix: Consider using npm ci with a committed package-lock.json to lock the full dependency tree rather than relying on semver ranges alone

description: Architecture table references features/inline-edit and features/settings as distinct modules but neither has a CLAUDE.md sub-file; other comparable modules (core, providers, features/chat, style) all have sub-files
suggested_fix: Add src/features/inline-edit/CLAUDE.md and src/features/settings/CLAUDE.md to complete the documentation pattern

description: Discovery workflow uses /api/skills?q=QUERY (line 32) but API Reference table documents the search endpoint as /api/skills/search?q=QUERY (line 178); the two paths contradict each other within the same file
suggested_fix: Verify the correct endpoint with the registry and update either the body example or the API reference table to match

evidence: Line 136 reads: 'Show 3-5 most relevant results maximum' — no criterion defined for relevance
description: Vague quantifier 'relevant' used without definition; agent cannot deterministically apply the filter
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete ranking criterion such as 'sorted by installs descending'

evidence: Line 141 reads: 'Offer to help directly if no good skill exists or user declines' — 'good' is undefined
description: Vague qualifier 'good' used without definition; agent cannot determine when a skill qualifies as good
suggested_fix: Replace with a measurable threshold, e.g. 'if no skill has ≥50 installs or a description matching the user task'

evidence: Line 21 reads: 'best practices matter (testing, deployment, APIs, documentation)' — 'best practices' is underspecified
description: Vague phrase 'best practices' used; the parenthetical examples help but do not remove the ambiguity of what constitutes best practice
suggested_fix: Replace with specific criteria or link to an explicit standard

evidence: Line 99: npx skills-installer install @owner/repo/skill-name — no version pin on skills-installer
description: Skill instructs use of npx without a pinned version of skills-installer; a compromised npm release would execute arbitrary code in the user environment
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version: npx skills-installer@<version> install ... and note the version in the skill

evidence: Line 32: curl "https://claude-plugins.dev/api/skills?q=QUERY&limit=20&offset=0"
description: Skill instructs agent to issue outbound curl GET requests to an external registry on every non-trivial task invocation; external dependency for core agent functionality
suggested_fix: Document the registry's uptime/availability guarantees and add a fallback instruction for offline or rate-limited scenarios

evidence: "@types/bun": "latest" in devDependencies
description: devDependency @types/bun uses the floating 'latest' tag; breaking type changes will surface silently on the next install
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version or use a caret range (e.g. "@types/bun": "^1.2.0")

evidence: "typescript": "^5" in peerDependencies
description: Peer dependency typescript uses a broad ^5 range; any future 5.x release with breaking changes will be accepted without explicit review
suggested_fix: Narrow to ^5.x.x or a specific minor baseline matching the project's tested TypeScript version

evidence: packages/cli/CLAUDE.md is byte-for-byte identical to CLAUDE.md at repo root
description: Root CLAUDE.md and packages/cli/CLAUDE.md are identical; no cli-specific overrides exist, creating silent drift risk if either file is updated independently
suggested_fix: Remove packages/cli/CLAUDE.md and rely on root-level Claude Code context inheritance, or add cli-specific content to justify the separate file

evidence: Instructional line reads: "Let some mess in." — "some" is a vague quantifier; immediately followed by three concrete examples that narrow the meaning.
description: Vague quantifier "some" in instructional prose; the following sentences (tangents, asides, half-formed thoughts) partially resolve the ambiguity.
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete directive, e.g. "Include tangents, asides, or self-corrections where they fit."

evidence: Glob of target-repo/commands/trading-ideas/ returns no CLAUDE.md file; plugin root confirmed absent
description: No CLAUDE.md in plugin root (commands/trading-ideas/); R49 requires it for Claude Code integration context
suggested_fix: Create commands/trading-ideas/CLAUDE.md describing the plugin purpose, /trading-ideas:research invocation, and customization options

description: README Usage shows /trading-ideas AAPL (direct-file install name) but plugin.json description explicitly says 'via /trading-ideas:research command' — two install paths yield different command names with no explanation
suggested_fix: Add a note in the README Usage section explaining that plugin install (/trading-ideas:research) and direct-file install (/trading-ideas) produce different command names

evidence: Command body opens with 'When given a stock ticker' with no prior branch for empty $ARGUMENTS
description: No empty input handling: bare invocation with no ticker leaves behavior undefined
suggested_fix: Add opening guard: if no arguments provided, ask the user for a ticker symbol before proceeding with the analysis framework

evidence: argument-hint declares [--detailed] but no conditional branch for this flag exists anywhere in the command body
description: --detailed flag declared in argument-hint has no corresponding conditional behavior; README implies enhanced output for the flag
suggested_fix: Either add a conditional section for --detailed (e.g. additional deep-dive sections) or remove the flag from argument-hint and README examples

description: plugin.json declares no commands array; auto-discovery from the commands/ directory may work but explicit declaration is safer
suggested_fix: Add "commands": ["commands/research.md"] to plugin.json to explicitly register the available command

description: plugin.json has no skills array; the plugin's sole artifact skills/adversarial-spec/SKILL.md is not declared in the manifest, breaking auto-discovery
suggested_fix: Add "skills": ["skills/adversarial-spec/SKILL.md"] to plugin.json

evidence: No example blocks found anywhere in SKILL.md (searched for ## Examples, <example>, and similar markers)
description: Zero example blocks present; skills require at least one worked invocation example showing input and expected output
suggested_fix: Add an ## Examples section with a sample invocation (e.g., user asks to refine a spec file) and the skill's expected output format

evidence: "conduct a comprehensive interview" (line ~200) and "write a comprehensive spec" (line ~263)
description: 'comprehensive' used twice as a vague quantifier; replace with concrete criteria such as 'covers all 8 interview topic categories' or 'all required sections populated'
suggested_fix: Replace both instances of 'comprehensive' with measurable criteria referencing the 8 interview topic categories listed in Step 0.5

evidence: "it's a thorough requirements gathering session" at line ~200
description: 'thorough' is a vague quantifier; replace with 'covers all 8 interview topic categories in Step 0.5'
suggested_fix: Replace 'thorough' with 'covers all 8 interview topic categories listed in Step 0.5'

evidence: "suitable for importing into issue trackers" at line ~839
description: 'suitable' is a vague quantifier; specify which import format is produced (e.g., 'JSON compatible with Jira/Linear import schemas')
suggested_fix: Replace 'suitable for importing into issue trackers' with the specific JSON schema or format name used

description: Bash command template at Step 3 interpolates MODEL_LIST (derived from AskUserQuestion, which allows free-text 'Other' input) without quoting; a value like 'gpt-4o; cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa' would be executed by the shell
suggested_fix: Quote all user-derived substitutions in every bash template in the SKILL.md: --models "$MODEL_LIST" --doc-type "$TYPE"

evidence: TELEGRAM_API.format(token=token, method=method) at line 63 sends full spec content to api.telegram.org on every debate round via send_long_message
description: Full spec content transmitted to external Telegram API on every debate round; TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN from environment; no explicit user acknowledgment of data sharing required before --telegram is used
suggested_fix: Add explicit user consent step in SKILL.md before enabling --telegram; document that spec content is sent externally

description: subprocess.run at line 330 passes user-provided model name as a positional CLI argument to external 'codex' binary without validating the model name format; a crafted model string could inject unexpected flags
suggested_fix: Validate model name with re.match(r'^[\w./:@-]+$', actual_model) before constructing cmd list

evidence: GLOBAL_CONFIG_PATH = Path.home() / ".claude" / "adversarial-spec" / "config.json" at line 15; save_global_config writes to this path at line 101
description: Plugin writes config and profile data to user home directory locations (~/.claude/adversarial-spec/ and ~/.config/adversarial-spec/) that are outside the repo and undocumented in the SKILL.md
suggested_fix: Document all write locations in a Configuration Storage section in SKILL.md; consider making the config path overridable via an environment variable

description: litellm==1.80.13 is exact-pinned to one version; security patches in later patch releases are missed until the pin is manually updated
suggested_fix: Use litellm>=1.80.13,<2.0.0 to receive patch-level security fixes while staying on the tested minor version

description: skills/adversarial-spec/SKILL.md is the plugin's sole NL artifact but is absent from any skills[] declaration in plugin.json; tooling that relies on explicit manifest declarations cannot locate it
suggested_fix: Add skills field to plugin.json referencing the SKILL.md path

description: 'vary significantly' used as vague quantifier before the processing-time table; the table immediately follows but the intro sentence itself is imprecise
suggested_fix: Replace 'vary significantly' with the actual range spread or remove the sentence entirely since the table provides the specifics

evidence: Pattern appears inside a Python string literal passed to print(..., file=sys.stderr); actual subprocess.run on line 110 uses shell=False with a fixed argv list
description: curl-pipe-sh pattern detected in error help-text string, not executed; actual subprocess call is shell=False with constructed argv
fp_reason: The curl|sh string is inside a Python string literal printed as a user-facing install hint, never passed to a shell
rule_gap: Pattern matcher should exclude occurrences inside Python string literals / print statements rather than flagging source-level text

description: NOTEBOOKLM_REFRESH_CMD_USE_SHELL=1 enables shell=True execution of user-controlled NOTEBOOKLM_REFRESH_CMD env-var string; no runtime warning when opt-in is active
suggested_fix: Emit a warnings.warn() or stderr advisory when NOTEBOOKLM_REFRESH_CMD_USE_SHELL is set to 1 at runtime

evidence: plugin.json contains name, description, author but no version field; Claude Code marketplace requires version for registration and versioned installs
description: plugin.json is missing the required version field, preventing marketplace publication and versioned plugin resolution
suggested_fix: Add "version": "0.1.0" field alongside name and description in plugin.json

evidence: Line 31: 'If significant changes detected' — 'significant' is an unanchored vague quantifier with no numeric or structural threshold defined
description: Vague quantifier 'significant' in update-mode step 1 leaves change detection threshold undefined for the orchestrating agent
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete threshold such as 'If 5 or more files changed, or any file under src/ changed'

evidence: Line 109: 'Notable dependencies' in subagent prompt template — 'notable' is a subjective, unanchored qualifier
description: Vague quantifier 'notable' in the example subagent prompt template leaves dependency scope undefined
suggested_fix: Replace with 'Direct imports and significant transitive dependencies (depth 1-2)'

description: Vague term 'non-obvious' appears in subagent prompt template and output template sections; subjective without definition
suggested_fix: Replace with 'undocumented assumptions, side effects, or behaviors that differ from what the function or file name implies'

evidence: Line 26: curl-pipe-sh string appears inside print(..., file=sys.stderr); grep confirms no subprocess/os.system call anywhere in the file; the script never executes the command
description: Script outputs curl-pipe-sh installation advice in its stderr error message but does not execute it; recommends an insecure install pattern to users
fp_reason: The curl|sh pattern is inside a sys.stderr print() statement used as user-facing advisory text, not executable code; the script has no shell execution surface
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should distinguish print/log-only occurrences from actual subprocess/os.system/eval execution to avoid CRITICAL false positives on help-text strings
suggested_fix: Replace the curl-pipe-sh recommendation with 'pip install tiktoken' (already shown two lines below) or a versioned release URL

evidence: Line 53: 'pip install tiktoken' with no version specifier; same pattern repeated at line 291
description: SKILL.md instructs the orchestrating agent to run pip install tiktoken without a version constraint, allowing arbitrary future versions including breaking or malicious releases
suggested_fix: Pin to a known-good range: pip install 'tiktoken>=0.7,<1.0'

evidence: Line 4: dependencies = ["tiktoken"] in PEP 723 inline script spec with no version constraint
description: Inline UV script dependency 'tiktoken' carries no version pin; uv will resolve to the latest release on every cold run
suggested_fix: Change to "tiktoken>=0.7,<1.0" in the inline dependencies array

description: CLAUDE.md body duplicates skills/karpathy-guidelines/SKILL.md without @import; changes to the skill will not propagate automatically.
suggested_fix: Replace CLAUDE.md body with a single @import directive: @skills/karpathy-guidelines
